test_cases.title: View Salary page — salary office list
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant or admin with access to Accounting.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_ACCOUNTANT', 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT', 'ROLE_ADMIN') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Salary page
3. Verify a list of salary offices is displayed
4. Verify each office shows: name, report period, approve period
5. Verify a salary office filter/dropdown is present
6. Select a specific salary office from the filter
7. Verify the employee list updates to show only employees from that office
8. Verify 'All' option shows employees from all accessible offices
test_cases.expected_result: Salary page shows all accessible salary offices with period info. Filter works correctly.
test_cases.notes: Filter bug #2841: localStorage with stale data can cause empty filter. ACCOUNTANT+ADMIN role combination may show zero SO.

test_cases.title: Salary office filter — all accessible offices visible (#2841)
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ACCOUNTANT + ADMIN dual roles.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_ACCOUNTANT' AND e.enabled = true AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_role er2 JOIN ttt_backend.role r2 ON er2.role_id = r2.id WHERE er2.employee_id = e.id AND r2.name = 'ROLE_ADMIN') ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the ACCOUNTANT+ADMIN user
2. Navigate to Accounting > Salary page
3. Open the salary office filter dropdown
4. Count the number of offices in the dropdown
5. Compare with expected number (all offices for ADMIN role)
6. Verify no offices are missing
7. Clear browser localStorage and reload
8. Verify filter shows correct offices after localStorage clear
9. Test with >20 salary offices — verify pagination works
test_cases.expected_result: All salary offices visible in filter. No missing offices due to localStorage corruption.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2841: filter showed subset of offices. Also: >20 SO pagination bug, filter breaks with nameless SO, names don't update on language switch.

test_cases.title: Salary office filter — language switch updates names
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant on Salary page with salary offices that have both EN and RU names.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Salary page
3. Note salary office names in current language (e.g., English)
4. Click language switcher → switch to Russian
5. Verify salary office names update to Russian translations
6. Switch back to English
7. Verify names revert to English
test_cases.expected_result: Salary office names update immediately on language switch. No stale cached names.
test_cases.notes: Bug from #2841 comments: SO names don't update on language switch.

test_cases.title: Archived salary offices hidden from dropdowns (#3323)
test_cases.preconditions: At least one archived salary office exists in the system.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Salary page
3. Open the salary office dropdown
4. Verify archived salary offices are NOT listed
5. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
6. Open the salary office dropdown
7. Verify archived offices are NOT listed here either
8. Navigate to Admin > Salary offices
9. Verify archived offices ARE visible but dimmed/grayed
10. Verify archived offices are at the bottom of the list
11. Verify archived offices cannot be edited
test_cases.expected_result: Archived SO hidden from Accounting dropdowns. Visible but non-editable and dimmed in Admin panel.
test_cases.notes: #3323 (OPEN, Sprint 15): archived SO hidden from Accounting, dimmed in Admin.

test_cases.title: Changing periods page — view current periods
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant/admin.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Verify Report period displayed (month + year)
5. Verify Approve period displayed (month + year)
6. Verify Approve period ≤ Report period (invariant)
7. Verify 'Edit' buttons are present for both periods
test_cases.expected_result: Both periods displayed correctly. Approve period is at most equal to report period.
test_cases.notes: Invariant: approve ≤ report. Non-salary offices return 404 on PATCH.

test_cases.title: Advance report period +1 month
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant with a salary office where report period can advance.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Note current report period
5. Click 'Edit' for Report period
6. In the picker, select +1 month
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify period advances
9. Verify employees in that SO can no longer report in the old period
10. Verify success notification
test_cases.expected_result: Report period advances by 1 month. Old period locked for employee reporting.
test_cases.notes: Max 1-month delta from saved value. Must be first of month.

test_cases.title: Advance approve period +1 month (triggers side effects)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Approve period at least 1 month behind report period.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for Approve period
5. Select +1 month
6. Click 'Save'
7. Verify period advances
8. Verify PeriodChangedEvent triggers:
   a. Auto-reject of unapproved hours (if enabled)
   b. Vacation day recalculation
9. Check: employees in the closed approve period can no longer be confirmed
test_cases.expected_result: Approve period advances. Side effects fire: auto-reject + vacation recalc.
test_cases.notes: PeriodChangedEvent vs PeriodReopenedEvent: advance triggers changed, revert triggers reopened.

test_cases.title: Revert approve period -1 month (reopen)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Approve period can be moved back (within 2-month lower bound).
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select salary office
4. Note current approve period
5. Click 'Edit' for Approve period
6. Select -1 month (previous month)
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify period reverts
9. Verify PeriodReopenedEvent fires:
   a. Previously closed month is now open for confirmation
   b. Auto-rejected hours (if any) can be re-confirmed
10. Login as manager, verify confirmation possible for the reopened month
test_cases.expected_result: Approve period reverted. Closed month reopened for confirmation. No data loss.
test_cases.notes: Lower bound: cannot go back >2 months from today. PeriodReopenedEvent fires on revert.

test_cases.title: Period — 2+ month jump blocked in UI (#3350)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant on Changing periods page.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for Report period
5. Verify months >1 ahead of saved value are DISABLED in picker
6. Click 'Edit' for Approve period
7. Verify months >1 ahead or >1 behind saved value are DISABLED
8. Attempt to force submit via browser console (bypass UI)
9. Verify 400 error from backend validation
test_cases.expected_result: Months beyond 1-month delta are disabled in UI. Backend enforces the same rule.
test_cases.notes: All offices now follow same rule — Persei special rule removed (#3264). Sequential saves needed for multi-month changes.

test_cases.title: Period — confirmation month ahead of report period disabled (#3350 bug 3)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant on Changing periods.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for Approve period
5. Verify months AHEAD of the saved Report period are DISABLED
6. Attempt to select a month after the report period
7. If selectable: save → verify 400 error
8. Verify error notification appears explaining the constraint
test_cases.expected_result: Approve period months after report period are disabled. Constraint enforced in UI and backend.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3350 finding: confirmation period month AHEAD of saved report period not disabled in UI.

test_cases.title: Period — report month before confirmation disabled (#3365)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant on Changing periods.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Changing periods
3. Select salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for Report period
5. Verify months BEFORE the saved Approve period are DISABLED
6. Attempt to select such a month
7. Verify it cannot be selected or 400 error on save
test_cases.expected_result: Report period cannot precede approve period. Constraint enforced.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3365 (OPEN): month preceding saved confirmation period not disabled. Partially fixed.

test_cases.title: Period — partial save prevention (#3350 bug 4)
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant on Changing periods with both periods editable.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Changing periods
3. Select salary office
4. Edit Report period: select +1 month
5. Edit Approve period: select a value that creates constraint violation with the new report period
6. Save
7. Verify NEITHER period saves (both-or-nothing)
8. If one saves and other fails: document as partial save bug
test_cases.expected_result: No partial saves. Both periods validated as a pair before committing.
test_cases.notes: Known issue: one period may save while other fails validation → inconsistent state.

test_cases.title: Period — labels and translations (#3351)
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant on Changing periods in both EN and RU.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Changing periods (English)
3. Verify page title matches spec
4. Verify 'Edit' button label
5. Verify tooltip/modal labels
6. Verify datepicker month names
7. Switch to Russian
8. Repeat verification for all labels
9. Note any missing or incorrect translations
test_cases.expected_result: All labels, titles, tooltips, and datepicker content correctly translated in both languages.
test_cases.notes: #3351 (OPEN): page title rename, tooltip/modal/datepicker translation fixes.

test_cases.title: Pay single APPROVED vacation — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant/admin. Employee with an APPROVED+EXACT vacation with payment month in an open period.
Query: SELECT v.id, e.login, v.start_date, v.end_date FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'APPROVED' AND v.period_type = 'EXACT' AND e.enabled = true AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev WHERE ev.employee = e.id AND ev.available_vacation_days >= 0 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Vacation payment page
3. Select the salary office containing the employee
4. Find the APPROVED vacation in the payment table
5. Verify payment details: employee name, dates, days count, payment month
6. Click the checkbox to select the vacation
7. Click 'Pay' button
8. Confirm payment in the confirmation dialog
9. Verify success notification
10. Verify vacation status changes to 'Paid' in the table
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify 'PAID'
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation status changes to PAID. Payment recorded. Vacation disappears from pending payment list.
test_cases.notes: 5-check payment guard: APPROVED + EXACT + payment month in approve period + office not archived + employee in office. All must pass.

test_cases.title: Bulk pay multiple vacations
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Multiple APPROVED+EXACT vacations in the same salary office.
SETUP: May need to create vacations via API and approve them.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Ensure 2+ APPROVED vacations exist for different employees in same SO
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Vacation payment page
3. Select the salary office
4. Select the 'Select all' checkbox (or individually check 2+ vacations)
5. Click 'Pay' button
6. Confirm payment
7. Verify all selected vacations change to 'Paid'
8. Verify vacation count decreases in the table
test_cases.expected_result: Multiple vacations paid in one action. All selected move to PAID status.
test_cases.notes: Bulk payment uses same 5-check guard per vacation. Any failing check skips that vacation.

test_cases.title: Payment blocked — vacation not in EXACT period type
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation with period_type != EXACT (e.g., NON_EXACT).
Query: SELECT v.id, e.login FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON v.employee_id = e.id WHERE v.status = 'APPROVED' AND v.period_type != 'EXACT' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Vacation payment page
3. Attempt to find the non-EXACT vacation in the payment table
4. If visible: attempt to pay
5. Verify payment is BLOCKED with appropriate error
6. Or: verify the vacation is not shown in the payment list at all
test_cases.expected_result: Non-EXACT vacations cannot be paid. Either not shown or blocked with error.
test_cases.notes: 5-check: only EXACT period type eligible for payment. NON_EXACT created when payment month adjusted post-approval.

test_cases.title: Payment with negative combined balance — 500 error (#3363)
test_cases.preconditions:
AV=true office. Employee with combined (current + next year) vacation balance < 0.
Query: SELECT e.login, SUM(ev.available_vacation_days) AS total FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = true AND e.enabled = true GROUP BY e.login HAVING SUM(ev.available_vacation_days) < 0 LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Identify an AV=true employee with negative combined balance
2. If no such employee exists: create the scenario via day corrections
3. SETUP: Create an APPROVED vacation for this employee via API
4. Login as accountant
5. Navigate to Vacation payment page
6. Find the vacation
7. Attempt to pay
8. Verify: if bug exists → HTTP 500 error
9. Verify: if fixed → appropriate error message (not 500)
10. Check: payment with current year negative but sum positive → should succeed
test_cases.expected_result: Payment with combined negative balance shows proper error (not 500). Payment succeeds when sum is non-negative.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3363 (OPEN, Sprint 15): AV=true, combined balance <0 → 500. Works when sum ≥0. Not on production.

test_cases.title: Auto-payment of expired approved vacations
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation older than 2 months from current date (expired threshold).
SETUP: Via test endpoint or clock manipulation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via test API — trigger auto-payment scheduler: POST /api/vacation/test/v1/pay-expired-approved
1. Verify endpoint returns 200
2. Check if the expired APPROVED vacation's status changed to PAID
3. If auto-payment triggered: verify correct payment date and amount
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, payment_date FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Expired APPROVED vacations auto-paid by scheduler. Status changes to PAID with correct payment details.
test_cases.notes: 2-month threshold for auto-payment of unpaid approved vacations.

test_cases.title: Payment page — salary office filter completeness (#2841)
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant with access to multiple salary offices.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Vacation payment page
3. Open salary office filter dropdown
4. Count offices and compare with expected count
5. Verify ALL accessible offices are present (not subset)
6. Navigate to Sick leave accounting page
7. Open salary office filter
8. Verify same completeness (bug #2841 also affects sick leave page)
test_cases.expected_result: All accessible offices in filter on both Payment and Sick leave accounting pages.
test_cases.notes: Same filter bug as Salary page (#2841). Also test >20 SO pagination and nameless SO.

test_cases.title: Manual day correction — add days (AV=No office)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Employee in AV=false (Russia) office with known balance.
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.available_vacation_days, o.name FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = false AND e.enabled = true AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Day corrections page (or Salary page inline edit)
3. Find the employee from the AV=false office
4. Note current available vacation days
5. Click the days value to edit inline
6. Enter a positive correction: +3
7. Enter a comment: 'Annual adjustment'
8. Save
9. Verify available days increased by 3
10. Verify the correction is logged in the events feed
DB-CHECK: SELECT available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = <id> AND year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation days increased by 3. Comment saved. Events feed shows the correction.
test_cases.notes: AV=No: overwork/underwork don't affect vacation days. Available days CANNOT go negative.

test_cases.title: Manual day correction — prevent negative input for AV=No (#3283)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Employee in AV=false office.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-020 but with available_vacation_days > 0)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Day corrections
3. Find employee in AV=false office with balance > 0 (e.g., 10)
4. Click to edit days
5. Attempt to enter -15 (which would make balance negative)
6. Verify the minus sign is PREVENTED in the input field
7. If minus accepted: save and verify DB stores ≥0 (not negative)
8. Check events feed — verify no negative delta shown
test_cases.expected_result: AV=No offices: minus sign prevented in correction input. Balance cannot go negative.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3283 AS-IS: system pretends to accept negative, DB stores ≥0, feed shows negative delta (inconsistency). TO-BE: prevent minus sign entirely.

test_cases.title: Manual day correction — negative allowed for AV=Yes
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Employee in AV=true office (Cyprus, Germany).
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = true AND e.enabled = true AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Day corrections
3. Find employee in AV=true office
4. Note current balance (e.g., 5)
5. Enter correction: -8 (which makes balance negative: -3)
6. Enter comment: 'Underwork correction'
7. Save
8. Verify balance shows -3 (negative displayed correctly)
9. Verify events feed shows -8 delta
DB-CHECK: SELECT available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = <id> AND year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) — verify negative value stored
test_cases.expected_result: AV=Yes offices: negative corrections allowed. Negative balance displayed correctly. DB stores negative value.
test_cases.notes: AV=Yes: overwork/underwork affect vacation days, negative balance CAN result.

test_cases.title: Day correction — year-to-year distribution
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with vacation days across multiple years.
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.year, ev.available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.enabled = true GROUP BY e.login, ev.year, ev.available_vacation_days HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Find employee with multi-year balances (e.g., 2025: 5 days, 2026: 10 days)
3. Apply a negative correction of -7
4. Verify deduction follows FIFO: earliest year first
5. Expected: 2025 → 0 days, 2026 → 8 days (5 from 2025 + 2 from 2026 = 7 deducted)
6. Verify events feed shows correct per-year breakdown
7. Apply a positive correction of +3
8. Verify days added to current year
test_cases.expected_result: Negative corrections follow FIFO (earliest year first). Positive corrections add to current year.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3204: VACATION_MONTHLY_RECALCULATION logic differs from DAYS_ADJUSTMENT distribution. Negative accrued shown as zeros.

test_cases.title: Day correction — balance operates on sum not accrued days (architecture bug)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=false office with correction history.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-020)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Find employee with known accrued and balance values
3. Apply a manual correction
4. Verify: does the correction operate on 'balance sum' or 'accrued days'?
5. If balance sum: correction may allow payment for non-accrued days
6. Verify: manual correction event triggers redistribution between years
7. If no redistribution: document as known architecture bug
DB-CHECK: SELECT available_vacation_days, accrued_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Correction should operate on accrued days, not balance sum. Document actual behavior.
test_cases.notes: QA architecture finding: both corrections operate on balance sum instead of accrued days — fundamentally incorrect per #3283.

test_cases.title: Day correction — comment field validation
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant on correction page.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Day corrections
3. Select an employee
4. Enter correction value
5. Leave comment empty → attempt save
6. Verify if comment is required (should be — audit trail)
7. Enter a very long comment (>255 chars)
8. Verify backend validation (BigDecimal + comment 255 char limit)
9. Enter valid comment and save
test_cases.expected_result: Comment field enforces 255 char max. Verify if comment is required for corrections.
test_cases.notes: Backend validation: BigDecimal for days, 255 chars max for comment.

test_cases.title: AV=false — balance recalculation after day-off change (#3339)
test_cases.preconditions:
AV=false office. Employee with an active vacation that overlaps with a day-off period.
This is an edge case requiring specific calendar setup.
test_cases.steps:
1. Identify or create scenario: AV=false employee with vacation covering a date that also has a day-off
2. Delete the day-off (via calendar admin)
3. Verify vacation type conversion occurs (if applicable)
4. Verify balance recalculation result is CORRECT (not 0)
5. Check: does balance drop to 0 incorrectly? (#3339 bug)
6. Transfer a day-off within the vacation period
7. Verify recalculation is correct after transfer
test_cases.expected_result: Balance recalculation after day-off changes produces correct values. No incorrect drop to 0.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3339: delete/transfer day-off within vacation → conversion results in 0 balance instead of correct value.

test_cases.title: Salary page — employee list with correct data
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant viewing Salary page.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Salary page
3. Select a salary office
4. Verify employee list shows: name, vacation days, report status, payment info
5. Verify data matches DB values
6. Click an employee name
7. Verify employee details open (either popup or separate page)
8. Verify details include: current vacation balance, pending requests, payment history
test_cases.expected_result: Employee list accurate. Details accessible with correct vacation/payment data.
test_cases.notes: Salary page is the primary accounting interface for managing vacation payments and corrections.

test_cases.title: Departing employee tooltip (#3336)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who is in the process of being dismissed (beingDismissed=true from CS).
Query: SELECT e.login, e.last_date FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.last_date IS NOT NULL AND e.last_date > CURRENT_DATE AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY e.last_date ASC LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Salary page
3. Find the departing employee in the list
4. Verify a tooltip or indicator shows 'Date of leave: {date}'
5. Verify the tooltip appears stably (not flicker/disappear)
6. Check after CS sync removes the employee: tooltip should disappear
7. Note timing: tooltip shows while beingDismissed=true, disappears when employee removed from CS list
test_cases.expected_result: Departing employee shows leave date tooltip. Tooltip stable while beingDismissed flag is true.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3336: tooltip appears then disappears when CS sync removes employee. Timing issue between dismissal and salary calculation.

test_cases.title: Vacation days — per-year grouped view
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with vacation days across multiple years.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.enabled = true GROUP BY e.login HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ev.year) > 1 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Salary page, find the multi-year employee
3. View their vacation day balance
4. Verify per-year breakdown is shown
5. Verify total matches sum of all years
6. Compare with GET /api/vacation/v1/vacationDays/{login}/grouped API response
7. Verify accrued vs available distinction (if shown)
test_cases.expected_result: Per-year vacation day breakdown visible. Total matches DB sum. Accrued/available correctly distinguished.
test_cases.notes: API endpoint: GET /v1/vacationDays/{login}/grouped. Bug #3204: negative accrued shown as zeros.

test_cases.title: Accounting page — regular employee access blocked (#3012)
test_cases.preconditions:
Regular employee with ROLE_EMPLOYEE only.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_EMPLOYEE' AND e.enabled = true AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_role er2 JOIN ttt_backend.role r2 ON er2.role_id = r2.id WHERE er2.employee_id = e.id AND r2.name NOT IN ('ROLE_EMPLOYEE', 'ROLE_CONTRACTOR')) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as regular employee
2. Navigate directly to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Verify access is DENIED (redirect or error page)
4. Navigate to /accounting/salary
5. Verify access denied
6. Navigate to /accounting/periods
7. Verify access denied
8. Verify the Accounting menu item is NOT visible in the navigation
test_cases.expected_result: Regular employee cannot access any accounting routes. Menu item hidden. Direct URL access blocked.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3012 (CLOSED): /accounting/sick-leaves was accessible to regular employees using VACATIONS:VIEW instead of SICK_LEAVE_ACCOUNTING_VIEW.

test_cases.title: Sick leave accounting page — list and filter
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant with access to sick leave accounting.
Query: (same as TC-ACC-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Sick leave accounting
3. Verify page loads with employee sick leave list
4. Verify salary office filter works
5. Verify columns: employee name, sick leave dates, status, duration
6. Verify filter shows ALL accessible offices (#2841)
7. Select an office and verify correct employee list
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave accounting page loads with correct data. SO filter works completely.
test_cases.notes: Same SO filter bug as Salary page (#2841). SICK_LEAVE_ACCOUNTING_VIEW permission required.

test_cases.title: Sick leave — close (mark as closed by accountant)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Employee with an open sick leave.
Query: SELECT sl.id, e.login FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave sl JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON sl.employee_id = e.id WHERE sl.status IN ('OPEN', 'ISSUED') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Sick leave accounting
3. Find the employee with open sick leave
4. Click on the sick leave row
5. Click 'Close' or change status to 'Closed'
6. Confirm the action
7. Verify status changes to CLOSED
8. Verify the sick leave duration is finalized
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, end_date FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave closed by accountant. Status = CLOSED. End date finalized.
test_cases.notes: Sick leave has dual status model (internal + external). Accountant operates on external status.

test_cases.title: Sick leave — overdue notification count
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Sick leaves past expected end date without closure.
Query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE status IN ('OPEN', 'ISSUED') AND start_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '14 days'
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Sick leave accounting page
3. Verify overdue counter/badge shows count of overdue sick leaves
4. Compare with DB query result
5. Click on overdue filter
6. Verify only overdue sick leaves displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Overdue count matches DB. Filter shows only overdue sick leaves.
test_cases.notes: API endpoint: GET /v1/sick-leaves/overdue-count.

test_cases.title: Period API — missing first-day-of-month validation on approve period
test_cases.preconditions: API access with accountant/admin token.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — PATCH /api/ttt/v1/{officeId}/periods/approve with start=2026-03-15 (not first of month)
2. Verify: does the request succeed? (BUG: missing validation)
3. If succeeds: verify DB has 2026-03-15 as approve period start
4. If fails: verify proper 400 error with clear message
5. Compare with report period: PATCH /api/ttt/v1/{officeId}/periods/report with start=2026-03-15
6. Verify report period correctly validates first-of-month (getDayOfMonth() != 1 check)
CLEANUP: If bug confirmed, restore valid period via PATCH
test_cases.expected_result: Approve period should reject non-first-of-month dates. Report period already validates correctly.
test_cases.notes: BUG-PERIOD-1 (HIGH): Approve period missing first-day-of-month validation at line 104. Report period has the check.

test_cases.title: Period API — NPE on null start (BUG-PERIOD-2)
test_cases.preconditions: API access with accountant/admin token.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — PATCH /api/ttt/v1/{officeId}/periods/approve with body: {}
2. Verify: does the request return 500 NullPointerException?
3. If 500: confirm BUG-PERIOD-2 (missing null check on start field)
4. If 400: bug is fixed with proper validation
5. Repeat for report period with empty body
CLEANUP: No changes made (read-only test)
test_cases.expected_result: Empty body should return 400 (validation error), not 500 (NPE).
test_cases.notes: BUG-PERIOD-2 (HIGH): PATCH with {} → 500 NullPointerException. Missing null check on request.getStart().

test_cases.title: Period API — stack trace leakage (BUG-PERIOD-3)
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — PATCH /api/ttt/v1/{officeId}/periods/approve with start='invalid-date'
2. Check response body for Java stack traces
3. If stack trace present: confirm BUG-PERIOD-3
4. Verify: error response should be a clean JSON error, not raw exception details
5. Repeat with other invalid inputs: start='null', start=12345
test_cases.expected_result: Invalid date format returns clean 400 error without exposing internal stack traces.
test_cases.notes: BUG-PERIOD-3: Invalid date format returns full Java trace. Security: stack traces expose internal implementation details.

test_cases.title: Period API — permission inconsistency (BUG-PERIOD-4)
test_cases.preconditions: API access with multiple token types.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — GET /api/ttt/v1/periods/report/offices/min — note: requires JWT only
2. Via API — GET /api/ttt/v1/periods/approve/offices/min — note: accepts JWT AND API token
3. Verify the permission difference: report min/max requires JWT only; approve min/max accepts both
4. Document the inconsistency
5. Test with API token only (no JWT): verify report min/max fails, approve min/max succeeds
test_cases.expected_result: Document permission inconsistency between report and approve period endpoints.
test_cases.notes: BUG-PERIOD-4: inconsistent auth requirements between report and approve period min/max endpoints.

test_cases.title: Vacation day correction API — negative value handling (AV-dependent)
test_cases.preconditions: API access. Employees in both AV=true and AV=false offices.
test_cases.steps:
1. For AV=false employee: POST /api/vacation/v1/vacationDays/{login}/correction with days=-5
2. Verify: DB stores ≥0 (negative part lost) — AS-IS bug from #3283
3. For AV=true employee: POST correction with days=-5
4. Verify: DB correctly stores negative value
5. Compare: manual correction vs automatic correction handling of negatives
6. Check events feed: does the feed show the correct delta (including negative)?
test_cases.expected_result: AV=false: DB stores ≥0 (negative ignored). AV=true: DB correctly stores negative. Feed shows actual delta.
test_cases.notes: #3283: DB stores ≥0 for manual, feed preserves negative. Automatic corrections DO write negatives to DB.

test_cases.title: All Projects tab — table loads with correct columns
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ADMIN role.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_ADMIN' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN user
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects
3. Verify 'All projects' tab is active by default
4. Verify table columns: Name, Customer, Supervisor, Manager, Type, Status, Actions
5. Verify pagination is displayed (multiple pages of projects)
6. Verify 3 action buttons per row (task templates, edit tracker data, project info)
7. Verify NO 'Create project' button exists anywhere on the page
8. Verify 'Supervisor' column (not 'Senior Manager' — renamed after PM Tool integration)
test_cases.expected_result: All Projects tab shows correct 7 columns. No create button (PM Tool is source of truth). Pagination works. 3 action buttons per row.
test_cases.notes: PM Tool integration removed project creation from TTT (#3093). Column 'Senior Manager' was renamed to 'Supervisor'.

test_cases.title: Project search — by project name and customer
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user. At least 1 project with known name exists.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects
3. Click search field (placeholder: 'Project name, customer')
4. Type a known project name fragment (e.g., 'ABtasty')
5. Verify scope buttons appear: 'Project' and 'Customer'
6. Click 'Project' scope button
7. Verify table filters to show only matching projects
8. Clear search, type a known customer name
9. Click 'Customer' scope button
10. Verify table filters by customer name
11. Clear search — verify all projects return
test_cases.expected_result: Search filters projects by name or customer. Scope buttons toggle search target. Results update instantly.
test_cases.notes: Search has keyboard layout auto-correction (SuggestionMappingUtil.correctLayout).

test_cases.title: Column filters — Supervisor, Manager, Type, Status dropdowns
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects > All projects
3. Click filter icon on 'Supervisor' column header
4. Verify dropdown with checkbox list of supervisors appears
5. Select one supervisor, close dropdown
6. Verify table filters to that supervisor's projects only
7. Click filter icon on 'Type' column
8. Verify 9 type options: Production, Learning, Administration, Commercial, Idle time, Internal, Investment, Investment without invoicing, Project manager
9. Deselect all, select 'INTERNAL' only
10. Verify table shows only INTERNAL projects
11. Click filter icon on 'Status' column
12. Verify default checked: Active, Unconfirmed, Suspended, Acceptance, Warranty
13. Verify default unchecked: Finished, Cancelled
14. Clear all filters — verify full list returns
test_cases.expected_result: Column filters work as checkbox dropdowns. Status defaults exclude Finished/Cancelled. 9 project types, 7 statuses available.
test_cases.notes: Status defaults: Active/Unconfirmed/Suspended/Acceptance/Warranty shown; Finished/Cancelled hidden. ~55 supervisors in dropdown.

test_cases.title: Show inactive projects toggle
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. Projects with Finished/Cancelled status exist.
Query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.project WHERE status IN ('FINISHED', 'CANCELLED')
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects > All projects
3. Note current page count (e.g., 11 pages for active projects)
4. Check 'Show inactive projects' checkbox
5. Verify page count increases significantly (e.g., ~147 pages with all projects)
6. Verify Finished and Cancelled projects now appear in the table
7. Uncheck 'Show inactive projects'
8. Verify table returns to original active-only count
test_cases.expected_result: Checkbox toggles Finished+Cancelled project visibility. Active-only: ~200 projects (11 pages). All: ~2900+ projects (147 pages).
test_cases.notes: Scale: ~200 active, ~2900+ total. 20 projects per page.

test_cases.title: Project Info dialog — read-only fields and PM Tool link
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A project with a valid pmtId exists.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.pmt_id FROM ttt_backend.project p WHERE p.pmt_id IS NOT NULL AND p.status = 'ACTIVE' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects > All projects
3. Click the 'More about project' button (info icon, 3rd action button) on a project row
4. Verify dialog opens with URL hash #<projectId>
5. Verify ALL fields are read-only: Name, Account name, Customer, Country, Supervisor, Manager, Owner, Watchers, Status, Type, Model, Total cost, First/Last report dates
6. Verify project name is a clickable link to PM Tool: https://pm.noveogroup.com/projects/{pmtId}/profile/general
7. Verify Supervisor/Manager/Owner names link to CompanyStaff profiles (cs.noveogroup.com/profile/{login})
8. Verify 'History of changes' expandable section exists
9. Expand history — verify chronological changes (date, user, field from→to)
10. Close dialog
test_cases.expected_result: All project fields read-only. Name links to PM Tool. Employee names link to CS. History shows audit trail.
test_cases.notes: PM Tool URL pattern: https://pm.noveogroup.com/projects/{pmtId}/profile/general. Falls back to plain text if pmtId is null.

test_cases.title: Edit Tracker Data dialog — 3 editable URL fields
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user with edit permission on a project.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name FROM ttt_backend.project p WHERE p.status = 'ACTIVE' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects > All projects
3. Click 'Edit tracker data' button (pencil icon, 2nd action button) on a project row
4. Verify dialog opens with exactly 3 editable fields:
   a. 'Script of synchronization with tracker' (pre-filled with default script URL)
   b. 'Link to task tracker' (may be empty)
   c. 'Link to task tracker proxy server' (may be empty, has helper link to Google Docs)
5. Verify all fields accept URL input with 'https://' prefix
6. Enter a valid URL in 'Link to task tracker'
7. Click Save
8. Verify success notification
9. Reopen dialog — verify saved URL persists
10. Clear the entered URL, Save again
11. Verify field reverts to empty
test_cases.expected_result: Only 3 tracker-related fields editable. All project metadata (name, customer, type, etc.) is NOT in this dialog. URLs saved/cleared correctly.
test_cases.notes: After PM Tool migration (#3093), 'Edit Project' was renamed to 'Edit Tracker Data' with only 3 URL fields. ProxyDescription links to Google Docs setup guide.

test_cases.title: Task Templates dialog — CRUD operations
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A project in ACTIVE status.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name FROM ttt_backend.project p WHERE p.status = 'ACTIVE' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects > All projects
3. Click 'Task templates' button (1st action button) on a project row
4. Verify dialog opens with task template list (may be empty: 'No templates')

test_cases.notes: Task templates define available tasks per project for time reporting. Templates are auto-prefixed with project name.

test_cases.title: Project name sorting — ascending and descending

9. Verify sorting switches to customer column
test_cases.expected_result: Column sorting toggles between ascending/descending. Sort indicator visible.

test_cases.title: Pagination — navigate through project pages

test_cases.title: My Projects tab — shows only current user's managed projects

PM or ADMIN user who manages at least 1 project.

2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects

4. Verify tab label shows count in parentheses matching projects managed
5. Verify fewer columns: Name, Manager, Status, Actions (no Customer/Supervisor/Type)

7. Verify project Name column — text should be present (may have PM Tool link or '#' href)

test_cases.expected_result: My Projects shows only user-managed projects. Fewer columns (4 vs 7). Tab label shows count.
test_cases.notes: Known bug: My Projects name column has href='#' (non-functional link) — incomplete PM Tool integration.

test_cases.title: Project info — pmtId null fallback (plain text name)

test_cases.expected_result: Projects without pmtId show name as plain text, not a link. No error or broken link displayed.
test_cases.notes: Frontend checks pmtId existence; falls back to plain text. Some legacy projects have no pmtId.

test_cases.title: CS profile links — Supervisor/Manager names link to CompanyStaff

test_cases.expected_result: Supervisor and Manager names link to cs.noveogroup.com/profile/{login}.
test_cases.notes: Links open in same tab or new tab depending on click behavior.

test_cases.title: PM user — sees Projects page with limited scope

User with PM role (not ADMIN).

test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM user (not ADMIN)
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects
3. Verify All Projects tab loads (PM has PROJECTS:VIEW)
4. Verify all 7 columns visible

7. Verify PM can see: Projects, Employees
8. Verify PM cannot see: TTT parameters, API, Export, Production calendars
test_cases.expected_result: PM has access to Projects and Employees admin pages. No access to Settings/API/Calendar/Export.
test_cases.notes: PM has PROJECTS:VIEW and EMPLOYEES:VIEW permissions.

test_cases.title: Calendar list — view calendars and events for selected year
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN or CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT user.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_ADMIN', 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars

4. Verify Year picker (default: current year)
5. Verify Calendar dropdown shows available calendars (Russia, Cyprus, France, etc.)

7. Verify events table shows columns: Date, Working hours, Reason, Actions

test_cases.expected_result: Calendars page loads with year picker, calendar dropdown, and events table. 10+ calendars available. Russia calendar has 18+ events for 2026.
test_cases.notes: 10 calendars: Armenia, Cyprus, Empty calendar, France, Georgia, Germany, Montenegro, Russia, Uzbekistan, Vietnam.

test_cases.title: Create new calendar — name uniqueness and Latin-only
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars > Setting up calendars
3. Click 'Add a calendar' button
4. Verify dialog opens with Name field (placeholder: 'Please enter the calendar title in Latin')
5. Enter a unique Latin name (e.g., 'TestCalendar2026')
6. Click 'Add'
7. Verify calendar appears in the dropdown
8. Try to add another calendar with the SAME name
9. Verify validation error: duplicate name rejected
10. Try adding a calendar with Cyrillic characters (e.g., 'Тест')
11. Verify behavior (may be accepted or rejected based on validation)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /v2/calendars/{newCalendarId} to remove test calendar
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar created with unique Latin name. Duplicate names rejected (case-insensitive on frontend, case-sensitive on backend — potential gap). Latin characters enforced by placeholder hint.
test_cases.notes: Backend: CalendarNameExistsValidator. Frontend: AddCalendarValidationSchema.ts uses case-insensitive check. Gap: backend is case-sensitive. #2651

test_cases.title: Delete calendar — only empty calendars allowed
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A test calendar with NO events and NO SO mapped.
SETUP: Via API — POST /v2/calendars with body {"name": "TempTestCal"} to create empty calendar
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars
3. Select the empty test calendar from dropdown
4. Verify no events listed
5. Locate delete calendar action (if available in UI)
6. Delete the empty calendar
7. Verify calendar removed from dropdown
8. Now select a calendar that HAS events (e.g., Russia)
9. Verify delete action is unavailable or returns error if attempted
CLEANUP: If calendar wasn't deleted via UI, delete via API
test_cases.expected_result: Only empty calendars can be deleted. Calendars with events cannot be deleted (#2653).
test_cases.notes: Ticket #2653 confirmed: only empty calendars deletable.

test_cases.title: Create calendar event — date, working hours, reason
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A test calendar exists.
SETUP: Via API — POST /v2/calendars to create 'TestEventCal' if not exists
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars
3. Select the test calendar
4. Click 'Create a new event' button
5. Verify dialog: Date (date picker), Working hours (dropdown: 0, 7, 8), Reason (text field)
6. Select a future date not already used
7. Select working hours = 0 (full holiday)
8. Enter reason: 'Test Holiday'
9. Click 'Create'
10. Verify new event appears in the table at correct date position
11. Verify event shows: selected date, 0h, 'Test Holiday'
12. Create another event with 7h (pre-holiday) and different date
13. Verify both events in table
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /v2/days/{eventId} for both created events
test_cases.expected_result: Event created with date, working hours (0/7/8), and reason. Table updates sorted by date.
test_cases.notes: Duration: 0 (holiday), 7 (pre-holiday), 8 (transferred working day). Reason is required (min 1 char).

test_cases.title: Calendar event — inline reason editing
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user. Calendar with at least 1 event with a reason.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars
3. Select a calendar with events (e.g., Russia)
4. Click on an event's reason text in the table
5. Verify text converts to an editable textbox (inline edit)
6. Change the reason text
7. Click outside the textbox or press Enter
8. Verify 'Changes saved' toast/notification appears
9. Verify the new reason text persists in the table
10. Verify via API — PATCH /v2/days/{id} only updates 'reason' field
test_cases.expected_result: Inline edit: clicking reason converts to textbox. Save triggers auto-save. Only reason is editable via PATCH (not date/duration).
test_cases.notes: Design issue: PATCH only updates reason field — date and duration are immutable after creation. Auto-save triggers even on no-change (#2204 regression).

test_cases.title: Delete calendar event — only future events

4. Scroll to find a PAST event (already passed date)

6. Scroll to find a FUTURE event
7. Verify delete button IS shown for future events
8. Click delete on a future event
9. Verify confirmation or immediate deletion
10. Verify event removed from table
test_cases.expected_result: Past events: no delete button. Future events: delete available. Event removed after deletion.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2890 reported admin COULD delete past events. Verify fix: delete button hidden for past events.

test_cases.title: Calendar event — duplicate date validation

test_cases.expected_result: Duplicate date within same calendar rejected with validation error. Backend: @DateUniqueOnCreate validator.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2656: 18 duplicate events created (race condition). Bug #3232: validation message silently lost. Regression test essential.

test_cases.title: SO-calendar change — edit dialog and next-year logic
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A salary office with an assigned calendar.
Query: SELECT oc.office_id, o.name AS office_name, c.name AS cal_name, oc.calendar_id FROM ttt_calendar.office_calendar oc JOIN ttt_calendar.office o ON oc.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_calendar.calendar c ON oc.calendar_id = c.id ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars > Calendars for SO
3. Click edit button on a salary office row
4. Verify 'Changing the calendar' dialog opens
5. Verify salary office name displayed (read-only)
6. Verify current calendar displayed (read-only)
7. Verify 'Select a calendar' dropdown with all available calendars
8. Select a DIFFERENT calendar
9. Click 'Change'
10. Verify behavior: if SO had a previous calendar, change applies from NEXT YEAR only
11. Verify current year calendar unchanged
12. Restore original calendar assignment
CLEANUP: Revert SO calendar to original via API — PUT /v2/offices/{officeId}/calendars/{calendarId}
test_cases.expected_result: SO-calendar change dialog: read-only SO name, current calendar, selectable new calendar. If previous calendar exists: change from next year only. If no previous: immediate.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3300: next-year change applied immediately to ALL years. 16 QA comments. Verify since_year logic: records stored only when calendar changes.

test_cases.title: Calendar event duration boundary — min 0, max 12
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars
3. Select a test calendar
4. Click 'Create a new event'
5. Select a future date
6. Verify working hours dropdown options: 0, 7, 8
7. Verify duration field constraints via API:
   DB-CHECK: POST /v2/days with duration=0 → success
   DB-CHECK: POST /v2/days with duration=12 → success
   DB-CHECK: POST /v2/days with duration=13 → validation error (@Max(12))
   DB-CHECK: POST /v2/days with duration=-1 → validation error (@Min(0))
8. Verify UI dropdown limits options to 0/7/8 only
test_cases.expected_result: UI: 3 options (0, 7, 8). API: accepts 0-12 (backend @Min(0) @Max(12)). API rejects <0 or >12.
test_cases.notes: Frontend limits to 0/7/8 dropdown. Backend allows 0-12 range. Gap: API accepts values UI can't create (e.g., 4h).

test_cases.title: Calendar event reason — all-spaces makes uneditable
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user.
SETUP: Via API — POST /v2/days with calendarId, date, duration=0, reason=' ' (single space)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars
3. Select the calendar with the space-reason event
4. Find the event in the table
5. Verify reason appears empty/blank
6. Try to click on the reason to inline-edit
7. Verify the field appears empty but contains whitespace
8. Try to change the reason text
9. Verify whether editing is possible or blocked
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /v2/days/{eventId}
test_cases.expected_result: All-spaces reason makes the field appear empty but contain whitespace. Editing behavior may be blocked (#2902 bug).
test_cases.notes: Bug #2902: all-spaces reason → uneditable afterward. PATCH only updates reason field.

test_cases.title: Employee list — view with search and pagination
test_cases.preconditions:
User with EMPLOYEES:VIEW permission (PM, DM, TL, ACC, CACC, DIR, HR, ADM).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_ADMIN' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Employees
3. Verify 'Employees' tab is active (default)
4. Verify employee table loads with pagination (20+ pages)
5. Verify columns display employee names and roles
6. Verify table is entirely READ-ONLY — no create/edit/delete buttons
7. Verify search by name field exists
8. Search for a known employee name
9. Verify results filter to matching employees
10. Clear search — verify full list returns
test_cases.expected_result: Employee list is read-only (data synced from CompanyStaff). Search by name works. Pagination with 20+ pages.
test_cases.notes: No CRUD — employees imported from CompanyStaff sync. TTT only patches existing employees (e.g., task carry-over settings).

test_cases.title: Show dismissed employees toggle

ADMIN user. Dismissed employees exist in the system.

5. Check 'Show dismissed' checkbox
6. Verify more employees appear (previously hidden dismissed employees)
7. Verify dismissed employees can be distinguished from active ones
8. Uncheck 'Show dismissed'
9. Verify list returns to active employees only
test_cases.expected_result: Show dismissed toggle reveals fired/dismissed employees. Default: hidden. Active+dismissed > active only.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2702: empty employee names with 'Show fired' enabled.

test_cases.title: Subcontractor tab — separate list for contractors

ADMIN user. Subcontractors exist.

test_cases.notes: Contractors separated from regular employees. ~2 pages of subcontractors on timemachine.

test_cases.title: Employee sorting — including Russian Ё character collation
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.

8. Switch back to English

test_cases.notes: Bug #2515: Ё sorts before all others — PostgreSQL collation issue. Bug #2514: sort by Manager causes BadSqlGrammarException.

test_cases.preconditions:
User with TL role only.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_TECH_LEAD' AND e.enabled = true AND e.id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM ttt_backend.employee_role WHERE role_id IN (SELECT id FROM ttt_backend.role WHERE name IN ('ROLE_ADMIN', 'ROLE_VIEW_ALL', 'ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER', 'ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER'))) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as TL
2. Open Admin panel dropdown
3. Verify visible: Employees only
4. Verify NOT visible: Projects, TTT parameters, Production calendars, API, Export
5. Navigate to /admin/employees — verify loads
6. Navigate to /admin/projects directly — verify denied
test_cases.expected_result: TL: access to Employees only (EMPLOYEES:VIEW). No Projects or other admin pages.
test_cases.notes: TL has EMPLOYEES:VIEW but NOT PROJECTS:VIEW.

test_cases.title: EMPLOYEE — no admin panel access except Account
test_cases.preconditions:
User with EMPLOYEE role only (no other admin roles).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM ttt_backend.employee_role WHERE role_id IN (SELECT id FROM ttt_backend.role WHERE name NOT IN ('ROLE_EMPLOYEE', 'ROLE_CONTRACTOR'))) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as EMPLOYEE-only user
2. Verify navigation bar does NOT show 'Admin panel' dropdown
3. Navigate directly to /admin/projects
4. Verify access denied or redirect
5. Navigate to /admin/employees — verify denied
6. Navigate to /admin/settings — verify denied
7. Navigate to /admin/calendar — verify denied
8. Navigate to /admin/account — verify this ONE page IS accessible
9. Verify account settings work normally
test_cases.expected_result: EMPLOYEE: no Admin panel in navigation. All /admin/* pages denied EXCEPT /admin/account.
test_cases.notes: Account page has no permission check — accessible to all. All other admin pages require specific permissions.

test_cases.title: VIEW_ALL — read access to most admin pages
test_cases.preconditions:
User with VIEW_ALL role.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_VIEW_ALL' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as VIEW_ALL user
2. Open Admin panel dropdown
3. Verify visible: Projects, Employees, TTT parameters, Production calendars, API, Offices
4. Verify Export is NOT visible (STATISTICS:EXPORT is ADM-only)
5. Navigate to each visible page — verify loads
6. Verify calendars page loads but edit operations may be restricted
7. Navigate to /admin/export directly — verify denied
test_cases.expected_result: VIEW_ALL: access to Projects, Employees, Settings, Calendar, API, Offices. No Export (ADM-only).
test_cases.notes: VIEW_ALL has most VIEW permissions but NOT STATISTICS:EXPORT.

test_cases.title: Calendar page — VIEW_ALL sees buttons but should be read-only
test_cases.preconditions: User with VIEW_ALL role (not ADMIN, not CACC).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as VIEW_ALL user
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars
3. Check if 'Add a calendar' button is visible
4. Check if 'Create a new event' button is visible
5. Check if delete buttons appear on events
6. If buttons visible: try to create an event
7. Verify whether action succeeds or fails with permission error
8. Document actual behavior (bug #2916: VIEW_ALL sees create/edit/delete buttons)
test_cases.expected_result: VIEW_ALL with CALENDARS:VIEW should see calendar data but NOT have create/edit/delete capability. Bug #2916: buttons shown despite read-only intent.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2916 (closed): VIEW_ALL users see CRUD buttons. Backend uses role check (ADMIN/CACC), not VIEW permission. Verify if fixed.

test_cases.title: CONTRACTOR — no admin access
test_cases.preconditions:
User with CONTRACTOR role.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.type = 'CONTRACTOR' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as CONTRACTOR
2. Verify navigation bar does NOT show 'Admin panel' dropdown
3. Navigate directly to /admin/projects — verify denied
4. Navigate to /admin/employees — verify denied
5. Navigate to /admin/account — verify accessible
test_cases.expected_result: CONTRACTOR: no admin panel access except /admin/account. Same as EMPLOYEE.
test_cases.notes: ROLE_CONTRACTOR gets no explicit permissions in any provider.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — manual trigger and status verification
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. API secret token available.
Query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.project WHERE pm_tool_id IS NOT NULL
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Note current pm_sync_status last entry timestamp
1. Via API — POST /v1/test/project/sync (manual sync trigger)
2. Wait for sync completion (~2-5 minutes depending on project count)
3. DB-CHECK: SELECT * FROM ttt_backend.pm_sync_status ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1 — verify new entry
4. DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.pm_tool_sync_failed_entity — check for failures
5. Login as ADMIN
6. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects
7. Verify project data refreshed (names, supervisors, managers match PM Tool)
test_cases.expected_result: Manual sync creates pm_sync_status entry. Failed entities tracked in pm_tool_sync_failed_entity. Project data updated from PM Tool.
test_cases.notes: Cron: every 15 min. Manual: POST /v1/test/project/sync. Rate limit: 50 req/min. Page size: 100.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — field mapping verification (draft→ACTIVE)
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. At least one PM Tool project exists.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.status, p.pm_tool_id, p.pmt_id FROM ttt_backend.project p WHERE p.pm_tool_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY p.updated_at DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: Query projects with pm_tool_id to see current mapping
2. Verify field mappings:
   - pm_tool_id populated (PM Tool's ttt_id)
   - pmt_id populated (PM Tool's own PK, used for URL links)
   - name matches PM Tool
   - manager_id corresponds to PM Tool's pmId
   - senior_manager_id corresponds to PM Tool's projectSupervisorId (renamed from supervisor)
   - owner_id corresponds to PM Tool's ownerId
   - status: if PM Tool status was 'draft', TTT should show 'ACTIVE'
3. Login as ADMIN
4. Open project info dialog for a PM Tool-synced project
5. Verify PM Tool link: https://pm.noveogroup.com/projects/{pmtId}/profile/general
6. Verify Supervisor, Manager, Owner fields match DB data
test_cases.expected_result: PM Tool fields correctly mapped to TTT project. Draft status silently converted to ACTIVE. pmtId drives PM Tool URL link.
test_cases.notes: Draft→ACTIVE is silent conversion. pmtId may be null for Phase 1 (TTT-created) projects.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — accounting name immutability
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.accounting_name, p.pm_tool_id FROM ttt_backend.project p WHERE p.pm_tool_id IS NOT NULL AND p.accounting_name IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: Query a project with both name and accounting_name set
2. Note the current accounting_name value
3. Trigger PM Tool sync (POST /v1/test/project/sync)
4. Wait for sync completion
5. DB-CHECK: Query the same project — verify accounting_name is UNCHANGED
6. Verify project.name may have changed (it gets updated from PM Tool)
7. But accounting_name stays at its original value (set once on first sync, never overwritten)
test_cases.expected_result: Accounting name immutable after first sync. Project name updates from PM Tool; accounting name does not. (#3083 design)
test_cases.notes: By design: accounting_name set once from PM Tool name, never updated. If first sync has wrong data, manual DB fix required.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — sales employee filtering
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user.
Query: SELECT * FROM ttt_backend.pm_tool_sync_failed_entity ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: Check pm_tool_sync_failed_entity for failures related to sales employees
2. Trigger sync
3. Monitor sync logs or check failed entities after sync
4. Verify: employees with type='sales' in PM Tool are filtered out
5. Verify: sales employees NOT set as manager, owner, or supervisor on any project
6. DB-CHECK: SELECT p.name, e.login as manager FROM ttt_backend.project p LEFT JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON p.manager_id = e.id WHERE p.pm_tool_id IS NOT NULL — verify no sales-type employees as managers
7. Verify: sales-type watchers filtered from observer lists
test_cases.expected_result: Sales employees filtered by removeSalesFromProject(). No sales-type employees as manager/owner/supervisor/watcher. (#3389)
test_cases.notes: CSToolEntityReference.isSales() — case-insensitive check for 'sales' type. Employees and contractors processed normally.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — missing employee handling (HTTP 500)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: SELECT * FROM ttt_backend.pm_tool_sync_failed_entity — check for existing failures
2. Verify if any failures are caused by missing employee IDs
3. Review the error description: should mention IllegalStateException with missing employee CS IDs
4. Verify the ENTIRE project sync fails for that project (not just the missing field)
5. Verify other projects sync successfully despite this project's failure
6. Verify the failed project is tracked for retry in pm_tool_sync_failed_entity
test_cases.expected_result: Missing employee → IllegalStateException → HTTP 500. Project sync fails individually; other projects unaffected. Failed entity tracked for retry. (#3384)
test_cases.notes: Design issue: throws IllegalStateException, not a proper business exception. Causes HTTP 500 instead of meaningful error.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — presales IDs append-only behavior
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A project with presales IDs.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.pre_sales_ids FROM ttt_backend.project p WHERE p.pre_sales_ids IS NOT NULL AND p.pre_sales_ids != '' AND p.pm_tool_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: Note current pre_sales_ids for a PM Tool project
2. Trigger sync
3. DB-CHECK: Verify pre_sales_ids after sync — should only grow (append-only)
4. Verify old presales IDs are NOT removed even if PM Tool no longer has them
5. Verify format: comma-separated ticket IDs
test_cases.expected_result: Presales IDs are append-only — sync adds new IDs but never removes existing ones. Stale IDs accumulate by design. (#3083)
test_cases.notes: By design: presales history preservation. Stale IDs accumulate over time.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — rate limiter at 50 req/min
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.project WHERE pm_tool_id IS NOT NULL — count PM Tool projects
2. Note the time before triggering sync
3. Trigger sync: POST /v1/test/project/sync
4. Monitor sync duration
5. Calculate expected duration: (project_count / 100) * (100 / 50) minutes for page fetches
6. Verify sync completes without 429 errors
7. DB-CHECK: Verify pm_tool_sync_failed_entity has no rate-limit failures
test_cases.expected_result: Sync rate-limited at 50 req/min (configurable). No 429 errors. Sync completes within expected duration.
test_cases.notes: Guava RateLimiter at 50/60 ≈ 0.83 req/sec. acquire() blocks thread. Page size: 100 projects.

test_cases.title: PM Tool sync — failed entity retry in batches
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. At least one entry in pm_tool_sync_failed_entity.
Query: SELECT * FROM ttt_backend.pm_tool_sync_failed_entity ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: Query pm_tool_sync_failed_entity for existing failures
2. Note the failed entity IDs and count
3. Trigger sync
4. DB-CHECK: After sync, check if failed entities were retried
5. Verify: successfully retried entities removed from failed table
6. Verify: still-failing entities remain in failed table
7. Verify retry batch size: 10 (configurable via pmTool.sync.retry-batch-size)
test_cases.expected_result: Failed entities retried in batches of 10 after main sync. Successful retries cleared from table. Persistent failures remain.
test_cases.notes: Retry batch size: 10 (default). Timeout per entity: 10s.

test_cases.title: CS Sync — new salary office not created (#3241)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.office — current office count
2. Trigger CS sync endpoint
3. DB-CHECK: Verify count after sync — check if new offices appeared
4. Verify: field UPDATES on existing offices work (e.g., name changes)
5. Verify: NEW office creation fails or succeeds
6. If creation fails: document as #3241 still open
7. Check vacation and calendar services for same office sync behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Existing office updates work. New office creation may fail (bug #3241). Delta sync doesn't guarantee consistency (#2989).
test_cases.notes: Bug #3241 (closed): new SO not synced. Bug #2989 (OPEN): full sync needed for consistency. Bug #3303: sync on startup.

test_cases.title: CS Sync — NULL office_id handling (#3236)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: SELECT e.login, e.office_id FROM ttt_backend.employee WHERE office_id IS NULL AND enabled = true — check for NULL office
2. If such employees exist, verify they don't cause 500 errors in admin pages
3. Navigate to Admin panel > Employees
4. Search for an employee with NULL office_id
5. Verify the employee displays correctly (no crash)
6. Verify Salary page doesn't crash when processing these employees
test_cases.expected_result: Employees with NULL office_id should display without errors. Bug #3236: should skip invalid records and log warnings.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3236 (OPEN): NULL office_id → 500 errors. Need validation to skip invalid records.

test_cases.title: Regression: Cross-calendar event deletion isolation (#3221)
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. Two different calendars (e.g., Georgia and Cyprus) both have an event on the same date.
SETUP: Via API — create events on same date in 2 different calendars if needed
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Production calendars
3. Select Georgia calendar
4. Note a date that also exists in Cyprus calendar
5. Delete the event from Georgia calendar
6. Verify Georgia calendar event removed
7. Switch to Cyprus calendar
8. Verify Cyprus calendar event is UNAFFECTED
9. DB-CHECK: Verify no day-off processing triggered for Cyprus calendar employees
10. Verify no incorrect email notifications sent to Cyprus employees
CLEANUP: Restore Georgia calendar event if needed
test_cases.expected_result: Deleting event from one calendar does NOT affect employees on other calendars. No cross-calendar cascade. (#3221 CRITICAL)
test_cases.notes: CRITICAL bug #3221: CalendarDeletedEvent didn't scope by calendar_id — processed ALL employees with same-date day-offs.

test_cases.title: Regression: SO calendar change timing — next year only (#3300)
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A salary office with an existing calendar assignment.
Query: SELECT oc.office_id, o.name, c.name AS calendar_name FROM ttt_calendar.office_calendar oc JOIN ttt_calendar.office o ON oc.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_calendar.calendar c ON oc.calendar_id = c.id LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Production calendars > Calendars for SO
3. Edit a salary office's calendar (change to a different calendar)
4. Save the change
5. DB-CHECK: SELECT * FROM ttt_calendar.office_calendar WHERE office_id = <id> ORDER BY since_year
6. Verify: change recorded with since_year = NEXT YEAR (not current year)
7. Verify current year's calendar assignment is UNCHANGED
8. Navigate to Production calendars > Setting up calendars
9. Verify current year still shows old calendar's events for that SO
CLEANUP: Restore original calendar assignment
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar change for SO applies from NEXT YEAR only (if previous calendar existed). Current year unchanged. (#3300)
test_cases.notes: Bug #3300: change applied immediately to ALL years. 16 QA comments. Logic: previous calendar=null → immediate; exists → next year.

test_cases.title: Regression: Calendar event duplication on rapid creation (#2656)
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A test calendar.
SETUP: Via API — ensure test calendar exists
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Production calendars
3. Select test calendar
4. Click 'Create a new event' rapidly (try to submit same date twice quickly)
5. Verify only ONE event created for the date
6. DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_calendar.calendar_days WHERE calendar_id = <id> AND date = '<date>' — should be 1
7. Try via API: simultaneously POST two events with same calendar_id + date
8. Verify duplicate rejection
CLEANUP: Delete test events
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate events created. @DateUniqueOnCreate validator prevents duplicates. Race condition (#2656: 18 duplicates) should be fixed.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2656: 18 duplicate events. Check + insert NOT atomic. Verify uniqueness constraint added.

test_cases.title: Regression: Calendar audit fields preserved after edit (#2648)
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. Calendar with events.
Query: SELECT cd.id, cd.created_at, cd.created_by, cd.reason FROM ttt_calendar.calendar_days cd WHERE cd.created_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: Note created_at and created_by for an existing calendar event
2. Login as ADMIN
3. Navigate to Production calendars
4. Find the event and edit its reason (inline edit)
5. Save the change
6. DB-CHECK: Query the same event — verify created_at and created_by are PRESERVED (not NULL)
7. Verify updated_at and updated_by reflect the edit
8. Verify the reason text changed
test_cases.expected_result: Editing a calendar event preserves created_at/created_by fields. Only updated_at/updated_by change. Bug #2648 fix verified.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2648 (closed): created_at/created_by became NULL after editing event.

test_cases.title: Regression: Project trailing spaces cause duplicate name (#3348)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user. Two projects with similar names.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects
3. Via API: PATCH /v1/projects/{id} with name = 'ExistingProject  ' (trailing spaces)
4. Verify response — should either reject with validation error or trim and check uniqueness
5. If PATCH succeeds: verify the name in DB is trimmed
6. If trimmed name matches existing project: verify AlreadyExistsException returned
7. Login and verify UI shows the project correctly
test_cases.expected_result: Trailing spaces should be trimmed. Trimmed name checked for uniqueness. No silent duplicate creation. (#3348 OPEN)
test_cases.notes: Bug #3348 (OPEN): trailing spaces → silent failure. AlreadyExistsException handling in PATCH returns conflicting project.

test_cases.title: Regression: Project deletion FK constraint on task templates (#2098)
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. A project with associated task templates.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, COUNT(tt.id) AS template_count FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.task_template tt ON p.id = tt.project_id GROUP BY p.id, p.name HAVING COUNT(tt.id) > 0 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. DB-CHECK: Identify a project with task templates
2. Login as ADMIN
3. Navigate to Admin panel > Projects
4. Try to delete the project (via API: DELETE /v1/projects/{id})
5. Verify error: should fail with FK constraint (task_template_project_fkey)
6. Verify error is handled gracefully (not raw 500 with SQL error)
7. Verify the project is NOT deleted
test_cases.expected_result: Project with task templates cannot be deleted. FK constraint prevents deletion. Should return meaningful error, not 500. (#2098)
test_cases.notes: Bug #2098 (closed): 500 on deletion with FK violation. No pre-check for dependent records.

test_cases.title: Regression: Project creation without required fields via API (#2053)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API: POST /v1/projects with minimal body (missing seniorManagerLogin and country)
2. Verify response — should either reject or accept
3. If accepted: verify project created in DB without these fields
4. Login as ADMIN
5. Navigate to Projects and find the created project
6. Verify how it displays without supervisor and country
7. Verify this is a security gap (UI enforces these but API doesn't)
CLEANUP: Delete test project if created
test_cases.expected_result: API should enforce required fields (seniorManagerLogin, country). Bug #2053: API allows creation without these fields. Security gap.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2053 (OPEN): API bypasses frontend validation for required fields.

test_cases.title: Regression: Firefox/Safari first calendar event creation (#2791)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user. Firefox or Safari browser.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open Firefox (or Safari) browser
2. Login as ADMIN
3. Navigate to Admin panel > Production calendars
4. Select a test calendar
5. Click 'Create a new event'
6. Select date, working hours, reason
7. Click 'Create'
8. Verify event is created successfully
9. If fails: check if 'Invalid date' was sent instead of ISO date
10. Verify this is fixed (bug #2791)
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar event creation works on Firefox/Safari. Bug #2791: first event sent 'Invalid date' on Firefox/Safari.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2791 (closed): Firefox/Safari date parsing issue. Verify fix.

test_cases.title: Regression: Duplicate project name returns generic error (#2674/#2125)
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user.
Query: SELECT p.name FROM ttt_backend.project p WHERE p.status = 'ACTIVE' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Note an existing project name
2. Via API: PATCH /v1/projects/{anotherProjectId} with the duplicate name
3. Verify response code — should be 400 or 409 (not 500)
4. Verify error message is informative: 'Project already exists' or similar
5. Login as ADMIN
6. Navigate to Projects > Edit Tracker Data for any project
7. If project name editing were possible, test duplicate name behavior
8. Document actual error message returned
test_cases.expected_result: Duplicate project name returns 400/409 with meaningful message, not 500. Bug #2125: PATCH returns 500 for duplicate. Bug #2674: generic error.
test_cases.notes: Bugs #2674, #2125 (closed): generic/500 errors on duplicate name.

test_cases.title: Regression: Employee sort by Manager causes SQL error (#2514)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Employees
3. Click to sort by Manager column (if available)
4. Verify sorting works without error
5. If sort fails: verify it's the BadSqlGrammarException from jOOQ (#2514)
6. Try sorting by other columns to verify they work
7. Document actual behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Sorting by Manager should work without SQL error. Bug #2514: BadSqlGrammarException in jOOQ. Verify fix.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2514 (closed): sort by Manager → BadSqlGrammarException.

test_cases.title: Regression: Contradictory role data between endpoints (#2063)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger CS sync to update employee data
2. Login as ADMIN
3. Navigate to Admin panel > Employees
4. Select an employee and check their roles
5. Via API: GET /v1/employees/{login}/roles
6. Compare roles from UI and API
7. Verify they match
8. If mismatch: cache not refreshed after sync (#2063)
test_cases.expected_result: Employee roles consistent between UI and API after sync. No contradictory data from stale cache.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2063 (closed): contradictory role data because cache not refreshed after CS sync.

test_cases.title: Regression: Accounting period — report month before confirmation (#3365)
test_cases.preconditions: ADMIN or ACCOUNTANT user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to Admin panel > Offices or Accounting > Periods
3. Try to select a report period month that is BEFORE the confirmation period
4. Verify the month is disabled (should not be selectable)
5. If selectable: try to save
6. Verify 400 error if saved (frontend validation gap)
7. Document the actual behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Report period month before confirmation period should be disabled. Bug #3365: not disabled, saving causes 400.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3365 (Sprint 15): frontend validation gap — disabled month not enforced.

test_cases.title: Regression: ClickUp tracker integration broken (#3148)
test_cases.preconditions: User with a ClickUp tracker configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as user with ClickUp tracker
2. Navigate to /admin/account > Trackers
3. Verify ClickUp tracker configuration displays
4. Try to sync tasks from ClickUp
5. Verify sync works or displays meaningful error
