1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select a project
4. Verify 'Actions' dropdown does NOT contain 'Copy table' in view-only mode
5. Click 'Open for editing' on an employee
6. Open Actions dropdown
7. Verify 'Copy table' option is now visible
test_cases.expected_result: Copy table is unavailable in read-only mode. It appears only after entering editing mode via 'Open for editing'.
test_cases.notes: Actions dropdown contents change based on editing mode state.

test_cases.title: Copy table — empty project produces empty or no-data clipboard
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with no assignments for current week.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select a project with no employees or no assignments in current date range
4. Enter editing mode
5. Click 'Copy table' from Actions dropdown
6. Paste clipboard into text editor
7. Verify output is empty or contains only headers
test_cases.expected_result: Copy table handles empty state gracefully — clipboard contains header row only or is empty. No error occurs.

test_cases.title: Copy table — includes all employees and weekly totals
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with 3+ employees, each with hours in current week.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with 3+ employees having hours
4. Enter editing mode
5. Click 'Copy table'
6. Paste into spreadsheet application
7. Count employee rows vs visible employees in planner
8. Verify totals column matches planner weekly total
test_cases.expected_result: All visible employees are included. Weekly/daily totals match those displayed in the planner grid. Data is tab-separated and imports cleanly into spreadsheets.
test_cases.notes: Verify row count matches planner. Totals row should be at bottom.

test_cases.title: #3294 — Delete task on current date reflects immediately
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with at least one task that has assignments today.
Query: SELECT pa.project_id, pa.task_id FROM planner_assignment pa WHERE pa.date = CURRENT_DATE AND pa.hours > 0 LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project and enter editing mode
4. Note the task name and current total hours
5. Right-click or use context menu on the task to delete
6. Confirm deletion in dialog
7. Verify task row disappears from the planner grid immediately
8. Verify total hours recalculates without the deleted task's hours
test_cases.expected_result: Task deletion on current date is reflected immediately in the UI. No manual refresh required. Total hours update to exclude deleted task.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3294: after deleting a task, the planner did not refresh and stale data remained visible.

test_cases.title: #3296 — Approved status preserved after tracker import
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with tracker configured. Employee with APPROVED status for current period.
Setup: Ensure at least one employee has approved hours for today.
Query: SELECT pa.employee_id, pa.project_id FROM planner_assignment pa WHERE pa.approved = true AND pa.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project with tracker
3. Note which employees have APPROVED status (green checkmark or indicator)
4. Open Actions dropdown → click 'Download hours from tracker'
5. Wait for sync to complete
6. Verify previously APPROVED employees still show APPROVED status
7. DB-CHECK: SELECT approved FROM planner_assignment WHERE employee_id = <id> AND date = <date>
test_cases.expected_result: Tracker import does NOT clear the APPROVED status on employees whose hours were already approved. Only unapproved hours are affected by import.
test_cases.requirement_ref: GitLab #3296 — approved status cleared after import
test_cases.notes: Bug #3296: tracker sync was resetting approved=false on all assignments regardless of prior approval state.

test_cases.title: #3375 — Employee order matches Project Members DnD order
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with 3+ employees.
Query: SELECT pe.project_id FROM project_employee pe GROUP BY pe.project_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project
3. Note the order of employees shown in the planner grid
4. Open Project Settings → Project Members tab
5. Drag an employee from position 3 to position 1
6. Click OK to save
7. Return to the planner grid for the same project
8. Verify employee order matches the new DnD order from Project Members
test_cases.expected_result: Planner employee display order matches the order configured in Project Settings → Project Members. Reordering members updates the planner grid order.
test_cases.notes: Project Settings icon: .planner__project-group-add .uikit-button. Members tab allows DnD reordering.

test_cases.title: #3258 — Project sorting alphabetical + green highlight on new task
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Employee assigned to 2+ projects, one having tasks added recently.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Verify projects are listed in alphabetical order in the left sidebar
4. Add a new task to a project via the search/add task input
5. Verify the newly added task row appears with a green highlight/animation
6. Verify project list order remains alphabetical after task addition
test_cases.expected_result: Projects sorted alphabetically. Newly added tasks show a transient green highlight. Alphabetical order is maintained after adding tasks.
test_cases.notes: #3258 was a major UI refactoring commit. Green highlight was part of the new UX.

test_cases.title: #3308 — DnD order persists across date navigation
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with 3+ tasks, editing mode enabled.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project
3. Enter editing mode
4. Reorder tasks via DnD (move task 3 to position 1)
5. Note the new task order
6. Navigate to next day using date navigation arrow
7. Verify task order on the new day matches the reordered sequence
8. Navigate back to original day
9. Verify order is still the reordered sequence
test_cases.expected_result: DnD task order persists when navigating between dates. The reorder is saved to the backend (linked-list position system) and displayed consistently across dates.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3308: order was only stored in frontend state and lost on date change. Fix uses PATCH /v1/assignments/reorder.

test_cases.title: #2832 — Changes visible via WebSocket without manual refresh
test_cases.preconditions: Two browser sessions logged into the same project. PM editing mode enabled in session A.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM in browser session A, enter editing mode on project
2. Login as same or different PM in browser session B, viewing same project
3. In session A: edit hours for an employee on a specific date (e.g., change 4h to 6h)
4. In session B: verify the hours update appears within 2-3 seconds WITHOUT manual refresh
5. In session A: add a new task to an employee
6. In session B: verify the new task row appears in real-time
test_cases.expected_result: WebSocket broadcasts PATCH and ADD events. Session B sees changes from session A in real-time without page refresh.
test_cases.notes: Requires two parallel browser sessions. WebSocket topic: /topic/assignments/{projectId}.

test_cases.title: #3406 — Refresh button error handling
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker configured but in an error state or with connection issues.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with tracker
4. Click 'Refresh tickets' from Actions dropdown
5. If tracker is unreachable: verify error banner appears with descriptive message
6. Verify the error banner text includes the tracker name and error type
7. Verify the planner remains functional (not stuck in loading state)
8. Verify clicking Refresh again retries the operation
test_cases.expected_result: Error banner shows when tracker refresh fails. Message is informative (e.g., 'Failed to load from tracker: connection timeout'). Planner remains usable. Retry is possible.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3406: error notification was not shown on refresh failure. Banner selector: [class*='notification'] or getByText with error message.

test_cases.title: #2447 — Open for editing preserves existing assignments
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project where an employee already has manually entered hours.
Query: SELECT pa.project_id, pa.employee_id FROM planner_assignment pa WHERE pa.hours > 0 AND pa.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' GROUP BY pa.project_id, pa.employee_id LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project, note existing hours for an employee
4. Click 'Open for editing' on that employee
5. Verify generate API creates only new task slots — does NOT overwrite existing hours
6. Verify the existing manual hours remain unchanged
7. DB-CHECK: SELECT hours FROM planner_assignment WHERE employee_id = <id> AND project_id = <pid> AND date = <date>
test_cases.expected_result: 'Open for editing' generates empty assignment slots for tasks without entries. Existing hours/assignments are NOT modified or zeroed out.
test_cases.notes: The generate endpoint (POST /v1/assignments/generate) should not overwrite existing assignments.

test_cases.title: #2302 — Tracker sync does not zero out TTT-only tasks
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with tracker configured. Employee has hours on a TTT-only task (task not in tracker).
Query: SELECT pa.project_id, pa.employee_id, t.name FROM planner_assignment pa JOIN task t ON pa.task_id = t.id WHERE pa.hours > 0 AND t.tracker_task_id IS NULL AND pa.date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project with tracker
3. Note employee with hours on a TTT-only task (no tracker link)
4. Note the exact hours value
5. Open Actions → 'Download hours from tracker'
6. Wait for sync to complete
7. Verify TTT-only task hours remain unchanged
8. DB-CHECK: SELECT hours FROM planner_assignment WHERE task_id = <ttt_only_task_id> AND employee_id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Tracker sync only updates hours for tracker-linked tasks. TTT-only tasks (tracker_task_id IS NULL) retain their hours.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2302: after tracker sync, hours on TTT-only tasks were being zeroed. This is a data loss scenario.

test_cases.title: #2338 — Tracker timeout shows partial success state
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker. Network conditions that may cause timeout (or large project).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select large project with many tracker tasks
3. Open Actions → 'Download hours from tracker'
4. If operation times out: verify error notification appears
5. Verify any tasks that DID sync before timeout show updated hours
6. Verify tasks that did NOT sync retain their previous hours
7. Verify no data corruption (hours are either old or correctly updated, never partially written)
test_cases.expected_result: Timeout during tracker sync shows error notification. Partially synced tasks show correct hours. Non-synced tasks retain original hours. No partial/corrupt data.
test_cases.notes: Difficult to reproduce reliably. May need a large project or simulated slow network.

test_cases.title: Tracker credential types — Jira token vs username/password
test_cases.preconditions:
Two projects: one with Jira Token auth, one with Username/Password auth.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, t.type, t.auth_type FROM project p JOIN tracker t ON p.tracker_id = t.id WHERE t.auth_type IN ('TOKEN', 'PASSWORD') LIMIT 2
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with Jira Token auth tracker
4. Open Actions → 'Load from tracker'
5. Verify sync succeeds with token-based auth
6. Switch to project with Username/Password auth tracker
7. Open Actions → 'Load from tracker'
8. Verify sync succeeds with password-based auth
9. If either fails: verify error message mentions auth type
test_cases.expected_result: Both credential types (Jira PAT token and username/password) work for tracker sync. Error messages on auth failure are specific to the credential type.
test_cases.notes: Tracker types in TTT: Jira, GitLab, Redmine, YouTrack, Toggl, Clockify, ClickUp. Auth types: TOKEN, PASSWORD.

test_cases.title: Load from tracker vs Refresh tickets — distinct behaviors
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker configured and existing tracked tasks.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project with tracker
3. Open Actions dropdown and note available tracker options
4. Click 'Refresh tickets' — verify it updates the task list (names, new tickets) but NOT hours
5. Note that hours values remain unchanged after 'Refresh tickets'
6. Click 'Download hours from tracker' — verify it updates hours values for the current date range
7. Compare: Refresh = task metadata, Download = hours data
test_cases.expected_result: 'Refresh tickets' syncs task names/IDs from tracker without changing hours. 'Download hours from tracker' syncs actual reported hours. These are distinct operations with different outcomes.
test_cases.notes: Users confuse these two operations. Refresh = GET tasks from tracker. Download = GET hours/worklogs.

test_cases.title: Download hours from Actions dropdown UI flow
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with tracker and at least one employee with tracker hours.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project with tracker
3. Enter editing mode
4. Open 'Actions' dropdown
5. Click 'Download hours from tracker'
6. Verify loading indicator appears during sync
7. Verify hours cells update with tracker data after sync completes
8. Verify success notification or banner appears
test_cases.expected_result: Download hours operation shows loading state, then updates hours cells with tracker data. Success feedback is shown.
test_cases.notes: Actions dropdown items: 'Copy table', 'Download hours from tracker', 'Approve all', 'Refresh tickets'.

test_cases.title: #3296 — Approve all not reset by subsequent tracker import
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with tracker. Employee hours present.
SETUP: Ensure employees have reported hours for current week.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab, select project
3. Enter editing mode
4. Open Actions → 'Approve all' — approve all employees' hours
5. Verify all employees show approved status
6. Open Actions → 'Download hours from tracker'
7. Wait for sync to complete
8. Verify previously approved employees STILL show approved status
9. DB-CHECK: SELECT approved FROM planner_assignment WHERE project_id = <pid> AND date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'
test_cases.expected_result: Bulk 'Approve all' status is NOT cleared by subsequent tracker import. Approved flag persists through sync.
test_cases.notes: Related to TC-PLN-063 but tests the 'Approve all' + import sequence specifically.

test_cases.title: Tracker not configured — per-employee error state
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Employee without personal tracker credentials on a project that requires them.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Select project with tracker requiring per-employee credentials
4. Click 'Open for editing' on an employee who has NOT configured tracker
5. Attempt to load hours from tracker for that employee
6. Verify error banner or indicator specific to that employee
7. Verify other employees with configured trackers can still sync normally
test_cases.expected_result: Error is scoped to the specific employee without tracker credentials. Other employees' tracker operations are unaffected. Error message indicates credential issue.
test_cases.notes: Each employee may need their own tracker PAT. Missing credentials should show per-employee error, not block the whole project.

test_cases.title: Project Settings — 'Changes saved' notification on member add
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with available employees not yet added.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM project_employee WHERE project_id = <test_project_id>) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner → Tasks tab
3. Click the Project Settings icon (gear icon near project name)
4. In the 'Project settings' dialog, go to 'Project members' tab
5. Click the member dropdown/combobox
6. Select an employee not yet in the project
7. Click 'Add' or press Enter
8. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
9. Verify the new member appears in the members list
test_cases.expected_result: 'Changes saved' notification appears after adding a member. New member visible in the list immediately.
test_cases.notes: Project Settings dialog: getByRole('dialog', { name: 'Project settings' }). Members tab: getByRole('tab', { name: 'Project members' }).

test_cases.title: Project Settings — member dropdown filters already-added members
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with 2+ existing members and at least one available employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings → Project Members tab
3. Note existing members in the list
4. Click the member dropdown/combobox
5. Type the name of an already-added member
6. Verify the dropdown does NOT show the already-added member
7. Type the name of an available (not yet added) employee
8. Verify the dropdown DOES show that employee
test_cases.expected_result: Member dropdown filters out employees already in the project. Only available employees appear in suggestions.
test_cases.notes: Prevents duplicate member additions. Combobox uses typeahead filtering.

test_cases.title: Project Settings — inline role editing with Enter to save
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with at least one member whose role can be changed.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings → Project Members tab
3. Find a member in the list
4. Click on the member's role field (e.g., 'Developer')
5. Verify the role field becomes editable (inline edit mode)
6. Change the role to a different value (e.g., 'QA')
7. Press Enter to save
8. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
9. Verify the role value updates in the member list
test_cases.expected_result: Role field supports inline editing. Enter key saves the change. Success notification confirms the save.
test_cases.notes: Role editing is done inline in the members table, not via a separate dialog.

test_cases.title: Project Settings — delete member with confirmation
test_cases.preconditions:
PM logged in. Project with a member that can be safely removed (no critical assignments).
SETUP: Add a test member to the project if needed via API.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings → Project Members tab
3. Find the test member in the list
4. Click the trash/delete icon next to the member
5. Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog (if any)
6. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
7. Verify the member is removed from the list
8. Close Project Settings
9. Verify the member no longer appears in the planner grid
CLEANUP: Re-add the member if needed for other tests.
test_cases.expected_result: Member deletion via trash icon removes the member from the project. Success notification shown. Member disappears from both the settings dialog and the planner grid.
test_cases.notes: Trash icon next to each member row. May or may not have a confirmation dialog.

test_cases.title: Project Settings — tag CRUD with notifications
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with close-by-tag feature enabled.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings → 'Tasks closing' tab
3. Type a new tag name in the 'Add a tag' input
4. Press Enter or click Add
5. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
6. Verify the new tag appears in the tag list
7. Click delete icon on the tag
8. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
9. Verify the tag is removed from the list
test_cases.expected_result: Tag creation and deletion both show 'Changes saved' notification. Tags are added/removed from the visible list immediately.
test_cases.notes: Tags tab: getByRole('tab', { name: 'Tasks closing' }). Tag input: getByPlaceholder('Add a tag'). Close-by-tag is covered in t2724.xlsx but the Project Settings UI for tag CRUD is planner scope.

test_cases.title: Project Settings — Tags tab informational text
test_cases.preconditions: PM logged in. Project with close-by-tag feature.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings → 'Tasks closing' tab
3. Read the informational text at the top of the tab
4. Verify it explains that tags are used to close tasks matching the tag pattern
5. Verify the text matches the Confluence specification for close-by-tag feature
test_cases.expected_result: Informational text on Tags tab clearly explains the close-by-tag functionality. Text matches the specification from Confluence.
test_cases.notes: Verifies that the UI provides adequate context for the close-by-tag feature.

test_cases.title: Create a time report — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with at least one active project and assigned task.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_project ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.project p ON ep.project_id = p.id JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON t.project_id = p.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND p.status = 'ACTIVE' AND e.login != 'pvaynmaster' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks page (/report)
3. Verify the weekly grid is displayed with task rows and day columns
4. Click on an empty cell in the current week for an assigned task
5. Verify an input field appears in the cell
6. Type '4' (hours)
7. Press Enter or click outside the cell
8. Verify the cell displays '4' and the weekly/daily totals update
9. Verify the cell background is white (REPORTED state)
DB-CHECK: SELECT effort FROM ttt_backend.task_report WHERE executor_login = '<login>' AND task_id = <task_id> AND report_date = '<date>' — verify effort = 240 (minutes)
test_cases.expected_result: Report created with 4 hours (240 minutes). Cell shows '4'. Weekly row total and daily column total both increase by 4. Cell state is REPORTED (white background).
test_cases.notes: Effort stored in minutes in DB (×60). LockService prevents concurrent edits — cell shows lock icon if already being edited by another user.

test_cases.title: Edit existing report — change hours
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing report in REPORTED state.
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports with effort=120 (2 hours).
Query: (same as TC-RPT-001)
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a report for 2 hours on a weekday in the current open period
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Navigate to the week containing the setup report
4. Find the cell showing '2'
5. Click the cell to activate edit mode
6. Clear and type '6'
7. Press Enter
8. Verify the cell now shows '6'
9. Verify weekly/daily totals updated (+4)
10. Verify cell background is white (edit resets to REPORTED state)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/ttt/v1/reports with the report ID
test_cases.expected_result: Report updated to 6 hours. State reset to REPORTED (even if was APPROVED before). Totals reflect change.
test_cases.notes: Any effort change resets state to REPORTED and clears approver. This is a key regression test (state should always reset).

test_cases.title: Delete report by setting hours to 0
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing report.
SETUP: Via API — create a report for 3 hours.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports with effort=180
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Find the cell showing '3'
4. Click the cell, clear content, type '0'
5. Press Enter
6. Verify the cell is now empty (report deleted)
7. Verify weekly/daily totals decreased by 3
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.task_report WHERE executor_login = '<login>' AND report_date = '<date>' AND task_id = <task_id> — verify 0 (deleted)
test_cases.expected_result: Setting effort to 0 deletes the report. Cell empty, totals decreased. No DB record exists.
test_cases.notes: Effort=0 triggers deletion, not update to zero. This is an important behavioral detail.

test_cases.title: Report in closed period — blocked
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in a salary office where report period is ahead of the target date.
Query: SELECT e.login, op.start AS period_start FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.office_period op ON e.salary_office_id = op.office_id WHERE op.type = 'REPORT' AND e.enabled = true AND e.login != 'pvaynmaster' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Navigate to a week before the office report period start date
4. Verify cells for dates before the period start are NOT editable (grayed out or no input)
5. Attempt to click a closed-period cell
6. Verify no input field appears or input is rejected
test_cases.expected_result: Cells before report period start date are read-only. No editing possible for closed periods.
test_cases.notes: Period check: reportDate < office report period start → blocked. Extended period can override for individual employees.

test_cases.title: Add new task on My Tasks page
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee assigned to a project with available (unselected) tasks.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_project ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.project p ON ep.project_id = p.id JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON t.project_id = p.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND p.status = 'ACTIVE' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Click 'Add task' button (or '+' icon)
4. In the task selection dropdown/dialog, search for an available task
5. Select the task
6. Verify the task appears as a new row in the weekly grid
7. Verify the row has empty cells ready for reporting
test_cases.expected_result: New task row added to the grid. Task name displayed. Empty cells available for time entry.
test_cases.notes: Task visibility depends on employee-project assignment. Pinned tasks always appear.

test_cases.title: Pin/unpin task
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with at least one visible task.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-005)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Locate a task row in the grid
4. Click the pin icon (📌) on the task row
5. Verify pin icon becomes active/highlighted
6. Navigate to a different week
7. Verify the pinned task still appears even if no reports exist in that week
8. Navigate back, click the pin icon again to unpin
9. Verify task disappears from weeks where it has no reports
DB-CHECK: SELECT * FROM ttt_backend.pinned_employee_task WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND task_id = <task_id>
test_cases.expected_result: Pinned task visible on all weeks. Unpinned task only visible on weeks with existing reports.
test_cases.notes: POST /v1/reports/pin/{taskId}/employees/{login} to pin, DELETE to unpin.

test_cases.title: Rename task on My Tasks page
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a task that they can rename.
SETUP: Via API — ensure employee has at least one task.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Right-click (or click context menu) on a task name
4. Select 'Rename' option
5. In the rename popup, enter a new name
6. Select 'for all time' option
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify task name updates in the grid
test_cases.expected_result: Task name updated. All employees see the new name.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3278: rename in Confirmation popup triggers 400 'Employee login not found'. Verify rename works on My Tasks (should work).

test_cases.title: Week navigation — arrow buttons
test_cases.preconditions: Logged-in employee with tasks.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Note the current displayed week (Mon-Sun dates in header)
4. Click the right arrow (→) to advance one week
5. Verify the header updates to next week's dates
6. Verify task rows update with that week's data
7. Click the left arrow (←) twice
8. Verify navigating to the previous week's data
9. Verify totals recalculate per week
test_cases.expected_result: Week navigation arrows advance/retreat by one week. Header dates, cell data, and totals all update correctly.
test_cases.notes: 6 week tabs visible. Navigation beyond visible range shifts the tab set.

test_cases.title: Batch create reports — multiple cells in one week
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with multiple tasks.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_project ep ON e.id = ep.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.project p ON ep.project_id = p.id JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON t.project_id = p.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND p.status = 'ACTIVE' GROUP BY e.login HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.id) >= 2 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Click empty cell on Task 1, Monday — type '4', press Tab
4. Verify cursor moves to next cell (Tuesday)
5. Type '4', press Tab
6. Navigate to Task 2, Monday — type '2'
7. Press Enter
8. Verify all three cells saved correctly
9. Verify daily totals for Monday show 6 (4+2)
10. Verify weekly total for Task 1 shows 8
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all created reports
test_cases.expected_result: Multiple reports created efficiently. Tab key navigates between cells. All totals calculate correctly.
test_cases.notes: PUT /v1/reports batch endpoint used for multi-cell saves. Tab navigation is a key UX feature.

test_cases.title: Report with decimal hours (e.g., 1.5)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a task and open period.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Click empty cell, type '1.5'
4. Press Enter
5. Verify cell displays '1.5'
6. Verify totals include 1.5 in calculation
DB-CHECK: SELECT effort FROM ttt_backend.task_report — verify effort = 90 (minutes)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Decimal hours accepted. 1.5 hours stored as 90 minutes. Display shows '1.5'. Totals correct.
test_cases.notes: Decimal separator depends on user's office settings (dot or comma). Check config: decimalSeparator.

test_cases.title: TAB key stacking bug (regression #3398)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee on My Tasks page.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Click on an empty cell
4. Verify single input field appears
5. Press TAB key without entering a value
6. Verify cursor moves to next cell cleanly
7. Repeat TAB 3-4 more times rapidly
8. Verify NO duplicate/stacked input fields appear
9. Verify the grid remains visually clean
test_cases.expected_result: Tab navigation between cells does not create stacked/duplicate input fields. Each cell has at most one input.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3398: production report of duplicate input fields on TAB. If still present, document as known issue.

test_cases.title: Report comment — add and view
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing report.
SETUP: Via API — create a report.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create report for 4 hours
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Find the cell with the report
4. Hover over or click the cell to see options
5. Click comment icon (or right-click context menu)
6. Enter a comment: 'Code review for feature X'
7. Save the comment
8. Hover over the cell
9. Verify comment tooltip shows 'Code review for feature X'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: Comment saved and visible on hover. Comment persists across page reloads.
test_cases.notes: Comments are stored via PATCH on the report entity. stateComment field on the task_report.

test_cases.title: Cell locking — concurrent edit prevention
test_cases.preconditions:
Two accounts: employee and a manager who can report on their behalf.
Query: SELECT e.login AS employee, m.login AS manager FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee m ON m.enabled = true WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.login != m.login AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_project ep WHERE ep.employee_id = e.id) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee in Browser 1
2. Navigate to My Tasks, click a cell to start editing
3. Login as the manager in Browser 2
4. Navigate to the employee's report page (/report/<employee_login>)
5. Attempt to click the same cell
6. Verify a lock icon or 'cell locked' message appears
7. Verify the manager cannot edit the locked cell
8. In Browser 1, save or cancel the edit
9. In Browser 2, verify the cell becomes editable
test_cases.expected_result: Cell shows lock icon when another user is editing. HTTP 423 Locked returned for concurrent edit attempts. Lock released after save/cancel.
test_cases.notes: LockService uses in-memory map. Batch PUT skips locking. Lock timeout not documented.

test_cases.title: View another employee's report page (manager)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with REPORTS_VIEW permission and a subordinate.
Query: SELECT m.login AS manager, e.login AS employee FROM ttt_backend.employee m JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON e.enabled = true WHERE m.enabled = true AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_role er JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE er.employee_id = m.id AND r.name IN ('ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER', 'ROLE_ADMIN')) AND m.login != e.login ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to /report/<employee_login>
3. Verify the employee's report page loads
4. Verify the header shows the employee's name
5. Verify the weekly grid shows the employee's tasks and hours
6. Verify the manager can edit cells (if REPORTS_EDIT permission exists)
test_cases.expected_result: Manager can view and potentially edit employee's report page. Same grid layout with employee's data.
test_cases.notes: Contractor report page has known bug #3150: infinite spinner when opened from Statistics/Admin links.

test_cases.title: Contractor report page — spinner bug (regression #3150)
test_cases.preconditions:
A contractor employee and a manager/admin.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_CONTRACTOR' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as an admin/manager
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Find the contractor in the table
4. Click the contractor's name to open their report page
5. Verify the page loads without infinite spinner
6. If spinner occurs: note the error in console (global error expected)
7. Try direct navigation to /report/<contractor_login>
8. Note if the behavior differs between Statistics link and direct URL
test_cases.expected_result: Contractor's report page loads successfully from all entry points. No infinite spinner or global error.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3150 (OPEN since Nov 2024): Global error and spinner on contractor report from Statistics/Admin. Does NOT occur on contractor's own My Tasks page.

test_cases.title: Approve hours — By Projects tab, single task
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with REPORTS_APPROVE permission. Employee with REPORTED hours in current approval period.
SETUP: Via API — create a report for the employee.
Query: SELECT m.login AS manager, e.login AS employee, t.id AS task_id FROM ttt_backend.employee m JOIN ttt_backend.employee_project mp ON m.id = mp.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.project p ON mp.project_id = p.id JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON t.project_id = p.id JOIN ttt_backend.employee_project ep ON ep.project_id = p.id JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON ep.employee_id = e.id WHERE m.enabled = true AND e.enabled = true AND m.login != e.login AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_role er JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE er.employee_id = m.id AND r.name IN ('ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER', 'ROLE_SENIOR_MANAGER', 'ROLE_ADMIN')) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports with executor=employee, task=task_id, effort=480 (8h), date=today
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation page (/approve)
3. Click 'By Projects' tab
4. Select the project from the Project dropdown
5. Navigate to the week containing the report
6. Find the employee's row and the task with 8 hours
7. Click the approve (✓) button on the task row for the specific week
8. Verify success notification or visual state change (green background)
9. Verify the cell shows green/approved background color
DB-CHECK: SELECT state FROM ttt_backend.task_report WHERE id = <report_id> — verify 'APPROVED'
CLEANUP: Via API — PATCH report state back to REPORTED, then DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Hours approved. Cell shows green (APPROVED) background. DB state is APPROVED. Approver login recorded.
test_cases.notes: Approve sets state=APPROVED and records approverLogin. PROJECT_MANAGER type projects cannot be approved.

test_cases.title: Approve hours — By Employee tab
test_cases.preconditions:
Same as TC-RPT-016.
SETUP: Via API — create a report.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a report for the employee
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation page (/approve)
3. Click 'By Employee' tab (default)
4. Select the employee from the Employee dropdown
5. Navigate to the week containing the report
6. Find the task row with hours
7. Click the approve button
8. Verify approval succeeds
9. Verify Approve button behavior after approval (should not advance to next user — #3368 bug)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: Hours approved via By Employee tab. Cell shows green background.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3368: Approve button on 'By Employee' doesn't switch to next user or change work week (unlike By Projects).

test_cases.title: Reject hours with comment
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with a report to approve.
SETUP: Via API — create a report.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a report for the employee
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation page (/approve)
3. Select 'By Projects' tab, choose the project
4. Find the employee's report cell
5. Click the reject (✗) button
6. Verify rejection tooltip/popup appears with a textarea
7. Type a comment: 'Please split by subtasks'
8. Click confirm/reject button in the tooltip
9. Verify cell shows red/rejected background
10. Verify the comment is stored
DB-CHECK: SELECT r.description FROM ttt_backend.reject r JOIN ttt_backend.task_report tr ON tr.reject_id = r.id WHERE tr.id = <report_id>
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: Hours rejected with comment. Cell shows red (REJECTED) background. Reject record created in DB with the comment.
test_cases.notes: Comment optional in API but UI shows textarea. Reject creates a separate 'reject' table record.

test_cases.title: Re-report after rejection — clears rejected state
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a REJECTED report.
SETUP: Via API — create report, then reject it via PATCH state=REJECTED.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create report (4h), then PATCH state to REJECTED with stateComment='Fix'
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Find the cell with rejected report (red background)
4. Verify rejection indicator visible (red background or icon)
5. Click the cell, change hours from 4 to 6
6. Press Enter
7. Verify cell reverts to white background (REPORTED state)
8. Verify rejection indicator disappears
DB-CHECK: SELECT state, reject_id FROM ttt_backend.task_report WHERE id = <report_id> — verify state='REPORTED', reject_id=NULL
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.reject WHERE id = <old_reject_id> — verify 0 (deleted)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: Re-reporting deletes the reject record and resets state to REPORTED. No rejection history preserved.
test_cases.notes: Critical: reject record is DELETED (not archived). No history of the previous rejection remains.

test_cases.title: Bulk approve — 'Approve all' header button
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with multiple employees having REPORTED hours.
SETUP: Via API — create reports for 2+ employees.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create 2h reports for Employee A and Employee B on same day/task
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation page (/approve)
3. Select 'By Projects', choose the project
4. Navigate to the week with the setup reports
5. Click the 'Approve all' button in the week header
6. Verify confirmation dialog appears ('Approve all active tasks for this week?')
7. Confirm
8. Verify all REPORTED cells turn green (APPROVED)
9. Verify no APPROVED cells were double-processed
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all created reports
test_cases.expected_result: All REPORTED hours in the week approved in one action. Already APPROVED hours unaffected.
test_cases.notes: Granularity levels per #3354: per-cell, per-line (task), all active tasks, 'Approve all' header. Each should handle manual and auto-rejected hours identically.

test_cases.title: Confirmation — 'By Employee' vs 'By Projects' data parity
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with employees having various report states.
SETUP: Via API — create reports with mixed states (REPORTED, APPROVED, REJECTED).
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create 3 reports for same employee: one REPORTED, one APPROVED, one REJECTED
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation (/approve)
3. On 'By Projects' tab: select the project, note all cells/states for the employee
4. Switch to 'By Employee' tab: select the same employee
5. Compare: same tasks, same hours, same states visible?
6. Verify both tabs show identical data for the same employee/week
7. Check over/under-reporting notification appears on both tabs (#3368)
8. Note any discrepancies
test_cases.expected_result: Both tabs show identical report data for the same employee. Over/under notifications consistent.
test_cases.notes: Known issue #3368: 'By Employee' doesn't call statistic/report/employees endpoint → missing over/under notification. Multiple sub-bugs documented.

test_cases.title: Confirmation — orange dot on week tabs with pending items
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with reports to approve in specific weeks.
SETUP: Via API — create reports in different weeks.

2. Navigate to Confirmation (/approve)

4. Observe the 6 week tabs at top

6. Verify Week 2 tab has NO orange dot
7. Click Week 1 tab → verify pending reports visible
8. Click Week 2 tab → verify no pending reports
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the reports
test_cases.expected_result: Orange dots appear only on week tabs with pending (REPORTED) items. Empty weeks have no indicator.
test_cases.notes: Orange dot is the visual indicator for weeks needing attention.

test_cases.title: Rename task in Confirmation — bug regression (#3278)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager on Confirmation page with a task to rename.

test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a report for the employee
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation (/approve) > By Projects
3. Find a task name in the confirmation grid
4. Click the task name or right-click for context menu
5. Select 'Rename'
6. In the rename popup, enter a new name
7. For scope, select 'For all employees' (all checked)

CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report

test_cases.notes: Bug #3278: rename in Confirmation triggered 400 'Employee login not found'. Also: rename for single employee on By Projects closes popup without effect.

test_cases.title: Confirmation — employee dropdown update after approve all (#3267)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with employee having REPORTED hours.

test_cases.title: Over/under-reporting notification on Confirmation page
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who has reported significantly more or less than their norm.
SETUP: Via API — create reports totaling >110% of monthly norm.
Query: SELECT e.login, sr.month_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON sr.employee_login = e.login WHERE sr.report_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) AND sr.month_norm > 0 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create enough REPORTED hours to exceed 110% of the employee's monthly norm
1. Login as a manager with REPORTS_APPROVE permission
2. Navigate to Confirmation (/approve) > By Projects
3. Select the project containing the over-reporting employee
4. Verify a non-dismissible banner appears at the top
5. Verify the employee's name is highlighted in red (over-reporting)
6. Click or hover on the clock icon next to the name
7. Verify tooltip shows: deviation %, month, DM, projects with PM names
8. Switch to 'By Employee' tab
9. Verify the same over-reporting notification appears (#3368 known issue)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the excess reports
test_cases.expected_result: Over-reporting banner visible on both tabs. Employee name highlighted red. Tooltip shows detailed deviation info.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3368: 'By Employee' missing over/under notification. Also: period-dependent bug — approve month < current month AND report month = current month → missing on By Employee.

test_cases.title: Reject notification — rejected hours banner on My Tasks (#3321, #3268)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with REJECTED reports.
SETUP: Via API — create report, then reject it.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create report (4h), PATCH state=REJECTED with stateComment='Incorrect task'
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
3. Verify a rejection notification banner appears at the top of the page
4. Verify banner text indicates which task has rejected hours
5. Verify the rejection comment is visible (hover or click)
6. Click the link/button in the banner to navigate to the rejected week
7. Verify the rejected cell is highlighted (red background)
8. Verify no HTTP 400 'Period should be less than two months' error in console
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: Rejection banner visible. Comment accessible. Navigation link works. No API errors.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3268: backend 2-month sampling restriction caused 400 on notification API. Bug #3321: no notification when report month closed but confirmation open.

test_cases.title: Approve hours in closed report period (confirmation still open)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager approving hours where report period is closed but approve period is still open.
Query: SELECT op_r.start AS report_start, op_a.start AS approve_start, op_r.office_id FROM ttt_backend.office_period op_r JOIN ttt_backend.office_period op_a ON op_r.office_id = op_a.office_id WHERE op_r.type = 'REPORT' AND op_a.type = 'APPROVE' AND op_a.start < op_r.start
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation (/approve)
3. Select the salary office/project where report period is ahead of approve period
4. Navigate to a week in the gap (between approve period start and report period start)
5. Find REPORTED hours in this period
6. Approve them
7. Verify approval succeeds (report period closed doesn't block approval)
CLEANUP: Via API — revert state if needed
test_cases.expected_result: Approval succeeds for hours in the approve period even when report period is closed. The two periods are independent.
test_cases.notes: Key distinction: approve period controls confirmation, report period controls data entry. They are NOT the same.

test_cases.title: Advance report period by 1 month — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant or admin with access to Accounting > Changing periods page.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_ACCOUNTANT', 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT', 'ROLE_ADMIN') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods page
3. Select a salary office from the dropdown
4. Note the current Report period month
5. Click 'Edit' button for the Report period
6. In the period selection popup, select the next month (+1)
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify the report period advances by 1 month
9. Verify success notification appears
10. Verify the new period is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Report period advanced by 1 month. New period saved and displayed. Old period dates become read-only on My Tasks.
test_cases.notes: Report period must be first of month. Must be >= approve period.

test_cases.title: Advance approve period by 1 month
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant or admin. Approve period is at least 1 month behind report period.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-028)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Note current Approve period and Report period
5. Click 'Edit' for the Approve period
6. Select next month (+1) — must still be <= report period
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify approve period advances
9. Verify PeriodChangedEvent side effects:
   - Auto-reject of unapproved hours (if feature enabled)
   - Vacation recalculation triggered
test_cases.expected_result: Approve period advanced. Side effects triggered (auto-reject, vacation recalc). Cannot exceed report period.
test_cases.notes: Bug cluster #3350: max 1-month delta enforced (both directions). Validation uses SAVED values, not dynamic.

test_cases.title: Period — 2+ month jump blocked (#3350)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant on Changing periods page.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-028)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for the Report period
5. Attempt to select a month that is 2+ months ahead of current saved value
6. Verify the month is DISABLED in the calendar picker (not selectable)
7. If selectable: click 'Save' and verify 400 error from backend
8. Repeat for Approve period: attempt 2+ month jump
9. Verify both periods enforce 1-month max delta
test_cases.expected_result: Months more than 1 ahead/behind the SAVED value are disabled in the UI picker. If somehow submitted, backend returns 400.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3350 QA bugs: UI used DYNAMIC values instead of SAVED values. Partial saves possible. Sequential saves required for multi-month changes.

test_cases.title: Period — saved vs dynamic value validation (#3350 bug 4)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant on Changing periods page.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-028)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for Report period
5. Select 1 month forward — do NOT save yet
6. Click 'Edit' for Approve period
7. Verify: available months for Approve period are calculated from the SAVED report period, not the unsaved selection
8. If dynamic values are used (bug): the approve period allows months it shouldn't
9. Save Report period first, then verify Approve period options update
test_cases.expected_result: Period validation uses SAVED values. Unsaved changes to one period do not affect the other period's validation.
test_cases.notes: Key QA finding from #3350: popup used DYNAMIC (unsaved) values instead of SAVED values for available months.

test_cases.title: Report period preceding approve period — disabled (#3365)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant on Changing periods page.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-028)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for the Report period
5. Verify months BEFORE the saved approve period are DISABLED
6. Attempt to select a month before approve period
7. Verify it cannot be selected
8. If selectable: save and verify 400 error
9. Close without saving
test_cases.expected_result: Report period months before approve period are disabled. Constraint: report period >= approve period.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3365 (OPEN): Month preceding saved confirmation period not disabled for report period selection. Partially fixed.

test_cases.title: Extended report period — grant to individual employee
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant/admin. Employee whose office report period is already closed for a specific month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select the salary office
4. Find the employee who needs an extension
5. Click 'Extend' or 'Grant extended period' for the employee
6. Select the month to extend
7. Save the extension
8. Login as the employee
9. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
10. Navigate to the extended period month
11. Verify cells in the extended period are EDITABLE (not locked)
12. Enter a report value
13. Verify report saved successfully
test_cases.expected_result: Extended period granted. Employee can report in the otherwise-closed period. Extension visible in admin view.
test_cases.notes: PUT /v1/periods/report/employees/{login}. Auto-cleaned by ExtendedPeriodScheduler every 5 minutes. Blocks approve period advancement.

test_cases.title: Extended period blocks approve period advancement
test_cases.preconditions: Accountant/admin. At least one employee with active extended period in the salary office.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — PUT /v1/periods/report/employees/{login} to grant extended period
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select the salary office with the extended period
4. Attempt to advance the Approve period
5. Verify the advance is BLOCKED with an error message
6. Verify the error indicates which employee has the extended period
7. Remove the extended period (via admin or wait for auto-cleanup)
8. Re-attempt approve period advancement
9. Verify it now succeeds
test_cases.expected_result: Approve period cannot advance while extended periods exist. Error message identifies the blocking employee.
test_cases.notes: ExtendedPeriodScheduler auto-cleans every 5 minutes. Manual removal also possible.

test_cases.title: Period — partial save prevention (#3350 bug 4)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant on Changing periods page.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-028)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select a salary office
4. Click 'Edit' for Report period — select +1 month
5. Click 'Edit' for Approve period — select a value that would become invalid if report period saves first
6. Click 'Save' (whichever save button is available)
7. Verify BOTH periods are validated together
8. If one saves and the other fails: this is a partial save bug
9. Verify that a failed save for one period does NOT save the other
test_cases.expected_result: Both periods validated as a pair. No partial saves — either both succeed or both fail.
test_cases.notes: Known issue: one period may save while the other's validation fails. Result: inconsistent state.

test_cases.title: Auto-reject — unapproved hours rejected on approve period close
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Employee with REPORTED (unapproved) hours in the month being closed.
SETUP: Via API — create REPORTED hours, verify approve period is 1 month behind.
Note: Auto-reject feature was DISABLED (reverted) — this test verifies current behavior.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create REPORTED hours for the employee in the approve period month
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting > Changing periods
3. Select the salary office
4. Note REPORTED hours exist for the closing month (verify via Confirmation page)
5. Advance the Approve period by 1 month (closing the current month)
6. After save, navigate to Confirmation page
7. Check if REPORTED hours in the closed month are now REJECTED (auto-reject triggered)
8. Or: check if they remain REPORTED (auto-reject disabled)
9. Login as the employee
10. Navigate to My Tasks — check for auto-rejection notification banner
DB-CHECK: SELECT state FROM ttt_backend.task_report WHERE executor_login = '<login>' AND report_date IN (closed period)
test_cases.expected_result: If auto-reject is active: REPORTED hours become REJECTED with 'auto.reject.state' comment. Notification banner on My Tasks. If disabled: hours remain REPORTED.
test_cases.notes: Feature reverted from release/2.1 (#3285). Code exists but may be disabled. Test to verify current state. If active, triggers on approve period change (not report period).

test_cases.title: Auto-reject ONLY triggers on approve period change (not report period)
test_cases.preconditions:
Reports exist in REPORTED state. Both periods can be changed.
SETUP: Via API — create REPORTED hours.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create REPORTED hours in the month between approve and report period
1. Login as accountant
2. Advance the REPORT period by 1 month
3. Check: REPORTED hours in the gap → should remain REPORTED (not auto-rejected)
4. Verify no auto-rejection notification sent to employee
5. Now advance the APPROVE period by 1 month
6. Check: if auto-reject is active, REPORTED hours in the newly closed approve month → REJECTED
7. Confirm the trigger is approve period, NOT report period
test_cases.expected_result: Report period closure does NOT trigger auto-reject. Only approve period closure triggers it.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3285: auto-reject was incorrectly triggered on REPORT period closure. The bug was reverted. This test verifies it's fixed.

test_cases.title: Auto-reject — notification banner on My Tasks
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with auto-rejected hours (if feature is active).
SETUP: Must trigger auto-reject first (requires approve period advancement).
test_cases.steps:
1. Trigger auto-reject by advancing approve period (see TC-RPT-036)
2. Login as the affected employee
3. Navigate to My Tasks (/report)
4. Verify notification banner at top: 'Unconfirmed hours for task {name} were automatically rejected upon month closure'
5. Click 'Go to the report page' link in the banner
6. Verify navigation to the first week with rejected hours
7. Click the X button to dismiss the banner
8. Navigate away and return
9. Verify banner does NOT reappear (dismissed via localStorage)
10. Clear browser data and reload
11. Verify banner reappears (#2698 known edge case)
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-reject banner visible with task name. 'Go to report' link navigates correctly. Dismiss persists via localStorage.
test_cases.notes: AutoRejectedReportsContainer on ReportPage. localStorage key: 'hiddenAutoRejectWarnings'. Banner reappears after site data clear.

test_cases.title: Auto-reject — reopen period and re-confirm/edit (#3367)
test_cases.preconditions:
Approve period was advanced (auto-reject triggered), then accountant reopens period.
SETUP: Requires full cycle: create reports → close period → auto-reject → reopen.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Create REPORTED hours → advance approve period (triggers auto-reject) → revert approve period (reopen)
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation page
3. Find auto-rejected hours in the reopened period
4. Attempt to approve the auto-rejected hours
5. Verify approval succeeds WITHOUT HTTP 500 error
6. Login as the employee
7. Navigate to My Tasks for the reopened period
8. Attempt to edit an auto-rejected cell
9. Verify editing works WITHOUT HTTP 500 error
10. Verify re-reporting clears the auto-reject state
test_cases.expected_result: After period reopen: confirmation and editing of auto-rejected hours works. No 500 errors.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3367 (CLOSED): after period close→auto-reject→reopen, confirmation failed with 500. Fix in MR !5095.

test_cases.title: Auto-reject vs manual reject — identical behavior (#3354)
test_cases.preconditions:
Reports in both auto-rejected and manually rejected states.
SETUP: Create reports, auto-reject some via period close, manually reject others.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Create 4 reports. Close approve period (auto-rejects 2). Manually reject 2 others via Confirmation.
1. Reopen the approve period
2. Login as manager
3. On Confirmation page, attempt to approve an auto-rejected report
4. Verify behavior identical to approving a manually rejected report
5. Login as employee
6. On My Tasks, attempt to re-report on an auto-rejected cell
7. Verify behavior identical to re-reporting on a manually rejected cell
8. Compare: both types clear the reject record, reset to REPORTED state
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-rejected and manually rejected reports behave identically for all operations: approve, re-report, edit.
test_cases.notes: Requirement from #3354: auto-rejected hours MUST behave identically to manually rejected ones after period reopening.

test_cases.title: Statistics — Employee reports table basic view
test_cases.preconditions:
User with access to Statistics (ADMIN, CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT, OFFICE_DIRECTOR, ACCOUNTANT, DM, or TECH_LEAD).
Query: SELECT DISTINCT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_ADMIN', 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT', 'ROLE_OFFICE_DIRECTOR', 'ROLE_ACCOUNTANT') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as a user with statistics access
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Verify the table loads with columns: Employee, Reported hours, Monthly norm, Personal norm, Budget norm, Deviation %, Manager, Comment
4. Verify data is populated for the current month
5. Verify pagination works if >20 employees
6. Verify sorting by clicking column headers
test_cases.expected_result: Statistics table displays employee report data for current month. All columns present and populated.
test_cases.notes: Access matrix: ADMIN/CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT see all, OFFICE_DIRECTOR/ACCOUNTANT see office, DM/TECH_LEAD see subordinates, EMPLOYEE cannot access.

test_cases.title: Budget norm display — with/without admin vacation (#3381)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with administrative vacation in the current month AND employee without.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) AND sr.budget_norm IS NOT NULL AND sr.budget_norm != sr.month_norm LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin/accountant
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Find an employee with admin vacation: norm column shows '{individual} ({budget})'
4. Find an employee without admin vacation: norm column shows just '{budget}'
5. Verify the format matches #3381 spec
6. Hover over the 'Norm' column header
7. Verify info icon tooltip explains budget norm concept
test_cases.expected_result: Budget norm displayed differently based on admin vacation presence. Format: '{individual} ({budget})' when different, just '{budget}' when equal.
test_cases.notes: Budget norm = individual norm + administrative vacation hours. Deviation % calculated from budget norm.

test_cases.title: Individual norm — partial-month employee (#3353)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee whose first working day is NOT the 1st of a month (or has a last working day mid-month).
Query: SELECT e.login, e.first_working_day FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.first_working_day IS NOT NULL AND EXTRACT(DAY FROM e.first_working_day) > 1 AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Find the partial-month employee
4. Note their 'Personal norm' value
5. Compare with a full-month employee in the same office
6. Verify partial-month employee's personal norm is LESS than full-month (prorated)
7. Via API — GET /api/ttt/v1/employees/{login}/work-periods
8. Verify work period dates match employee's start/end dates
9. On My Tasks page: verify non-working days before employee start date are marked orange
test_cases.expected_result: Partial-month employee has prorated personal norm. Work periods endpoint returns correct date ranges.
test_cases.notes: #3353: new /work-periods endpoint. Non-working days orange on My Tasks, Confirmation, Planner. Re-hired employees have gap periods.

test_cases.title: Vacation impact on personal norm (#3380)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a vacation (paid or unpaid) in the current month.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.vacation v ON e.id = v.employee_id WHERE v.start_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) AND v.start_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month' AND v.status IN ('APPROVED', 'NEW') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Find the employee with a vacation this month
4. Note their 'Personal norm' value
5. Calculate expected norm: office monthly norm − vacation hours
6. Verify personal norm reflects the vacation deduction
7. Compare with My Tasks page norm display for the same employee
8. If discrepancy exists: note bug #3380
test_cases.expected_result: Personal norm reduced by vacation hours. Consistent between Statistics and My Tasks pages.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3380 (OPEN): vacation doesn't reduce personal monthly norm on My Tasks or Employee Reports. Discrepancy between qa-2 and stage.

test_cases.title: Over-the-limit toggle (#3306)
test_cases.preconditions:
Statistics page with employees having various deviation percentages.
Parameters: notification.reporting.over and notification.reporting.under in TTT Parameters.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Note all employees currently displayed
4. Toggle 'Only over the limit' switcher ON
5. Verify the table filters to show only employees exceeding the over/under thresholds
6. Verify the threshold values match TTT Parameters configuration
7. Toggle OFF
8. Verify all employees return to the table
9. Verify the switcher has immediate effect (no page reload needed)
test_cases.expected_result: Toggle filters employees by reporting deviation thresholds. ON: only over-limit shown. OFF: all shown.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3306 (CLOSED): toggle had no effect. Should now work. Default thresholds: over=10%, under=30%.

test_cases.title: DM field and Comment field on statistics (#3309)
test_cases.preconditions:
Statistics page with employee data.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-041)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Verify 'Manager' column exists with DM names
4. Click a manager name — verify it links to CS profile
5. Verify 'Comment' column exists
6. Click the comment field for an employee
7. Type 'Monthly review note'
8. Click outside to save (or press Enter)
9. Verify comment is saved
10. Change month in the date filter
11. Verify the comment field shows the comment for THAT specific month (per-employee per-month)
12. Return to original month — verify original comment still there
test_cases.expected_result: Manager column with CS links. Comment field inline-editable, stored per employee per month.
test_cases.notes: QA bugs from #3309: font size should be 13px, spelling error in column title, comments aggregated incorrectly.

test_cases.title: Future/dismissed employee exclusion (#3320)
test_cases.preconditions: Statistics page. Ideally an employee with a future start date or recent dismissal exists.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Verify NO employees with future start dates appear in the table
4. Verify employees with future start dates don't show negative deviation %
5. Check the employee search dropdown
6. Verify dismissed employees are NOT in the search dropdown
7. If dismissed employees appear in the table: verify they are NOT searchable in dropdown (#3320)
DB-CHECK: SELECT login, first_working_day FROM ttt_backend.employee WHERE first_working_day > CURRENT_DATE AND enabled = true
test_cases.expected_result: Future employees excluded from statistics. Dismissed employees not searchable. No false under-reports.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3320: future employees shown with false negative deviation. Fix: switched to vacation statistic endpoint.

test_cases.title: Deviation percentage calculation and display
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with known reported hours and budget norm.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.reported_effort, sr.budget_norm, CASE WHEN sr.budget_norm > 0 THEN ROUND((sr.reported_effort * 60 - sr.budget_norm)::numeric / sr.budget_norm * 100, 1) ELSE NULL END AS deviation FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) AND sr.budget_norm > 0 ORDER BY ABS(sr.reported_effort * 60 - sr.budget_norm) DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports
3. Find the employee from the query
4. Verify the Deviation % column shows the calculated value
5. Check formatting: integer except (-1,+1) range → 1 decimal place
6. Check color coding: positive (red/over), negative (purple/under), zero (neutral)
7. Check N/A display: employees with 0 budget norm → '+N/A%'
8. Verify N/A sorts to top of the table
test_cases.expected_result: Deviation % formula: (reported - budgetNorm) / budgetNorm × 100%. Display formatting matches spec.
test_cases.notes: ExcessStatus enum: HIGH (>0%), LOW (<0%), NEUTRAL (==0%), NA (budgetNorm=0). N/A sorts to top.

test_cases.title: Forgotten reports notification trigger
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with less than 90% of monthly norm reported.
SETUP: Via test API — trigger forgotten notifications.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) AND sr.budget_norm > 0 AND sr.reported_effort * 60 < sr.budget_norm * 0.9 LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via test endpoint — POST /api/ttt/test/v1/notifications/reports-forgotten to trigger
1. Verify the endpoint returns 200
2. Check email inbox of under-reporting employees
3. Verify notification email received with correct details
4. Verify email contains: employee name, reported hours, norm, deficit
DB-CHECK: SELECT * FROM ttt_backend.notification WHERE type = 'REPORTS_FORGOTTEN' ORDER BY created_time DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.expected_result: Forgotten report notifications sent to employees below 90% norm threshold.
test_cases.notes: Normal schedule: Mon/Fri 16:00. Test endpoint triggers immediately. Threshold: 90% of personal norm.

test_cases.title: Reports changed notification trigger
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager who has reported on behalf of an employee.
SETUP: Via API — create report as API token owner for another employee.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports with executor=<employee_login> (as API token owner)
SETUP: Via test endpoint — POST /api/ttt/test/v1/notifications/reports-changed to trigger
1. Verify the endpoint returns 200
2. Verify employee receives notification that someone reported on their behalf
3. Check email content: reporter name, task, hours, date
test_cases.expected_result: Employee notified when manager/other user reports on their behalf.
test_cases.notes: Normal schedule: Daily 07:50. Cross-employee reporting is a known permission gap (BUG-REPORT-4).

test_cases.title: Reject notification — 5-minute scheduler
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a recently rejected report.
SETUP: Via API — create report and reject it.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create report, PATCH state=REJECTED with comment
SETUP: Via test endpoint — POST /api/ttt/test/v1/notifications/reject to trigger
1. Verify the endpoint returns 200
2. Check employee email for rejection notification
3. Verify email contains: task name, rejection comment, link to report
4. Verify reject record marked as notified
DB-CHECK: SELECT executor_notified FROM ttt_backend.reject WHERE id = <reject_id> — verify true
test_cases.expected_result: Reject notification email sent to employee. Reject record marked executor_notified=true.
test_cases.notes: Runs every 5 minutes normally. executor_notified=false until sent. Bug #3321: no notification when report month closed.

test_cases.title: Accounting report notifications — budget exceeded
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Employee with over-reported hours exceeding budget norm.
SETUP: Reports exceeding budget norm must exist.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant
2. Navigate to Statistics > Employee reports or accounting notifications area
3. Verify over-reporting employees are flagged
4. Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports/accounting/notifications to send accounting-level notifications
5. Verify notification sent to relevant accountants/managers
6. Verify notification content: employee name, excess hours, projects
test_cases.expected_result: Accounting notifications triggered for budget exceedance. Relevant stakeholders notified.
test_cases.notes: Only AUTH_USER required for this endpoint (no specific permission check).

test_cases.title: Regular employee — My Tasks access only
test_cases.preconditions:
Regular employee with ROLE_EMPLOYEE only.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_EMPLOYEE' AND e.enabled = true AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee_role er2 JOIN ttt_backend.role r2 ON er2.role_id = r2.id WHERE er2.employee_id = e.id AND r2.name != 'ROLE_EMPLOYEE') ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the regular employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks (/report) — verify access
3. Attempt to navigate to Confirmation (/approve)
4. Verify access denied or redirect
5. Attempt to navigate to Statistics (/statistics)
6. Verify access denied or redirect
7. Attempt to navigate to Accounting routes
8. Verify access denied or redirect
test_cases.expected_result: Regular employee can access My Tasks only. No access to Confirmation, Statistics, or Accounting.
test_cases.notes: Permission: REPORTS_VIEW allows GET /reports. REPORTS_EDIT allows POST/PATCH/DELETE. REPORTS_APPROVE for confirmation.

test_cases.title: Manager — Confirmation page access
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with PROJECT_MANAGER or SENIOR_MANAGER role.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON e.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER', 'ROLE_SENIOR_MANAGER') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to Confirmation (/approve)
3. Verify page loads with By Employee and By Projects tabs
4. Verify the manager can see employees from their projects
5. Verify the manager can approve/reject hours
6. Navigate to Statistics — verify access
7. Verify Statistics scope: only their subordinates/projects visible
test_cases.expected_result: Manager has full Confirmation access. Statistics shows only their scope (subordinates/projects).
test_cases.notes: REPORTS_APPROVE permission grants confirmation page access.

test_cases.title: Self-approval via API — no check (BUG-REPORT-2)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who is also a manager on a project.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-054)
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a report as the manager (on their own task)
1. Via API — PATCH /api/ttt/v1/reports/{id} with state=APPROVED (using same user's token)
2. Verify the request succeeds (HTTP 200)
3. Verify the report is now APPROVED with approverLogin = executorLogin
4. Note: there is NO executor≠approver validation — self-approval is possible
DB-CHECK: SELECT executor_login, approver_login FROM ttt_backend.task_report WHERE id = <id> — verify both are the same
test_cases.expected_result: Self-approval succeeds via API. No validation preventing approver = executor. This is a known bug.
test_cases.notes: BUG-REPORT-2 (HIGH): No executor≠approver check in API. UI may prevent this by not showing own reports in Confirmation.

test_cases.title: Direct create-as-APPROVED bypass (BUG-REPORT-3)
test_cases.preconditions:
User with REPORTS_EDIT and REPORTS_APPROVE permissions.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-054)
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports with state=APPROVED in the request body
2. Verify the request succeeds
3. Verify the report is created directly in APPROVED state
4. Note: this bypasses the REPORTED → APPROVED workflow entirely
DB-CHECK: SELECT state FROM ttt_backend.task_report WHERE id = <id> — verify 'APPROVED'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: Report created directly as APPROVED, bypassing normal workflow. No validation prevents this.
test_cases.notes: BUG-REPORT-3 (HIGH): POST accepts state=APPROVED. Should only allow REPORTED on create.

test_cases.title: No upper bound on effort (BUG-REPORT-1)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a task.
Query: (same as TC-RPT-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports with effort=90000 (1500 hours = 25 hours/day × 60 days)
2. Verify the request succeeds (no max validation)
3. Check: does the UI display this correctly on My Tasks?
4. Via API — try effort=999999
5. Verify response (should accept due to no upper bound)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: Unreasonably high effort values accepted by API. No upper bound validation exists.
test_cases.notes: BUG-REPORT-1 (HIGH): Only minimum (≥1 minute) validated. 25h (1500 min) accepted. No daily/weekly cap.

test_cases.title: Unauthenticated access to /effort and /employee-projects (BUG-REPORT-6)
test_cases.preconditions: No authentication required for this test.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via curl (no auth header) — GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/effort
2. Verify the response — if 200 with data, confirms missing @PreAuthorize
3. Via curl (no auth header) — GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/employee-projects
4. Verify the response — if 200, confirms missing @PreAuthorize
5. Compare with authenticated access — verify same data returned
test_cases.expected_result: If bug exists: both endpoints return data without authentication. Should require at minimum AUTH_USER.
test_cases.notes: BUG-REPORT-6 (LOW): Missing @PreAuthorize annotation on /effort and /employee-projects endpoints.

test_cases.title: Cross-employee reporting via API (BUG-REPORT-4)
test_cases.preconditions:
API token owner.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.login != 'pvaynmaster' ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/ttt/v1/reports with executorLogin = <another_employee_login>
2. Verify the report is created under the OTHER employee's name
3. Check: does this require any specific permission beyond AUTH_USER?
4. Verify the 'Reports changed' notification is generated for the target employee
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the report
test_cases.expected_result: API token owner can report for any employee. No executor validation beyond auth check.
test_cases.notes: BUG-REPORT-4: any authenticated user can create reports for any other employee via API.

test_cases.title: Approved status removed after tracker import (#3296)
test_cases.preconditions:
PM with an employee having APPROVED hours. External tracker configured.
