test_cases.title: Delete sick leave (OPEN, accounting=NEW)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an OPEN/NEW sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Find the OPEN sick leave row
4. Click the delete (X) action button
5. Confirm deletion in the dialog
6. Verify sick leave disappears from the active list (or shows State='Deleted')
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect 'DELETED'
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave soft-deleted. Status=DELETED in DB. Row disappears from active list.
test_cases.notes: Soft delete: sets status=DELETED. File/notify_also associations NOT cleaned up (BUG-SL-5).

test_cases.title: View sick leave details
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with at least one sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave with number and files.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number='SN-VIEW-01'
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click the view/details icon on the sick leave row
4. Verify details dialog opens showing:
   - Employee name
   - Start date and End date
   - Calendar days count
   - Sick note number
   - State
5. Verify close button (X) dismisses the dialog
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Details dialog shows all sick leave attributes. All fields match table data.
test_cases.notes: Modal type: SICK_LIST_DETAILS. Employee view does NOT show accounting status column.

test_cases.title: Edit file attachments — add and remove
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an OPEN sick leave that has 1 file attached.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with 1 file.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — upload a file, create sick leave with filesIds=[uuid]
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click edit on the sick leave row
4. Verify current file is shown in the attachment list
5. Upload a second file
6. Remove the original file
7. Click 'Save'
8. Open details — verify only the new file is attached
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave_file WHERE sick_leave_id = <id> — expect 1
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Original file removed, new file added. Diff-and-sync on update replaces old associations.
test_cases.notes: Edit uses two-step PATCH: first dates/number, then file diff + second PATCH for filesIds.

test_cases.title: Accounting status NEW → PROCESSING (accountant)
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant with access to the sick leave's office. OPEN/NEW sick leave exists.
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave.
Query: SELECT e.login AS employee, a.login AS accountant FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office_accountant oa ON e.office_id = oa.office_id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee a ON oa.employee_id = a.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND a.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave for the employee
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to Sick Leave Accounting page (/accounting/sick-leaves)
3. Find the sick leave row
4. Verify Status column shows inline dropdown with current value 'New'
5. Click the Status dropdown
6. Select 'Pending' (PROCESSING)
7. Verify Status updates to 'Pending'
8. Verify State remains 'Started' or 'Planned' (OPEN)
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, accounting_status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect status=OPEN, accounting_status=PROCESSING
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Accounting status changes to PROCESSING. Main status stays OPEN. Accountant ID auto-set.
test_cases.notes: Inline dropdown — no modal. Status coupling: PROCESSING → OPEN.

test_cases.title: Accounting status PROCESSING → PAID → auto-close
test_cases.preconditions:
OPEN/PROCESSING sick leave with a document number. Accountant has access.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number, set accounting_status=PROCESSING.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number='SN-PAY-01', then PATCH accounting_status=PROCESSING
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find the PROCESSING sick leave
4. Click Status dropdown
5. Select 'Paid'
6. Verify Status changes to 'Paid'
7. Verify State changes to 'Ended' (CLOSED) — auto-close on PAID
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, accounting_status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect status=CLOSED, accounting_status=PAID
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Setting PAID auto-closes the sick leave. Both status and accounting_status update. Main status derived from accounting status.
test_cases.notes: Critical coupling: PAID → CLOSED, REJECTED → REJECTED, PROCESSING → OPEN.

test_cases.title: Accounting status → REJECTED → status=REJECTED
test_cases.preconditions:
OPEN/NEW sick leave. Accountant has access.
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find the NEW sick leave
4. Click Status dropdown
5. Select 'Rejected'
6. Verify Status changes to 'Rejected'
7. Verify State also changes to 'Rejected'
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, accounting_status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect status=REJECTED, accounting_status=REJECTED
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Accounting REJECTED sets main status to REJECTED as well. Both dimensions show 'Rejected'.
test_cases.notes: Dual 'Rejected' state: both State and Status show 'Rejected'. Known UX issue: 'Rejected Rejected' in filter (BUG-SL-4).

test_cases.title: Reverse: PAID → NEW (reopen closed sick leave)
test_cases.preconditions:
CLOSED/PAID sick leave. Admin or chief accountant user.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, set PAID via PATCH.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number, PATCH accounting_status=PAID (auto-closes)
1. Login as admin/chief accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find the PAID/Ended sick leave
4. Click Status dropdown (should be available for admin/chief accountant)
5. Select 'New'
6. Verify Status reverts to 'New'
7. Verify State reverts to 'Started' or 'Planned' (OPEN) — reopened!
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, accounting_status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect status=OPEN, accounting_status=NEW
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Setting accountingStatus=NEW on a PAID sick leave REOPENS it. Status reverts to OPEN. No state machine guardrail prevents this.
test_cases.notes: Unrestricted transitions — any-to-any (BUG-SL-3). Paid→New reversal is a design risk.

test_cases.title: Accountant comment — inline tooltip
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant with an OPEN sick leave to comment on.
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find the sick leave row
4. Click the comment (speech bubble) icon
5. Verify tooltip overlay with textarea appears
6. Type 'Pending certificate from clinic'
7. Save the comment (click outside or save button)
8. Verify comment icon indicates a comment exists
DB-CHECK: SELECT accountant_comment FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect 'Pending certificate from clinic'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Comment saved via inline tooltip (not a modal). Stored in accountant_comment field.
test_cases.notes: Inline Tooltip overlay with textarea, not a modal. ADD_COMMENT modal is orphaned in code.

test_cases.title: Computed states: Planned vs Started vs Overdue
test_cases.preconditions:
Three sick leaves: future start (Planned), current (Started), past end (Overdue).
SETUP: Via API — create 3 sick leaves with different date ranges.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create 3 sick leaves for the same employee:
  a) Future start (next month) — should show as 'Planned'
  b) Current period (started yesterday, ends next week) — should show as 'Started'
  c) Past end (ended last week, not closed) — should show as 'Overdue'
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Verify sick leave (a) shows State = 'Planned'
4. Verify sick leave (b) shows State = 'Started'
5. Verify sick leave (c) shows State = 'Overdue'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all 3 sick leaves
test_cases.expected_result: Three OPEN sick leaves display different computed states based on date comparison. Planned/Started/Overdue are query-time computations, not stored.
test_cases.notes: DB stores only OPEN. API returns SCHEDULED(=Planned)/OVERDUE computed from dates. PATCH only accepts OPEN/CLOSED.

test_cases.title: Edit DELETED sick leave — only comment allowed
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant, with a DELETED sick leave in the system.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, then DELETE it.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, then DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Filter by State = 'Deleted'
4. Find the deleted sick leave row
5. Verify edit (pencil) icon is NOT visible (edit disabled)
6. Verify Status dropdown shows plain text (not selectable)
7. Verify comment (speech bubble) icon IS still visible
8. Click the comment icon
9. Add a comment 'Duplicate entry removed'
10. Save the comment
DB-CHECK: SELECT accountant_comment FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Only accountantComment can be modified on DELETED sick leaves. Edit and status dropdown disabled. Error: 'exception.validation.sickLeave.update.closed' if other fields modified.
test_cases.notes: hasUnmodifiableForDeletedRequestField() — all fields except accountantComment are unmodifiable on DELETED.

test_cases.title: Delete PAID sick leave — blocked
test_cases.preconditions:
CLOSED/PAID sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number, PATCH to PAID.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number, PATCH accounting_status=PAID
1. Login as the employee (or any user)
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my or /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find the PAID sick leave
4. Attempt to delete (click delete button if visible)
5. Verify deletion is blocked — either button is hidden or an error is returned
6. Via API — attempt DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
7. Verify HTTP 400: 'exception.validation.sickLeave.delete.closed'
CLEANUP: Via API — PATCH accounting_status=NEW (to reopen), then DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: PAID sick leave cannot be deleted. UI hides delete action. API returns 400.
test_cases.notes: Delete guard checks accountingStatus == PAID only. CLOSED+non-PAID CAN be deleted.

test_cases.title: Accounting page — table columns and filters
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant user.
Query: SELECT a.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee a JOIN ttt_backend.employee be ON a.login = be.login JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_ACCOUNTANT' AND a.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Verify 10 columns visible: Employee, Sick leave dates, Days, Work days, Sick note, Accountant, Salary office, State, Status, Actions
4. Click 'Salary office' filter — verify dropdown shows list of offices (27+)
5. Click 'State' filter — verify options: Planned, Started, Overdue, Ended, Rejected, Deleted
6. Click 'Status' filter — verify options: New, Pending, Paid, Rejected
7. Apply 'State: Started' filter
8. Verify table shows only OPEN sick leaves in progress
9. Clear filter
10. Verify table returns to unfiltered view
test_cases.expected_result: Accounting page shows 10 columns with working filters for Salary office (27), State (6+), Status (4).
test_cases.notes: State filter may show 'Rejected Rejected' label duplication (BUG-SL-4). Status is inline dropdown.

test_cases.title: Accounting — edit sick leave dates
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant with a non-deleted, non-PAID sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Click edit (pencil) icon on the sick leave row
4. Verify edit dialog shows: Employee (read-only), Start date, End date, Calendar days (auto-calc), Sick note number
5. Change End date to 3 days later
6. Verify Calendar days recalculates
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify table updates with new dates
DB-CHECK: SELECT end_date, total_days, work_days FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id>
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Accountant can edit dates. Calendar days and working days auto-recalculate. Employee field is read-only in edit dialog.
test_cases.notes: Edit dialog has NO accounting status field — managed only via inline dropdown.

test_cases.title: Accounting — edit PAID sick leave as admin
test_cases.preconditions:
PAID sick leave. User with ROLE_ADMIN or ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number, PATCH to PAID.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, PATCH accounting_status=PAID
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find the PAID sick leave
4. Verify edit icon IS visible for admin
5. Click edit
6. Change End date
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify dates updated
DB-CHECK: SELECT end_date FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id>
CLEANUP: Via API — PATCH accounting_status=NEW, then DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Admin/chief accountant CAN edit PAID sick leaves. Regular accountant (office-level) can only edit PAID for their own office.
test_cases.notes: PAID guard: only ADMIN, CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT, or assigned office accountant can modify.

test_cases.title: Accounting — office accountant scope restriction
test_cases.preconditions:
Office accountant and a sick leave from a DIFFERENT office.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for an employee from a different office.
Query: SELECT e.login AS employee, a.login AS accountant, e.office_id, a2.office_id AS acc_office FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office_accountant oa ON oa.office_id != e.office_id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee a ON oa.employee_id = a.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND a.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for an employee in office A; login as accountant for office B
1. Login as the accountant (office B)
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find the sick leave from office A
4. Verify the sick leave IS visible (accounting view shows all)
5. Attempt to change the accounting status via dropdown
6. Verify action is blocked — error 'exception.vacation.no.permission'
7. Via API — attempt PATCH accounting_status=PROCESSING as office B accountant
8. Verify HTTP 403
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Office accountant can view but NOT modify accounting status for employees from other offices.
test_cases.notes: Uses officeAccountantRepository.existsByEmployeeIdAndOfficeId(). Shared VacationSecurityException class.

test_cases.title: Accounting — overdue sick leave count badge
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. At least one OVERDUE sick leave (OPEN + past end_date).
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with past end_date (started and ended in the past).
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with start_date = 2 weeks ago, end_date = 1 week ago
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Check the page for an overdue count indicator or badge
4. Filter by State = 'Overdue'
5. Verify the created sick leave appears
6. Via API — GET /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/count
7. Verify response includes open and overdue counts
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Overdue sick leaves counted and surfaced. GET /count endpoint returns open_count and overdue_count.
test_cases.notes: OverdueSickLeaveCommand — per-request check, not cron. API: GET /v1/sick-leaves/count.

test_cases.title: Accounting status: any-to-any transition verification
test_cases.preconditions:
Admin or chief accountant. Multiple sick leaves in different accounting statuses.
SETUP: Via API — create 4 sick leaves, set accounting_status to NEW, PROCESSING, PAID, REJECTED respectively.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create 4 sick leaves with different accounting statuses
1. Login as admin
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Test transition: NEW → PAID (skip PROCESSING)
4. Test transition: PAID → REJECTED
5. Test transition: REJECTED → PROCESSING
6. Test transition: PROCESSING → NEW (backwards)
7. For each transition, verify:
   - Status dropdown allows the selection
   - Main State updates according to coupling rules
   - Accountant field updates on first change
CLEANUP: Via API — PATCH all to NEW, then DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: All transitions allowed — no state machine enforcement. This confirms BUG-SL-3: unrestricted accounting status transitions.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: Paid→New reopens a closed sick leave. No guardrails.

test_cases.title: DM/TL — My Department tab
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER or ROLE_TECH_LEAD. Subordinate employees with sick leaves.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for a subordinate.
Query: SELECT m.login AS manager, e.login AS employee FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id JOIN ttt_backend.employee be ON m.login = be.login JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER','ROLE_TECH_LEAD') AND e.enabled = true AND m.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave for the subordinate employee
1. Login as the manager (DM/TL)
2. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees
3. Verify 'My department' tab is visible and active
4. Verify columns: Employee, Sick leave dates, Calendar days, State, Status, Actions
5. Find the subordinate's sick leave in the table
6. Verify State and Status columns are displayed correctly
7. Verify Status is shown as plain text (not dropdown — manager cannot change accounting status)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: My Department tab shows subordinate employees' sick leaves. Status shown as plain text, not editable dropdown.
test_cases.notes: Three tabs: My department (DM/TL), My projects (PM), personal. #2622: tab badge was per-page, now total.

test_cases.title: DM/TL — create sick leave for subordinate
test_cases.preconditions:
DM or TL with a subordinate employee.
Query: (same as TC-SL-027)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the DM/TL
2. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees
3. Click 'My department' tab
4. Click 'Add a sick note' or create button
5. In the creation dialog, search and select the subordinate employee
6. Set Start date and End date (5 days)
7. Enter sick note number 'SN-MGR-01'
8. Click 'Save'
9. Verify sick leave appears in the table for the selected employee
DB-CHECK: SELECT employee, status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <created_id>
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: DM/TL can create sick leave for subordinates. Employee search works. force=true hardcoded in manager view (bypasses overlap check).
test_cases.notes: Manager view: no file upload capability. force=true hardcoded. Client-side overlap check capped at 100 records.

test_cases.title: PM — My Projects tab (view only, no create/edit)
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER. Employee on PM's project has a sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for an employee on PM's project.
Query: SELECT pm.login AS pm, e.login AS employee FROM ttt_backend.project_member pme JOIN ttt_backend.employee be_pm ON pme.employee_id = be_pm.id AND pme.role ILIKE '%manager%' JOIN ttt_vacation.employee pm ON pm.login = be_pm.login JOIN ttt_backend.project_member pme2 ON pme2.project_id = pme.project_id AND pme2.employee_id != pme.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.employee be_e ON pme2.employee_id = be_e.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.login = be_e.login WHERE pm.enabled = true AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave for the employee on PM's project
1. Login as the PM
2. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees
3. Click 'My projects' tab
4. Verify the employee's sick leave is visible
5. Verify NO create button is available for PM
6. Verify NO edit/delete action buttons on the row
7. Verify PM can only view — no modification actions
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: PM can VIEW sick leaves for project employees but CANNOT create, edit, or delete. checkIfCurrentEmployeeIsNotPM() enforces this.
test_cases.notes: PM from another project cannot modify. Exception: PM who is also the employee's manager CAN edit.

test_cases.title: DM/TL — edit subordinate's sick leave
test_cases.preconditions:
DM/TL with a subordinate who has an OPEN sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for the subordinate.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for the subordinate
1. Login as the DM/TL
2. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees
3. Click 'My department' tab
4. Click edit (pencil) icon on the subordinate's sick leave
5. Change End date by 2 days
6. Click 'Save'
7. Verify dates updated in the table
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: DM/TL can edit subordinate's sick leave dates. Calendar days recalculate.
test_cases.notes: DM/TL can create and edit for their subordinates. PM can only view (#2873).

test_cases.title: Manager view — State and Status filters
test_cases.preconditions:
DM/TL with multiple sick leaves in different states.
SETUP: Via API — create 3 sick leaves in OPEN, CLOSED, DELETED statuses.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leaves in different statuses
1. Login as the DM/TL
2. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees
3. Click 'State' filter dropdown
4. Verify 'Deleted' option is NOT visible for non-accountant roles
5. Select 'Started' filter
6. Verify only active sick leaves shown
7. Click 'Status' filter dropdown
8. Select 'New'
9. Verify combined filter works
10. Clear all filters
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all created sick leaves
test_cases.expected_result: State filter hides 'Deleted' for non-accountant roles (#2622 fix). Filters work in combination.
test_cases.notes: #2622 bug: 'Deleted' was visible for non-accountants. Filters should reset when switching tabs.

test_cases.title: Tab badge counters — total count, not per-page
test_cases.preconditions:
DM/TL with more sick leaves than fit on one page.
SETUP: Create enough sick leaves to span 2+ pages (>10).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the DM/TL
2. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees
3. Check the tab badge counter on 'My department' tab
4. Verify the counter shows the TOTAL count (e.g., 15), not per-page (e.g., 10)
5. Navigate to page 2 of the table
6. Verify badge counter remains the same
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE created sick leaves if any
test_cases.expected_result: Badge counter shows total sick leaves for the tab, not the current page count. #2622 fix.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2622: counter was per-page (not total) → added API response field.

test_cases.title: Employee sees only own sick leaves
test_cases.preconditions:
Regular employee (no management roles).
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for this employee AND for a different employee.
Query: SELECT e1.login AS employee, e2.login AS other FROM ttt_vacation.employee e1, ttt_vacation.employee e2 WHERE e1.enabled = true AND e2.enabled = true AND e1.id != e2.id AND e1.manager_id IS NOT NULL AND e2.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for employee, create sick leave for other
1. Login as the regular employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Verify only own sick leave is visible in the table
4. Verify other employee's sick leave is NOT visible
5. Via API — GET /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves?employeeLogin=<other> as employee
6. Verify response is empty or returns own sick leaves only
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE both sick leaves
test_cases.expected_result: Employee sees only own sick leaves. Cannot access others' data. Regression test for #3213.
test_cases.notes: PRODUCTION BUG #3213: all employees could see everyone's sick leaves. CRITICAL regression test.

test_cases.title: Accounting route inaccessible to regular employee
test_cases.preconditions:
Regular employee without ROLE_ACCOUNTANT, ROLE_ADMIN, ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT, ROLE_VIEW_ALL, or ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e LEFT JOIN ttt_backend.employee be ON e.login = be.login LEFT JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id LEFT JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND (r.name IS NULL OR r.name NOT IN ('ROLE_ACCOUNTANT','ROLE_ADMIN','ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT','ROLE_VIEW_ALL','ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER')) GROUP BY e.login ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the regular employee
2. Navigate directly to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Verify page is not accessible — redirect or access denied
4. Verify the navigation menu does NOT show 'Sick Leave Records' or 'Accounting > Sick Leaves' link
test_cases.expected_result: Regular employee cannot access /accounting/sick-leaves. Route protected by SICK_LEAVE_ACCOUNTING_VIEW permission.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3012: route was accessible to regular employees (checked VACATIONS:VIEW instead of SICK_LEAVE_ACCOUNTING_VIEW).

test_cases.title: Any authenticated user can create sick leave for any employee (BUG)
test_cases.preconditions:
Two employees: creator and target.
Query: SELECT e1.login AS creator, e2.login AS target FROM ttt_vacation.employee e1, ttt_vacation.employee e2 WHERE e1.enabled = true AND e2.enabled = true AND e1.id != e2.id ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.expected_result: Any authenticated user can create a sick leave for any employee by supplying their login. This is a known permission bug (BUG-SL-1).
test_cases.notes: No @PreAuthorize beyond AUTHENTICATED_USER on create endpoint. Unlike vacation/day-off which check ownership.

test_cases.title: Any user can delete non-PAID sick leave (BUG)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee A's sick leave, Employee B as deleter.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for employee A.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for employee A
1. Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} as employee B
2. Verify HTTP 200 — deletion succeeds
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect 'DELETED'
test_cases.expected_result: Any authenticated user can delete any non-PAID sick leave. No owner check on delete endpoint (BUG-SL-1 related).
test_cases.notes: deleteById() only guards PAID accounting status. No owner check, no PM check. Differs from vacation/day-off.

test_cases.title: PM from another project cannot edit sick leave
test_cases.preconditions:
PM who is NOT the employee's manager or TL. Employee has an OPEN sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for the employee.
Query: Find a PM and employee where PM has no management relationship to employee.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for the employee
1. Via API — PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} as the PM with updated endDate
2. Verify HTTP 403: 'exception.vacation.no.permission'
DB-CHECK: SELECT end_date FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — verify unchanged
test_cases.expected_result: PM who is not the employee's manager/TL/accountant gets VacationSecurityException (403).
test_cases.notes: Uses VacationSecurityException (not SickLeave-specific). Error: exception.vacation.no.permission.

test_cases.title: Employee cannot see accounting status on personal page
test_cases.preconditions:
Regular employee with a sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Verify table columns: Sick leave dates, Calendar days, Number, Accountant, State, Actions
4. Verify there is NO 'Status' (accounting status) column
5. Verify the employee cannot see or change the accounting status
test_cases.expected_result: Employee personal page does NOT show accounting status column. Employees see State only.
test_cases.notes: Columns: dates, days, number, accountant, state, actions. No accounting status visible.

test_cases.title: Date validation — startDate after endDate rejected
test_cases.preconditions:
Enabled employee.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click 'Add a sick note'
4. Set Start date = next Friday, End date = next Monday (start > end)
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify validation error: 'validation.sickLeave.dates.order'
7. Verify error highlighted on both Start date and End date fields
test_cases.expected_result: Creation blocked with date order validation error. Error applied to both date fields.
test_cases.notes: Error: validation.sickLeave.dates.order. Both startDate and endDate get constraint violation.

test_cases.title: Overlapping sick leave — rejected
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing OPEN sick leave (Mon-Fri next week).
SETUP: Via API — create first sick leave.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for Mon-Fri next week
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click 'Add a sick note'
4. Set Start date = Wednesday next week, End date = Wednesday + 3 days (overlaps existing)
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify error: 'exception.validation.sickLeave.dates.crossing'
7. Verify second sick leave NOT created
test_cases.expected_result: Overlapping sick leaves blocked. Error: exception.validation.sickLeave.dates.crossing. Applied to startDate field.
test_cases.notes: Overlap check excludes DELETED and REJECTED sick leaves. Only checks against OPEN/CLOSED.

test_cases.title: Overlap check skips DELETED and REJECTED sick leaves
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a DELETED sick leave (Mon-Fri) and a REJECTED sick leave (Mon-Fri of week after).
SETUP: Via API — create 2 sick leaves, delete one, reject the other via accountant.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave A (Mon-Fri), DELETE it. Create sick leave B (Mon-Fri week after), PATCH accounting_status=REJECTED.
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click 'Add a sick note'
4. Set dates overlapping the DELETED sick leave A dates
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify sick leave created successfully (no crossing error)
7. Delete the new sick leave
8. Create another sick leave overlapping REJECTED sick leave B dates
9. Verify also created successfully
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all created sick leaves
test_cases.expected_result: Overlap check correctly excludes DELETED and REJECTED sick leaves. New sick leaves can be created for those date ranges.
test_cases.notes: findCrossingSickLeaves() must skip DELETED+REJECTED. Tickets #2636, #2973 confirm this.

test_cases.title: Vacation crossing — force=false triggers 409
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing active vacation. Creating sick leave that overlaps.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation (APPROVED).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 5 AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create and approve a vacation for next week
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves with dates overlapping the vacation, force=false
2. Verify HTTP 409: 'exception.sick.leave.crossing.vacation'
3. Via API — POST same request with force=true
4. Verify HTTP 200 — sick leave created despite vacation crossing
DB-CHECK: Verify both sick leave and vacation exist for overlapping dates
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE sick leave, cancel and delete vacation
test_cases.expected_result: force=false → 409 Conflict. force=true → created successfully. EmployeeSickLeaveOverlapsVacationEvent published.
test_cases.notes: UI shows vacation crossing popup and lets user choose to force. force field is @NotNull on create.

test_cases.title: Vacation crossing — UI popup and force confirmation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing active vacation for next week.
SETUP: Via API — create and approve a vacation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create and approve a vacation for next week
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click 'Add a sick note'
4. Set dates overlapping the approved vacation
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify a crossing warning popup appears (VACATION_CROSSING modal)
7. Confirm/force creation in the popup
8. Verify sick leave created despite the overlap
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE sick leave, cancel and delete vacation
test_cases.expected_result: UI detects vacation crossing client-side and shows warning popup. User can confirm to force creation.
test_cases.notes: Frontend does client-side crossing check via pre-fetch. Modal type: VACATION_CROSSING.

test_cases.title: File attachment limit — max 5 files
test_cases.preconditions:
Enabled employee.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click 'Add a sick note'
4. Set valid dates
5. Upload 5 files (each < 5MB)
6. Verify all 5 files shown in attachment list
7. Attempt to upload a 6th file
8. Verify upload blocked or error: max 5 files
9. Click 'Save' with 5 files
10. Verify sick leave created with 5 attachments
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Maximum 5 files per sick leave. 6th file rejected. @Size(max=5) on filesIds.
test_cases.notes: Validated on DTO but not enforced in service. Test both UI and API enforcement.

test_cases.title: Document number max length 40 characters
test_cases.preconditions:
Enabled employee.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click 'Add a sick note'
4. Set valid dates
5. Enter a 40-character number: 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ12345678901234'
6. Click 'Save'
7. Verify sick leave created with the full 40-char number
8. Edit the sick leave
9. Try entering a 41-character number: 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ123456789012345'
10. Click 'Save'
11. Verify validation error or truncation
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: 40-char number accepted. 41+ chars rejected by @Size(max=40) validation.
test_cases.notes: @Size(max=40) on number field.

test_cases.title: Regression: visibility scope — page refresh (#2524)
test_cases.preconditions:
Regular employee with a sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create a sick leave for the employee.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for the employee
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Verify only own sick leave visible
4. Refresh the page (F5 / Ctrl+R)
5. Verify the page reloads and still shows ONLY own sick leaves
6. Open browser developer tools → Network tab
7. Verify the GET /v1/sick-leaves request includes employeeLogin parameter
8. Verify no other employee's data appears
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Page refresh maintains visibility scope. GET request always includes employeeLogin param. Regression test for HotFix #2524.
test_cases.notes: Production 'flickering bug': on refresh, frontend sent request WITHOUT employeeLogin → API returned ALL.

test_cases.title: Regression: rejection order dependency (#2973) — close then reject
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee and accountant. OPEN sick leave with number.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number='SN-973-A'.
Query: (same employee/accountant pair as TC-SL-013)
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave with number='SN-973-A'
1. Login as the employee
2. Close the sick leave (State → Ended)
3. Login as the accountant
4. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
5. Set accounting status to 'Rejected'
6. Verify State changes to 'Rejected' and accounting status = 'Rejected'
7. Navigate to My Tasks page for the employee
8. Verify norm values are correctly reverted (sick leave days NOT counted in norm)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Close → Reject sequence: norm correctly reverts. This is the CORRECT sequence per #2973.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2973 critical edge case: close→reject = OK, reject→close = BUG (norm incorrect).

test_cases.title: Regression: rejection order dependency (#2973) — reject then close attempt
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee and accountant. OPEN sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave
1. Login as the accountant
2. Set accounting status to 'Rejected'
3. Verify State = 'Rejected'
4. Login as the employee
5. Attempt to close the sick leave
6. Verify close action is NOT available for 'Rejected' state
7. If close is available (bug), verify norm calculation after close
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, accounting_status FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Close action should NOT be available for Rejected state. Per #2973 requirements: close only for Open and Overdue.
test_cases.notes: #2973 requirement: Close only for Open and Overdue. Closing Rejected was a bug. Closing Planned IS allowed.

test_cases.title: Regression: employee cannot edit Rejected sick leave (#2973)
test_cases.preconditions:
REJECTED sick leave for an employee.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, PATCH accounting_status=REJECTED.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, PATCH accounting_status=REJECTED
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Find the REJECTED sick leave
4. Verify edit icon is NOT visible or is disabled
5. Verify delete icon IS visible (REJECTED can be deleted since not PAID)
6. Verify employee cannot modify dates or number
DB-CHECK: Via API — PATCH /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id} as employee with new endDate
7. Verify HTTP 400 or 403
test_cases.expected_result: Employee CANNOT edit Rejected sick leave (view only). Per #2973 requirement 1. Accountant CAN still edit.
test_cases.notes: #2973: employee cannot edit in Rejected state. #2567: accountant has no editing restrictions.

test_cases.title: Regression: deleted sick leave norm propagation (#2778, #2783)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a sick leave that affects norm. Sick leave in current reporting period.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave covering several working days in the current period.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave (5 working days in current month)
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks page (time reporting)
3. Verify sick leave days are marked (orange color)
4. Verify norm tooltip shows reduced hours (accounts for sick leave)
5. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
6. Delete the sick leave
7. Navigate back to My Tasks page
8. Verify sick leave day colors revert to normal (black)
9. Verify norm tooltip recalculates (hours increase back to full norm)
10. Verify cell input is available again for those days
test_cases.expected_result: Deleting sick leave immediately reverts: day colors, norm tooltip values, cell input availability. No page refresh needed.
test_cases.notes: Bugs #2778/#2783: deleted SL still showed in tasks, norm not recalculated. Fix required real-time propagation.

test_cases.title: Regression: rejected sick leave colors in My Tasks (#2863)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a sick leave in current period. Accountant to reject it.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for current period.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave (working days in current month)
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks
3. Verify sick leave days are marked orange
4. Login as accountant
5. Reject the sick leave (PATCH accounting_status=REJECTED)
6. Login as the employee again
7. Navigate to My Tasks
8. Verify day colors revert to black (not orange)
9. Verify norm tooltip recalculates correctly
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Rejection reverts day colors from orange to black. Norm recalculated. #2863 fix verified.
test_cases.notes: #2863 HotFix: accountant rejection should revert day colors. Previously days remained orange.

test_cases.title: Regression: deleted sick leave in Planner (#2792)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a sick leave in current period.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for current period
1. Login as a user who can see the Planner
2. Navigate to Planner page
3. Verify employee's sick leave days are marked red
4. Delete the sick leave via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
5. Refresh or navigate back to Planner
6. Verify day markings are removed (no longer red)
CLEANUP: Already deleted
test_cases.expected_result: Deleted sick leave no longer marks days red in Planner. #2792 HotFix verified.
test_cases.notes: #2792: multiple fix attempts needed. Regression noted in comments.

test_cases.title: Regression: sick leave data in Confirmation page (#2819)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a sick leave in the current reporting period.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for current period
1. Login as a manager who can see Confirmation page
2. Navigate to Confirmation page
3. Switch to 'By Employees' view
4. Verify sick leave IS shown for the employee

8. Refresh Confirmation page
9. Verify deleted sick leave NO longer shows
CLEANUP: Already deleted
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave visible in both Confirmation views. Deleted sick leave removed. #2819 sub-bugs 2 and 4 verified.
test_cases.notes: #2819 had 4 sub-bugs: weekend hours, deleted SL displayed, day-off transfer, SL missing from 'By Employees'.

test_cases.title: Regression: rejected sick leave excluded from Availability Chart copy (#2812)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a REJECTED sick leave in current period.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, reject it.
test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to Availability Chart for the employee's period
3. Click 'Copy to clipboard' button
4. Paste the copied data into a text editor
5. Verify REJECTED sick leave is NOT included in the copied data
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Copy-to-clipboard excludes REJECTED sick leaves from the copied data. #2812 fix verified.
test_cases.notes: #2812 HotFix: rejected sick leaves were included in copied availability data.

test_cases.title: Regression: sick leave not displayed in tables (#3257)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a sick leave that exists in DB.

test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Verify sick leave is visible in the table
4. Login as the accountant
5. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
6. Verify the same sick leave is visible in accounting table
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave visible in both My Sick Leaves and Sick Leave Records (accounting). Regression test for #3257.
test_cases.notes: #3257: data existed in DB but not shown in UI. Affected both pages.

test_cases.title: Notification on sick leave creation (by employee)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a manager.
SETUP: Clear test emails for manager's address.
Query: SELECT e.login AS employee, m.login AS manager FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND m.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via test API — clear email queue for manager
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Create a sick leave (5 days)
4. Via test API — trigger notification sending (POST /api/ttt/test/v1/notifications)
5. Via email API — GET /api/email/v1/emails?to=<manager_email>
6. Verify notification received: template NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_OPEN
7. Verify notification includes: employee name, dates, duration
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Manager receives NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_OPEN email. Recipients: manager + notifyAlso + per-office receivers.
test_cases.notes: 5 event types. BUG: getEditorType() uses == (identity) instead of .equals() (BUG-SL-2).

test_cases.title: Notification on sick leave creation (by DM/TL)
test_cases.preconditions:
DM/TL creating sick leave for a subordinate.
SETUP: Clear test emails.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via test API — clear email queue
1. Login as the DM/TL
2. Navigate to /vacation/sick-leaves-of-employees
3. Create sick leave for a subordinate
4. Via test API — trigger notifications
5. Via email API — check emails
6. Verify template is NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_OPEN_BY_SUPERVISOR (not plain _OPEN)
7. Verify employee receives the notification (not the DM/TL)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Supervisor creation uses _BY_SUPERVISOR template. Employee notified. Editor type should be SUPERVISOR.
test_cases.notes: BUG-SL-2: getEditorType() always returns EMPLOYEE fallback due to == identity comparison.

test_cases.title: Notification on sick leave deletion
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a sick leave. Email queue cleared.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, clear emails.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, clear email queue
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Delete the sick leave
4. Via test API — trigger notifications
5. Via email API — check for NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DELETE template
6. Verify manager received the deletion notification
test_cases.expected_result: Deletion triggers NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_DELETE email to manager.
test_cases.notes: SickLeaveDeletedEvent → notifications. ID_105 email should exclude DELETED sick leaves.

test_cases.title: Notification on file attachment
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing sick leave. Email queue cleared.

1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Edit the sick leave
4. Attach a new file
5. Save
6. Via test API — trigger notifications
7. Via email API — check for NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_FILES_ADDED template
8. Verify notification sent to accountants of employee's office
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: File attachment triggers NOTIFY_SICKLEAVE_FILES_ADDED. Sent to office accountants only (not managers/PMs).
test_cases.notes: #2673: file email was sent to wrong recipients (managers/PMs too). Fix: only office accountants.

test_cases.title: Overlap notification excludes Deleted/Rejected sick leaves
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a DELETED sick leave overlapping a vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave, delete it. Create an active vacation for the same period.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave overlapping a vacation, delete the sick leave
1. Via API — check if overlap notification was generated for the DELETED sick leave
2. Verify no NOTIFY_EMPLOYEE_SICKLEAVE_OVERLAPS_VACATION for the deleted sick leave
3. Create a new OPEN sick leave overlapping the vacation (force=true)
4. Verify overlap notification IS generated for the new active sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Overlap notifications skip DELETED/REJECTED sick leaves. Only active overlaps trigger notifications.
test_cases.notes: #2973 requirement 3: overlap notification should NOT fire for Rejected or Deleted.

test_cases.title: Create sick leave with familyMember=true
test_cases.preconditions:
Enabled employee. #3408 feature deployed.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Click 'Add a sick note'
4. Set dates (5 days)
5. Check the 'Caring for a family member' checkbox
6. Click 'Save'
7. Verify sick leave created with familyMember indicator visible

CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave created with familyMember=true. Checkbox label: 'По уходу за членом семьи' (RU) / 'Caring for a family member' (EN).
test_cases.notes: Sprint 16 feature. Default unchecked (false). Depends on #3409 for budgetNorm changes.

test_cases.title: Default familyMember=false on create

6. Set dates, click 'Save'
7. Verify sick leave created
DB-CHECK: SELECT family_member FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect FALSE
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Default is familyMember=false (own illness). Checkbox unchecked by default.
test_cases.notes: All existing historical sick leaves retroactively set to familyMember=false.

test_cases.title: Toggle familyMember on existing sick leave
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an OPEN sick leave where familyMember=false.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave (default familyMember=false).
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave (familyMember=false by default)
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /sick-leave/my
3. Edit the sick leave
4. Check the 'Caring for a family member' checkbox
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify UI updates to show familyMember indicator
DB-CHECK: SELECT family_member FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE id = <id> — expect TRUE
7. Edit again, uncheck the checkbox
8. Save
DB-CHECK: Verify family_member reverted to FALSE
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: familyMember flag can be toggled on edit. Norm recalculation should trigger on change.
test_cases.notes: Toggling should trigger norm recalculation — familyMember=true doesn't deduct from individual norm.

test_cases.title: familyMember=true does NOT deduct from individual norm
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with no sick leaves in current period.
SETUP: Note the employee's current norm values.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks page
3. Note current monthly norm value from tooltip
4. Create a 5-day sick leave with familyMember=TRUE
5. Navigate back to My Tasks
6. Verify monthly norm value is UNCHANGED (family member sick leave does not deduct)
7. Delete the sick leave
8. Create a 5-day sick leave with familyMember=FALSE
9. Navigate to My Tasks
10. Verify monthly norm value DECREASED (own sick leave deducts from norm)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: familyMember=true: norm unchanged. familyMember=false: norm decreased. Family member sick leaves go to budgetNorm instead.
test_cases.notes: #3409: budgetNorm = Ni + admin_vac_hrs + familyMember_SL_hrs. familyMember=true goes to budgetNorm.

test_cases.title: familyMember display in Accounting page
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Sick leaves with both familyMember=true and false.
SETUP: Via API — create 2 sick leaves with different familyMember values.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave A (familyMember=false), sick leave B (familyMember=true)
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Verify familyMember column or indicator is visible
4. Verify sick leave A shows as own illness
5. Verify sick leave B shows as family member care
6. If filter available, test filtering by familyMember type
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE both sick leaves
test_cases.expected_result: Accounting page differentiates own vs family member sick leaves. Filter available.
test_cases.notes: #3408 affects both 'My Sick Leaves' and 'Sick Leave Records' accounting page.

test_cases.title: Historical sick leaves default to familyMember=false
test_cases.preconditions:
Sick leaves that existed before #3408 deployment.
Query: SELECT sl.id, sl.employee, sl.start_date FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave sl WHERE sl.status != 'DELETED' ORDER BY sl.start_date ASC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to /accounting/sick-leaves
3. Find sick leaves created before Sprint 16 deployment
4. Verify they all show as own illness (familyMember=false)
DB-CHECK: SELECT id, family_member FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave WHERE start_date < '2026-04-01' LIMIT 10 — all should be FALSE
test_cases.expected_result: All historical sick leaves retroactively treated as familyMember=false. No data migration issues.
test_cases.notes: Migration: all existing sick leaves default to familyMember=false.

test_cases.title: Cross-page: create sick leave → verify My Tasks, Planner, Confirmation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in current reporting period. No existing sick leaves.
SETUP: Verify norm values before sick leave creation.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks — note norm tooltip value and day colors for next week
3. Navigate to Planner — note day markings for next week
4. Create a sick leave for next week (5 working days)
5. Navigate to My Tasks
6. Verify days are now marked orange (sick leave color)
7. Verify norm tooltip decreased by sick leave working hours
8. Verify input cells disabled for sick leave days (or restricted)
9. Navigate to Planner
10. Verify days are marked red for sick leave period
11. Navigate to Confirmation page (if accessible)
12. Verify sick leave displayed in both 'By Employees' and 'By Projects' views
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave creation propagates to My Tasks (orange days, reduced norm), Planner (red marking), Confirmation (visible in both views).
test_cases.notes: 8 tickets (#2778-#2915) found norm propagation failures. Cross-page cache invalidation is the root issue.

test_cases.title: Cross-page: delete sick leave → all pages revert
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an active sick leave in current period.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for current week
1. Login as the employee
2. Verify My Tasks shows orange days and reduced norm
3. Verify Planner shows red day markings
4. Delete the sick leave from /sick-leave/my
5. Navigate to My Tasks
6. Verify day colors revert to normal (black/default)
7. Verify norm tooltip increases back to full value
8. Verify cells are editable again
9. Navigate to Planner
10. Verify red markings removed
CLEANUP: Already deleted
test_cases.expected_result: All pages revert after deletion: My Tasks (colors, norm, cells), Planner (markings). No stale cache.
test_cases.notes: Root issue: aggressive frontend caching. Pages don't invalidate on state changes from other pages.

test_cases.title: Cross-page: reject sick leave → norm reverts on all pages
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an active sick leave. Accountant to reject.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for current period
1. Login as the employee
2. Verify My Tasks shows orange days
3. Login as the accountant
4. Reject the sick leave (accounting_status=REJECTED)
5. Login as the employee
6. Navigate to My Tasks
7. Verify day colors revert to black (#2863 regression test)
8. Verify norm tooltip recalculates
9. Navigate to Planner
10. Verify red markings removed (#2792)
11. Check Availability Chart copy-to-clipboard (#2812)
12. Verify rejected sick leave NOT included in copied data
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Rejection triggers full propagation: My Tasks colors revert, norm recalculates, Planner clears, Availability excludes.
test_cases.notes: Combined regression test for 3 HotFix tickets. All involved rejection not propagating.

test_cases.title: Cross-page: day-off interaction with sick leave norm (#2901)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a calendar event (day-off) and overlapping sick leave.
SETUP: Via API — create calendar event, create overlapping sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — ensure a production calendar day-off exists in the period. Create overlapping sick leave.
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks
3. Verify norm accounts for both sick leave and day-off
4. Move the day-off outside the sick leave period (via /vacation/my Days Off tab)
5. Navigate to My Tasks
6. Verify norm recalculates to account for: sick leave still present, day-off moved out
7. Verify norm correctly reflects the day-off is no longer overlapping
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE sick leave, revert day-off if possible
test_cases.expected_result: Moving a day-off during sick leave triggers norm recalculation. Both sick leave and day-off impact reflected correctly.
test_cases.notes: #2901 HotFix: moving day-off during overlapping sick leave didn't trigger norm recalculation.

test_cases.title: Cross-page: daily norm tooltip accuracy (#2915)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a sick leave in the current period.
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create sick leave for 3 working days in current month
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Tasks
3. Hover over a sick leave day to see the norm tooltip
4. Verify the tooltip shows two values: actual hours / daily norm
5. Verify the second value (daily norm) is correct — should reflect reduced norm for sick leave
6. Hover over a non-sick-leave day
7. Verify the daily norm is the standard value (8h or office-specific)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave
test_cases.expected_result: Daily norm tooltip correctly shows reduced hours for sick leave days. Second value (after slash) is accurate.
test_cases.notes: #2915: tooltip norm (second value after slash) was wrong. Sprint 9 fix.

test_cases.title: Tab visibility — EMPLOYEE-only user sees 1 tab (My Tasks)
test_cases.preconditions:
User with EMPLOYEE role only (no manager/accountant/admin roles).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM ttt_backend.employee_role WHERE role_id IN (SELECT id FROM ttt_backend.role WHERE name != 'ROLE_EMPLOYEE')) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the EMPLOYEE-only user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify only 1 tab is visible: 'My tasks'
4. Verify no other tabs appear (My projects, Department, Office, etc.)
5. Verify the tab content loads with the employee's own task data
6. Verify the Statistics menu has NO submenu (direct link, not dropdown)
test_cases.expected_result: EMPLOYEE-only sees exactly 1 tab: 'My tasks'. Menu is a direct link to /statistics/general, not a dropdown.
test_cases.notes: Verified live: alsmirnov (EMPLOYEE-only) sees 1 tab. Permissions: VIEW_MY_TASKS only.

test_cases.title: Tab visibility — multi-role user sees 8+ tabs
test_cases.preconditions:
User with multiple roles (DM + TL + PM + accountant or similar).
Query: SELECT be.login, COUNT(DISTINCT r.name) AS role_count FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE be.enabled = true GROUP BY be.login HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT r.name) >= 5 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the multi-role user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Count visible tabs
4. Verify at least 8 tabs visible (My tasks, My projects, Employees on my projects, Department projects, Department employees, Office projects, Office employees, Tasks by employees)
5. Click each tab and verify data loads without error
6. Verify the Statistics menu shows a dropdown with 'General Statistics' and 'Employee Reports' sub-items
test_cases.expected_result: Multi-role user sees 8+ tabs based on their permissions. Maximum possible: 13 tabs (including Customer tabs). Menu shows dropdown.
test_cases.notes: Live verified: pvaynmaster (7 roles) sees 8 tabs. Customer tabs (VIEW_CUSTOMER) never observed.

test_cases.title: Date range filter — select custom range and verify data
test_cases.preconditions:
Any user with statistics access.
Query: SELECT be.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id WHERE be.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify default date range is Jan 1 – Dec 31 of current year
4. Click the start date picker, select first day of last month
5. Click the end date picker, select last day of last month
6. Click 'Refresh data' button
7. Verify table updates with data for the selected period only
8. Verify 'For the period' column shows hours within selected range
9. Verify 'Total' column may differ from 'For the period' (Total is all-time)
test_cases.expected_result: Date range filter restricts data to selected period. Two datepickers with calendar icons. 'For the period' reflects the range; 'Total' is cumulative.
test_cases.notes: Default: Jan 1 – Dec 31 current year. Period presets dropdown also available.

test_cases.title: Period preset dropdown — quick date range selection
test_cases.preconditions: Any user with statistics access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Click the period preset dropdown ('Please select a date range')
4. Verify preset options include common periods (This month, Last month, This quarter, This year, etc.)
5. Select 'Last month'
6. Verify start and end date pickers update to last month's range
7. Click 'Refresh data'
8. Verify data shows last month's statistics
test_cases.expected_result: Period preset dropdown auto-fills start and end date pickers. Data refreshes for the selected period.
test_cases.notes: Dropdown text: 'Please select a date range'. Presets auto-set both datepicker values.

test_cases.title: Tree vs Flat mode toggle
test_cases.preconditions: User with at least 2 tabs and data in the selected period.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify 'Flat' radio button is selected by default
4. Verify table shows flat rows (no hierarchy)
5. Click 'Tree' radio button
6. Verify table restructures into tree mode (expandable parent rows)
7. Click an expand icon on a parent row (employee → projects → tasks)
8. Verify child rows appear with indent
9. Click 'Flat' to switch back
10. Verify table returns to flat layout
test_cases.expected_result: Tree/Flat toggle changes table structure. Tree mode has expandable hierarchy. Flat is default.
test_cases.notes: Flat is default. Tree mode: employee → project → task hierarchy. Uses rc-table.

test_cases.title: Hours vs Days toggle
test_cases.preconditions: User with data in the selected period.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify 'Hours' radio button is selected by default
4. Note the values in 'For the period' and 'Total' columns
5. Click 'Days' radio button
6. Verify values recalculate (approximately hours / 8 = days)
7. Click 'Hours' to switch back
8. Verify original hour values restored
test_cases.expected_result: Hours/Days toggle converts displayed effort values. Hours is default. Days = Hours / 8.
test_cases.notes: Hours default. Days conversion via effortDisplayType setting.

test_cases.title: Search filter — keyboard layout correction
test_cases.preconditions: User with statistics access. Employee name known in both Latin and Cyrillic.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Type an employee name using the WRONG keyboard layout (e.g., type Cyrillic characters when meaning Latin, or vice versa)
4. Verify search still finds the correct employee
5. Clear the search
6. Type the employee name correctly
7. Verify the same result appears
8. Verify search works for: first name, last name, and login
test_cases.expected_result: Search with wrong keyboard layout still finds the employee. correctLayout() function handles transliteration.
test_cases.notes: #298: SuggestionMappingUtil.correctLayout() handles wrong keyboard. Also supports URL params (#2624).

test_cases.title: Table sorting — sort by column headers
test_cases.preconditions: User with data showing multiple rows.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Click 'For the period' column header
4. Verify rows sort ascending by period hours
5. Click again
6. Verify rows sort descending
7. Click 'Employee / project / task' column header
8. Verify rows sort alphabetically
9. Click 'Start date' column header
10. Verify rows sort by date
test_cases.expected_result: Columns are sortable with ascending/descending toggle. Sort indicators (↑↓) visible.
test_cases.notes: Sortable columns: Employee/project/task, For the period, Total, Start date, End date.

test_cases.title: Tree mode — expand employee node to see project/task hierarchy
test_cases.preconditions: User with data. Employee who reported on multiple projects.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Switch to 'Tree' mode
4. Find an employee row with an expand icon
5. Click the expand icon
6. Verify project rows appear as children
7. Click expand on a project row
8. Verify task rows appear as grandchildren
9. Verify hours sum: sum of task hours ≈ project hours ≈ employee total
10. Collapse all and verify rows disappear
test_cases.expected_result: Three-level tree: employee → project → task. Child rows appear on expand. Hours are hierarchically consistent.
test_cases.notes: rc-table tree-table with lazy child expansion. Each level fetches from a different API endpoint.

test_cases.title: Absence icons — sick leave and vacation icons with tooltip
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a vacation or sick leave in the selected date range.
SETUP: Ensure data exists — check via API.
Query: SELECT DISTINCT sl.employee FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave sl WHERE sl.status != 'DELETED' AND sl.start_date <= CURRENT_DATE AND sl.end_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as a user with statistics access
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Set date range to include the period with absence
4. Find the employee row
5. Verify sick leave icon (if applicable) appears on the row
6. Hover over the sick leave icon
7. Verify tooltip shows: total hours + individual periods with dates and status
8. Verify vacation icon appears (if applicable)
9. Hover over vacation icon
10. Verify tooltip shows vacation periods within the selected range only
test_cases.expected_result: AbsencesIcon shows IconSick and IconVacation inline. Tooltip shows breakdown: total hours + period dates + status + payment type.
test_cases.notes: #2435: Show only period within selected range (N-dash dates). Both sub-systems display icons.

test_cases.title: CompanyStaff profile link and Report page link
test_cases.preconditions: User viewing statistics with employee rows visible.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Find an employee row
4. Verify CompanyStaff profile link icon is present
5. Click the CS profile link icon
6. Verify it opens CompanyStaff profile page (https://companystaff.noveogroup.com/profile/{login})
7. Go back to statistics
8. Find the employee row again
9. Verify Report page link icon is present
10. Click the Report page link icon
11. Verify it navigates to the employee's report page
test_cases.expected_result: Two action icons per employee: CS profile link and Report page link. Both navigate correctly.
test_cases.notes: Hardcoded CS URL: https://companystaff.noveogroup.com/profile/ (tech debt #4).

test_cases.title: Reset all filters button
test_cases.preconditions: User with active filters applied.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Apply a search filter (type an employee name)
4. Change date range to a custom period
5. Select 'Tree' mode
6. Verify 'Reset all filters' button is enabled
7. Click 'Reset all filters'
8. Verify search cleared, date range reset to Jan 1 – Dec 31, mode reset to 'Flat'
9. Verify button becomes disabled (no active filters)
test_cases.expected_result: Reset clears all filters, date range, and view mode to defaults. Button disabled when no filters active.
test_cases.notes: Button disabled when no filters active. Resets: search, date range, list type, effort type.

test_cases.title: Access Employee Reports as privileged user
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ADMIN or DEPARTMENT_MANAGER role.
Query: SELECT be.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_ADMIN', 'ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER') AND be.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Verify Statistics menu shows dropdown with 'General Statistics' and 'Employee Reports'
3. Click 'Employee Reports'
4. Verify /statistics/employee-reports page loads
5. Verify page title shows 'Employee Reports'
6. Verify table with employee data is displayed
7. Verify columns: Employee, Manager, Reported, Norm, Excess (Превышение), Comment
test_cases.expected_result: Privileged users see Statistics submenu with both pages. Employee Reports loads with 6 columns and employee data.
test_cases.notes: Roles with access: ADMIN, CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT, OFFICE_ACCOUNTANT, DEPARTMENT_MANAGER, TECH_LEAD.

test_cases.title: Access Employee Reports as EMPLOYEE-only — 403
test_cases.preconditions:
User with EMPLOYEE role only.
Query: (same as TC-STAT-001)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the EMPLOYEE-only user
2. Navigate directly to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Verify 403 Forbidden or access denied page
4. Verify Statistics menu does NOT show 'Employee Reports' submenu
5. Verify the typo: 'You do'nt have access' (BUG-STAT-UI-1)
test_cases.expected_result: EMPLOYEE-only users get 403 on Employee Reports. No menu item visible. Known typo in error message.
test_cases.notes: BUG-STAT-UI-1: typo 'You do'nt have access' (misplaced apostrophe). Low severity.

test_cases.title: Employee search — live filtering by name/login
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user with access to multiple employees.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. In the search field, start typing an employee's first name
4. Verify table filters live as you type (no submit button needed)
5. Clear the search
6. Type the employee's last name
7. Verify same employee found
8. Clear and type the employee's login
9. Verify found by login
10. Clear and type using wrong keyboard layout
11. Verify search still finds the employee (layout correction)
test_cases.expected_result: Live search by first name, last name, login. Wrong keyboard layout correction works. No submit button — table updates as user types.
test_cases.notes: Note from #3195 QA: Bug 1 = no dynamic text filtering. This was fixed.

test_cases.title: Month picker — default is last open for approval
test_cases.preconditions:
Privileged user. Check which month is last open for approval in user's salary office.
Query: SELECT MAX(report_date) FROM ttt_backend.accounting_period WHERE status = 'OPEN' AND office_id = (SELECT office_id FROM ttt_vacation.employee WHERE login = '<user_login>')
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Check the month picker value
4. Verify it defaults to the last month open for approval/confirmation (not current month)
5. Change to a different month
6. Verify table refreshes with the new month's data
7. Change back to the default month
8. Verify data matches
test_cases.expected_result: Month picker defaults to last open for approval month (not current). Changing month reloads employee data.
test_cases.notes: #3195 Bug 6: default month was incorrectly set to current month instead of last open for approval.

test_cases.title: Employee row — click name opens CS profile
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user viewing Employee Reports.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Click an employee's name in the Employee column
4. Verify CompanyStaff profile page opens for that employee
5. Verify the URL is https://companystaff.noveogroup.com/profile/{login}
6. Go back to Employee Reports
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking employee name navigates to CompanyStaff profile page. Not to report page.
test_cases.notes: #3195 Bug 4: name click originally opened report page instead of CS page. Fixed.

test_cases.title: Employee row — report icon on hover
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user viewing Employee Reports.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Hover over an employee row
4. Verify report icon appears on hover
5. Hover over the icon
6. Verify tooltip: 'Employee report page'
7. Click the icon
8. Verify it navigates to the employee's report page
9. Go back to Employee Reports
test_cases.expected_result: Report icon appears on row hover. Tooltip: 'Employee report page'. Click navigates to /report page for that employee.
test_cases.notes: #3195 Bug 3: report icon not shown on hover. Was fixed.

test_cases.title: Employee row expand — project breakdown
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user. Employee with reports on 2+ projects in selected month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Click anywhere on an employee row (except the name)
4. Verify accordion expands showing project breakdown
5. Verify each project row shows: project name, reported hours, percentage of employee total
6. Verify projects are sorted by hours descending
7. Click the row again
8. Verify accordion collapses
test_cases.expected_result: Row click (not on name) expands accordion with per-project breakdown. Projects sorted by hours descending.
test_cases.notes: Click on name → CS page. Click elsewhere → expand. projectBreakdown re-fetched on each navigation.

test_cases.title: Manager column — CS link and filter dropdown
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user viewing Employee Reports with employees under different managers.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Verify 'Manager' column is present
4. Click a manager's name in the Manager column
5. Verify it opens CompanyStaff profile for that manager
6. Go back
7. Click the Manager column header filter dropdown
8. Select a specific manager
9. Verify table filters to show only employees under that manager
10. Clear the filter
11. Verify all employees shown again
test_cases.expected_result: Manager column: name links to CS profile. Header has filter dropdown to filter by manager.
test_cases.notes: #3309: Manager column + filter added in Sprint 14. Filter is dropdown in column header.

test_cases.title: Reported column — arrows and color coding
test_cases.preconditions:
Privileged user. Month with employees having over-report AND under-report.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.reported_effort, sr.month_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND sr.reported_effort > 0 ORDER BY ABS(sr.reported_effort - sr.month_norm/60.0) DESC LIMIT 10
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Find an employee with reported hours ABOVE budget norm
4. Verify red arrow up (↑) indicator next to the reported value
5. Verify text is red if excess % > notification.reporting.over threshold
6. Find an employee with reported hours BELOW budget norm
7. Verify purple arrow down (↓) indicator
8. Verify text is purple if |excess %| > notification.reporting.under threshold
9. Find an employee with reported ≈ norm (neutral)
10. Verify no arrow, normal text color
test_cases.expected_result: Reported column: red ↑ for over-report, purple ↓ for under-report. Color applied when excess exceeds configured thresholds.
test_cases.notes: Thresholds from admin settings: notification.reporting.over, notification.reporting.under. #3195 Bug 5: thresholds were not applied.

test_cases.title: Norm column — budgetNorm display rules (3 cases)
test_cases.preconditions:
Privileged user. Month with employees in each display case.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND sr.month_norm != sr.budget_norm LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. CASE 1 — Employee with admin vacation (budgetNorm != individualNorm):
   - Find employee with Norm showing format '{X} ({Y})' (e.g. '136 (144)')
   - Verify first number is individual norm, number in brackets is budget norm
4. CASE 2 — Employee with regular vacation/sick leave only:
   - Find employee with Norm showing single number (e.g. '144')
   - This is budgetNorm which equals individualNorm
5. CASE 3 — Employee with no absences:
   - Find employee with full office norm (e.g. '152')
   - This is the general office norm
6. Verify info icon next to 'Norm' column header
7. Hover over info icon
8. Verify tooltip explains the dual display format
test_cases.expected_result: Case 1: '{individual} ({budget})'. Case 2: '{budget}' (=individual). Case 3: '{budget}' (=office norm). Info tooltip present.
test_cases.notes: #3381: budgetNorm = individualNorm + admin vacation hours. #3409 adds family sick leave hours.

test_cases.title: Excess column — percentage formatting and N/A edge case
test_cases.preconditions:
Privileged user. Need employee with norm=0 and reported>0.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND (sr.month_norm = 0 OR sr.budget_norm = 0) AND sr.reported_effort > 0 LIMIT 3
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Find an employee with excess in range (−1%, +1%):
   - Verify display shows 1 decimal place (e.g. '0.5%' or '-0.3%')
4. Find an employee with excess ≥ 1% or ≤ −1%:
   - Verify display shows integer (e.g. '15%' or '-8%')
5. Find an employee with norm=0, reported=0:
   - Verify display shows '0%'
6. Find an employee with norm=0, reported>0:
   - Verify display shows '+N/A%'
   - Verify this employee sorts at TOP of the list (maximum value in sort)
test_cases.expected_result: (-1,+1) → 1 decimal. |excess|≥1 → integer. norm=0/reported=0 → 0%. norm=0/reported>0 → +N/A% (sorts as max).
test_cases.notes: #3195 Bug 7: was showing '0.00%' instead of '+N/A%'. Bug 8: wrong decimal formatting.

test_cases.title: Comment field — inline edit, save on blur/Tab
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user with Employee Reports access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Click the Comment field for an employee
4. Verify edit mode activates with cursor in textarea
5. Type 'Test comment — session 102'
6. Press Enter key
7. Verify Enter creates a new paragraph (NOT save)
8. Type 'Second line'
9. Press Tab key
10. Verify comment is saved (edit mode exits)
