11. Navigate away and back to /statistics/employee-reports
12. Verify the saved comment persists
13. Click the comment field again
14. Clear the text
15. Click outside the field
16. Verify comment is saved as empty on blur
test_cases.expected_result: Enter = new paragraph. Tab or click-outside = save + exit edit. Comments stored per employee per month. Persists across navigation.
test_cases.notes: #3309: Comment field added. API: POST /v1/statistic/report saves. No auto-save on tab close — data lost.

test_cases.title: Comment field — per-month storage
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user. Two different months with data.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select month A (e.g., last month)
4. Add comment for an employee: 'Month A comment'
5. Select month B (e.g., two months ago)
6. Add comment for same employee: 'Month B comment'
7. Switch back to month A
8. Verify comment shows 'Month A comment' (not 'Month B comment')
9. Switch to month B
10. Verify comment shows 'Month B comment'
test_cases.expected_result: Comments are per-employee per-month. Changing month shows that month's comments, not a shared comment.
test_cases.notes: Stored in statistic_report.comment, keyed by (employee_login, report_date).

test_cases.title: Default sorting — by excess percentage descending
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user with multiple employees having different excess values.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Without changing any sort, examine the Excess column values
4. Verify employees are sorted by excess % descending (highest over-reporters at top)
5. Verify +N/A% entries (if any) appear at the very top (sorted as maximum)
6. Verify under-reporters (negative %) appear at the bottom
test_cases.expected_result: Default sort: by excess percentage descending. +N/A% at top. Negative excess at bottom.
test_cases.notes: Confluence: 'По умолчанию — по Превышению (процент) по убыванию'.

test_cases.title: budgetNorm — employee with administrative vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who had an administrative (unpaid) vacation in the selected month.
Query: SELECT v.employee, v.start_date, v.end_date FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v WHERE v.payment_type = 'ADMINISTRATIVE' AND v.status IN ('NEW','APPROVED','PAID') AND v.start_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 month') ORDER BY v.start_date DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN or CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the month containing the admin vacation
4. Find the employee
5. Verify Norm column shows '{individualNorm} ({budgetNorm})' format
6. Verify budgetNorm = individualNorm + admin_vacation_working_hours
7. Verify Excess uses budgetNorm (not personalNorm) as denominator
DB-CHECK: SELECT month_norm, budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = '<month_start>'
test_cases.expected_result: budgetNorm includes admin vacation hours that personalNorm excludes. Display shows dual format. Excess formula uses budgetNorm.
test_cases.notes: Code: filterNonAdministrativeVacations() removes ADMINISTRATIVE from budgetNorm calculation input.

test_cases.title: budgetNorm — employee with family member sick leave (#3409)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a family member sick leave (familyMember=true) in the selected month.
Query: SELECT sl.employee, sl.start_date FROM ttt_vacation.sick_leave sl WHERE sl.family_member = true AND sl.status != 'DELETED' AND sl.start_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 month') ORDER BY sl.start_date DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the month with family member sick leave
4. Find the employee
5. Verify Norm column shows '{individualNorm} ({budgetNorm})'
6. Verify budgetNorm = individualNorm + familyMember_sickleave_working_hours
7. Verify own sick leave (familyMember=false) IS deducted from individual norm
8. Verify family member sick leave is NOT deducted from individual norm but IS added to budget norm
DB-CHECK: SELECT month_norm, budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = '<month_start>'
test_cases.expected_result: Family member sick leave hours add to budgetNorm (like admin vacation). Own sick leave reduces individualNorm. Different treatment.
test_cases.notes: #3409 Sprint 16: Nb = Ni + admin_vacation_hrs + familyMember_sickleave_hrs. Depends on #3408 deployment.

test_cases.title: personalNorm vs budgetNorm — no difference (regular vacation only)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with ONLY regular (paid) vacation in the month, no admin vacation or family sick leave.
Query: SELECT v.employee FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v WHERE v.payment_type = 'REGULAR' AND v.status IN ('NEW','APPROVED','PAID') AND v.start_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 month') AND v.employee NOT IN (SELECT v2.employee FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v2 WHERE v2.payment_type = 'ADMINISTRATIVE' AND v2.start_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 month')) LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the month with the regular vacation
4. Find the employee
5. Verify Norm column shows SINGLE number (e.g., '144') — no brackets
6. This means budgetNorm == individualNorm
7. Verify the norm is reduced by regular vacation hours (< full office norm)
DB-CHECK: SELECT month_norm, budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = '<month_start>' — expect month_norm = budget_norm
test_cases.expected_result: When no admin vacation or family sick leave, budgetNorm = individualNorm. Norm column shows single number.
test_cases.notes: renderNormHours: budgetNorm !== individualNorm → dual format; else → single number.

test_cases.title: Partial-month employee — mid-month hire (#3356)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who was hired mid-month (employment start date is NOT the 1st of the month).
Query: SELECT ep.employee_login, ep.period_start FROM ttt_vacation.employee_period ep WHERE EXTRACT(DAY FROM ep.period_start) > 1 AND ep.period_start >= '2025-01-01' ORDER BY ep.period_start DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the month when the employee was hired
4. Find the employee
5. Verify Norm is calculated only for working days FROM hire date TO end of month
6. Verify the norm is LESS than the full office norm for that month
7. Verify Excess % is calculated correctly against the partial norm
DB-CHECK: SELECT month_norm, budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = '<month_start>'
test_cases.expected_result: Mid-month hire: norm reflects only working days from hire date. effectiveBounds clamps the range.
test_cases.notes: #3356: effectiveBounds clamps date range to (max(rangeStart, periodStart), min(rangeEnd, periodEnd)).

test_cases.title: Terminated employee — shown in last month only (#3320)
test_cases.preconditions:
Recently terminated employee.
Query: SELECT ep.employee_login, ep.period_end FROM ttt_vacation.employee_period ep WHERE ep.period_end IS NOT NULL AND ep.period_end >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' ORDER BY ep.period_end DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the month the employee was terminated in
4. Verify the employee IS shown in the list
5. Note their norm (should be partial-month if terminated mid-month)
6. Switch to the month AFTER termination
7. Verify the employee is NOT shown
8. Switch to a month BEFORE termination
9. Verify the employee IS shown with full norm
test_cases.expected_result: Terminated employees: shown in their last active month with partial norm, hidden in subsequent months. #3320 fix.
test_cases.notes: #3320: filter uses ttt_vacation.employee_period dates, not ttt_backend.employee.last_date.

test_cases.title: norm=0, reported=0 — display 0%
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with norm=0 AND no reported hours in the month (e.g., full month vacation/sick leave).
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND sr.budget_norm = 0 AND sr.reported_effort = 0 LIMIT 3
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Find the employee with norm=0, reported=0
4. Verify Excess column shows '0%'
5. Verify no color coding (neutral)
6. Verify no arrow indicator
test_cases.expected_result: norm=0, reported=0 → Excess = '0%'. Neutral display, no arrows, no color.
test_cases.notes: Confluence 4.4.4: explicit corner case specification.

test_cases.title: norm=0, reported>0 — display +N/A%, sort as maximum
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with norm=0 AND reported hours > 0.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.reported_effort FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND sr.budget_norm = 0 AND sr.reported_effort > 0 LIMIT 3
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Verify default sort (by excess descending)
4. Check the TOP of the list for '+N/A%' entries
5. Find the employee with norm=0, reported>0
6. Verify Excess shows '+N/A%'
7. Verify this employee appears at the TOP (sorted as maximum value)
8. Verify reporting status indicator present (arrow up, color)
test_cases.expected_result: norm=0 with reported>0 → '+N/A%'. Sorted as maximum value — appears at top of descending sort.
test_cases.notes: #3195 Bug 7: was showing '0.00%' instead of '+N/A%'. ExcessStatus.NA in backend.

test_cases.title: Over-limit toggle — filter by threshold (#3306)
test_cases.preconditions:
Privileged user. Admin settings configured: notification.reporting.over=10, notification.reporting.under=-10.
Query: SELECT value FROM ttt_backend.application_settings WHERE key IN ('notification.reporting.over', 'notification.reporting.under')
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Note the total number of employees listed
4. Click the 'Only excess over limit' toggle (switch to ON)
5. Verify table filters to show ONLY employees whose excess exceeds thresholds:
   - Over-reporters: excess > notification.reporting.over (e.g., > 10%)
   - Under-reporters: |excess| > |notification.reporting.under| (e.g., < -10%)
6. Verify the filtered list is shorter than the full list
7. Verify all shown employees have excess outside the threshold range
8. Toggle OFF
9. Verify full list restored
test_cases.expected_result: Over-limit toggle filters employees by configured thresholds. Only excess > over OR < under thresholds shown.
test_cases.notes: #3306: toggle was broken — didn't filter. Fixed. Prerequisites: admin settings must be configured.

test_cases.title: Color indicators — over/under/neutral per thresholds
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user. Employees with different excess levels visible.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Find over-reporter (excess > notification.reporting.over threshold):
   - Verify red text color on Reported and Excess values
   - Verify red arrow up (↑)
4. Find under-reporter (|excess| > |notification.reporting.under| threshold):
   - Verify purple text color
   - Verify purple arrow down (↓)
5. Find neutral employee (excess within thresholds):
   - Verify normal text color (black)
   - Verify no arrow
6. Find employee with excess between 0 and threshold (e.g., +5% with threshold +10%):
   - Verify normal color (excess exists but below threshold)
   - Verify arrow direction (up for positive)
test_cases.expected_result: Red = over threshold. Purple = under threshold. Normal = within range. Arrows indicate direction regardless of threshold.
test_cases.notes: reportedNotificationStatus: LOW→underReported, HIGH/NA→overReported, NEUTRAL→normal. Thresholds NOT hardcoded.

test_cases.title: Excess behavior — working during vacation = over-report
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who worked during their regular vacation in the selected month.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.reported_effort, sr.month_norm, sr.budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND sr.reported_effort > sr.budget_norm / 60.0 ORDER BY (sr.reported_effort - sr.budget_norm / 60.0) DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Find the employee who worked during vacation
4. Verify their norm was reduced by vacation days (personal norm < office norm)
5. Verify their reported hours exceed the reduced norm
6. Verify they show as OVER-reported (red, arrow up)
7. Verify excess % is calculated against budgetNorm (not personalNorm)
test_cases.expected_result: Working during vacation → over-report. Budget norm denominator means admin vacation hours count against the employee.
test_cases.notes: #3381 behavior note: working during regular vacation/sick leave → over-report.

test_cases.title: CSV export — download classic statistics data
test_cases.preconditions: User with statistics access and data in selected period.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Set date range and select a tab with data
4. Click 'Export' dropdown button
5. Select 'Download CSV'
6. Verify CSV file downloads
7. Open the CSV file
8. Verify data matches the table content
9. Verify column headers present
10. Verify hours values match the displayed values
test_cases.expected_result: CSV downloads with table data. Values match what is displayed in the UI. Headers included.
test_cases.notes: Export dropdown has 3 options: Copy table, Download CSV, Copy link for Google tables.

test_cases.title: CSV export — hours vs days unit parameter (#1422)
test_cases.preconditions: User with statistics access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Set toggle to 'Hours'
4. Export CSV
5. Note values in the CSV
6. Switch toggle to 'Days'
7. Export CSV again
8. Compare values: Days CSV values ≈ Hours CSV values / 8
9. Verify units parameter is correctly passed in the export request
test_cases.expected_result: CSV export respects hours/days toggle. Hours and Days CSVs have proportional values (factor of 8).
test_cases.notes: #1422: units parameter support added to export endpoints.

test_cases.title: CSV export — empty params regression (#2191)
test_cases.preconditions: User with statistics access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Clear all filters (reset)
4. Without selecting any specific tab or filter, click Export → Download CSV
5. Verify export does NOT return 400 error
6. Verify CSV downloads with default data (or appropriate error message)
7. Apply a filter, then export
8. Verify export works with applied filters
test_cases.expected_result: Export with empty/default params should work (not 400). Regression test for #2191.
test_cases.notes: #2191 CLOSED: 400 error when params empty was a regression. Verify it stays fixed.

test_cases.title: Copy table to clipboard
test_cases.preconditions: User with statistics data visible.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Click 'Export' dropdown
4. Select 'Copy the table'
5. Verify success notification (table copied)
6. Open a spreadsheet application or text editor
7. Paste
8. Verify pasted data matches the statistics table structure
test_cases.expected_result: Table data copied to clipboard. Pasteable into spreadsheet. Data matches displayed table.
test_cases.notes: Export dropdown: 'Copy the table' option.

test_cases.title: Copy link for Google Tables
test_cases.preconditions: User with statistics data visible.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Click 'Export' dropdown
4. Select 'Copy link for Google tables'
5. Verify success notification (link copied)
6. Open a Google Sheets document
7. Use IMPORTDATA or paste the link
8. Verify data loads from the link
test_cases.expected_result: Shareable data link copied to clipboard. Compatible with Google Sheets IMPORTDATA function.
test_cases.notes: Third export option. Generates a URL that can be used as a data source.

test_cases.title: Admin-only largest customers export (#2096)
test_cases.preconditions: User with ADMIN role.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify 'employees-largest-customers' export option is available
4. Click export
5. Verify CSV downloads with customer-employee data
6. Login as a non-ADMIN user
7. Verify 'employees-largest-customers' export is NOT available
test_cases.expected_result: Largest customers CSV export is admin-only. Non-admin users don't see the option.
test_cases.notes: #2096: GET /v1/statistic/export/employees-largest-customers — admin-only endpoint.

test_cases.title: Norm export by individual calendar (#3400)
test_cases.preconditions: Admin or privileged user.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to the norm export feature (may be under Employee Reports or admin)
3. Trigger the individual calendar norm export
4. Verify CSV downloads
5. Verify CSV contains columns: login, name, surname, department_manager_login, salary_office, individual_norm
6. Verify individual_norm values are correct (compare with Employee Reports Norm column)
test_cases.expected_result: CSV export with per-employee individual norm by calendar. Contains employee details and norm.
test_cases.notes: #3400 Sprint 15: Simple CSV export. Production Ready.

test_cases.title: WSR tree view — basic display and expand/collapse
test_cases.preconditions: User with WSR access. Period with WSR data.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to the WSR (Weekly Status Report) view
3. Verify tree structure displays with weekly grouping
4. Expand a week node
5. Verify project/task breakdown appears
6. Try to collapse the expanded node
7. Note: #3041 — task branches may NOT collapse (known bug)
8. Verify data matches the selected period
test_cases.expected_result: WSR tree view renders weekly status data. Known bug: branches may not collapse (#3041).
test_cases.notes: #3030: layout broken. #3041: branches don't collapse. Both OPEN bugs.

test_cases.title: WSR — multiple sub-issues (#3289)
test_cases.preconditions: User with WSR access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to WSR view
3. Verify WSR lists open behavior (should not auto-open all)
4. Verify tree view is enabled (not disabled)
5. Check update button logic — verify it shows correct period data
6. Switch language to English
7. Verify tooltip translations are correct
8. Click the update button
9. Verify it doesn't break the list view
10. Check for unnecessary top-level node
test_cases.expected_result: Regression test for 6 WSR sub-issues: lists always open, tree view disabled, update button, tooltip translation, button breaks list, unnecessary node.
test_cases.notes: #3289 OPEN: 6+ sub-issues reported. All are WSR-specific regression checks.

test_cases.title: WSR — update button shows wrong period data (#3144)
test_cases.preconditions: User with WSR access. Select a specific week.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to WSR view
3. Select a specific weekly period
4. Verify data shown matches the selected week
5. Click the 'Update' button
6. Verify the data refreshes for the SAME period (not a different one)
7. Change the period selection
8. Click Update again
9. Verify the new period's data is shown
test_cases.expected_result: Update button refreshes data for the currently selected period, not a different one.
test_cases.notes: #3144 OPEN: update button shows wrong period data.

test_cases.title: ADMIN — sees all 13 tabs + all employees in Employee Reports
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_ADMIN.
Query: SELECT be.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_ADMIN' AND be.enabled = true LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Count all visible tabs
4. Verify maximum tabs visible (up to 13 depending on data: My tasks, My projects, Employees on my projects, Department projects, Department employees, Office projects, Office employees, Tasks by employees, Project tasks, Customer projects, Customer employees, Manager projects, Manager employees)
5. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
6. Verify ALL employees across all offices/departments are listed
7. Verify employee count matches total active employees
test_cases.expected_result: ADMIN: maximum tab visibility + all employees in Employee Reports. No data scoping.
test_cases.notes: ADMIN and CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT have identical access: all tabs + all employees.

test_cases.title: OFFICE_ACCOUNTANT — own office employees only
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_ACCOUNTANT assigned to a specific office.
Query: SELECT a.login, o.name AS office FROM ttt_vacation.employee a JOIN ttt_vacation.office_accountant oa ON a.id = oa.employee_id JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON oa.office_id = o.id WHERE a.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the OFFICE_ACCOUNTANT
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Verify only employees from the accountant's assigned salary office(s) are shown
4. Count employees — should be less than total
5. Search for an employee from a DIFFERENT office
6. Verify that employee is NOT found
7. Navigate to /statistics/general
8. Verify Office tabs are visible
9. Verify data is scoped to own office
test_cases.expected_result: OFFICE_ACCOUNTANT sees only employees from their assigned salary offices. No cross-office data.
test_cases.notes: Uses findAllByOfficesOrManagers() with officeIds filter.

test_cases.title: DEPARTMENT_MANAGER — subordinates including contractors (#3147)
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER. Has subordinates including at least one contractor.
Query: SELECT m.login AS dm, e.login AS employee, e.contractor FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id JOIN ttt_backend.employee be ON m.login = be.login JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER' AND m.enabled = true AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the DEPARTMENT_MANAGER
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Verify subordinate employees are listed (direct reports in department)
4. Verify contractor employees are also visible (#3147 fix)
5. Verify employees from OTHER departments are NOT shown
6. Navigate to /statistics/general
7. Verify Department tabs are visible (Department projects, Department employees)
8. Verify data is scoped to own department
test_cases.expected_result: DM sees subordinates including contractors. Other departments excluded. Department tabs visible.
test_cases.notes: #3147: DM access extended to include contractor statistics.

test_cases.title: TECH_LEAD — own subordinates only
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_TECH_LEAD.
Query: SELECT be.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_TECH_LEAD' AND be.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the TECH_LEAD
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Verify only own subordinate employees are listed
4. Verify department tabs visible in General Statistics
5. Verify employee count is smaller than DM count (TL has narrower scope)
test_cases.expected_result: TL sees only their direct subordinates in Employee Reports. Department tabs visible in General.
test_cases.notes: TL scope via techLeadId filter in findAllByOfficesOrManagers().

test_cases.title: OFFICE_HR — sees only assigned employees (#3247)
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_OFFICE_HR.
Query: SELECT be.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_OFFICE_HR' AND be.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as OFFICE_HR
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Verify only HR-assigned employees are shown (NOT all office employees)
4. Count employees — should be less than full office count
5. Verify employees outside HR assignment are NOT visible
test_cases.expected_result: HR sees only their assigned employees, not all office employees. #3247 fix prevents over-exposure.
test_cases.notes: #3247: HR managers previously saw ALL employees — fixed to show only assigned. Caused regression #3298.

test_cases.title: PROJECT_MANAGER — project tabs only, no Employee Reports
test_cases.preconditions:
User with ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER only.
Query: SELECT be.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_PROJECT_MANAGER' AND be.enabled = true AND be.id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM ttt_backend.employee_role WHERE role_id IN (SELECT id FROM ttt_backend.role WHERE name IN ('ROLE_ADMIN','ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT','ROLE_ACCOUNTANT','ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER','ROLE_TECH_LEAD'))) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the PROJECT_MANAGER
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify Project tabs visible (Tasks by employees, etc.)
4. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
5. Verify 403 or no access (PM has no Employee Reports access)
6. Verify Statistics menu has no 'Employee Reports' sub-item
test_cases.expected_result: PM sees project-related tabs in General Statistics but has NO access to Employee Reports.
test_cases.notes: PM → PROJECT tabs only. No Employee Reports access unless also has another qualifying role.

test_cases.title: Search by projects — regression after HR hotfix (#3298)
test_cases.preconditions: User with search by projects permission (VIEW_PROJECT or similar).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Use the search/filter to search by project name
4. Verify project search returns results
5. Verify no 403 or empty results error
6. Filter by a specific project
7. Verify only data for that project is shown
test_cases.expected_result: Search by projects works correctly. No regression from HR hotfix #3247.
test_cases.notes: #3298 CLOSED: Search by projects broke after HR hotfix #3247 — overlapping permission changes caused regression.

test_cases.title: Customer filter — requires VIEW_CUSTOMER permission
test_cases.preconditions: Two users: one with VIEW_CUSTOMER permission, one without.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as user WITH VIEW_CUSTOMER permission
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify 'Customer' filter is available (4th filter type)
4. Verify Customer tabs visible (Customer projects, Customer employees)
5. Login as user WITHOUT VIEW_CUSTOMER permission
6. Navigate to /statistics/general
7. Verify 'Customer' filter is NOT available
8. Verify only 3 filters visible (project, employee, task)
9. Verify no Customer tabs
test_cases.expected_result: Customer filter and tabs require VIEW_CUSTOMER permission. Without it: 3 filters and no customer tabs.
test_cases.notes: 4 filters with VIEW_CUSTOMER, 3 without. Customer tabs never observed in live testing.

test_cases.title: My Projects — parent total vs expanded children hour mismatch
test_cases.preconditions:
User with My Projects tab. Projects with active and dismissed employees.
Query: SELECT p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT pm.employee_id) AS members FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.project_member pm ON p.id = pm.project_id GROUP BY p.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pm.employee_id) > 5 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 3
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Select 'My projects' tab
4. Switch to 'Tree' mode
5. Note the parent row total hours (For the period) for a project
6. Expand the project node
7. Sum all visible child employee hours
8. Compare: parent total vs sum of visible children
9. If mismatch exists, enable showFired parameter (if available)
10. Verify mismatch resolves when fired employees included
test_cases.expected_result: Parent total may include fired employee hours while children don't show fired employees. Known systematic issue (8 tickets).
test_cases.notes: Root cause: /departments includes fired, /employees filters by showFired=false. 8 tickets: #2097,#2108,#1923,#2112,#2122,#2123,#2142,#2143.

test_cases.title: Department projects — parent-child hour inconsistency
test_cases.preconditions: User with Department projects tab. Department with dismissed employees.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as DEPARTMENT_MANAGER or ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Select 'Department projects' tab
4. Switch to 'Tree' mode
5. Find a department row with significant total hours
6. Expand it
7. Sum all visible project/employee hours
8. Compare with the parent total
9. Document any discrepancy amount
10. Verify via API: GET /v1/statistic/departments (includes fired) vs GET /v1/statistic/employees?showFired=false
test_cases.expected_result: Mismatch expected for departments with fired employees. Document the discrepancy for each tab.
test_cases.notes: #1923: 'Department projects'/'Office projects' sum > project total. Fired employee inclusion is root cause.

test_cases.title: Office projects — hour sum verification
test_cases.preconditions: User with Office projects tab.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN or OFFICE_DIRECTOR
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Select 'Office projects' tab
4. Switch to 'Tree' mode
5. Expand an office row
6. Sum children hours
7. Compare with parent total
8. Document any discrepancy
test_cases.expected_result: Same parent-child mismatch pattern as other tabs when fired employees exist.
test_cases.notes: All tree tabs share the same root cause. Testing all tabs confirms the systemic nature.

test_cases.title: showFired parameter — toggle fired employees visibility
test_cases.preconditions: Tab with fired employee hours contributing to mismatches.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Select any tab in Tree mode
4. Note the parent total
5. Expand and sum children (showFired=false default)
6. Note the mismatch
7. Via API: GET /v1/statistic/employees?showFired=true&...
8. Compare the API response with showFired=true vs showFired=false
9. Verify showFired=true response totals match the parent total
10. If UI has a 'show fired' toggle, test it
test_cases.expected_result: showFired=true includes dismissed employee hours — totals should match parent. showFired=false creates mismatches.
test_cases.notes: API: /v1/statistic/employees?showFired=true|false. Default is false. Parent totals always include fired.

test_cases.title: Customer team — extreme mismatch example (#2143)
test_cases.preconditions: User with Customer tabs visible. Customer with dismissed team members.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Select 'Customer team' tab (if visible)
4. Find a customer entry in Tree mode
5. Note parent total hours
6. Expand and sum children
7. Document the mismatch (historically extreme: 205.57 vs 36.9)
8. Compare with other tabs to confirm pattern
test_cases.expected_result: Customer team tab shows same mismatch pattern. Historically extreme differences documented.
test_cases.notes: #2143 CLOSED: extreme mismatch 205.57 vs 36.9. Most dramatic example of the systemic issue.

test_cases.title: Tasks by employees — project-level hour aggregation
test_cases.preconditions: User with 'Tasks by employees' tab (VIEW_PROJECT permission).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Select '@ Tasks by employees' tab
4. Switch to Tree mode
5. Expand a task to see employee breakdown
6. Sum employee hours
7. Verify sum matches the task total
8. Check for fired employee inclusion in the total
test_cases.expected_result: Task-level aggregation consistent with visible employees. Same showFired pattern applies.
test_cases.notes: VIEW_PROJECT permission required. Tree: task → employee hierarchy.

test_cases.title: Cache vs live data consistency — Employee Reports accuracy
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. Both cache table and live calculation available.
Query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select current month
4. Note reported hours and norm for 5 random employees
DB-CHECK: For each employee, verify:
  SELECT reported_effort, month_norm, budget_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
5. Compare UI values with DB values
6. Verify reported_effort (DB hours) matches 'Reported' column
7. Verify budget_norm / 60 (DB minutes → hours) matches 'Norm' column
test_cases.expected_result: Employee Reports data matches statistic_report table. Units: DB reported_effort in HOURS, month_norm/budget_norm in MINUTES.
test_cases.notes: Unit mismatch: reported_effort in HOURS, month_norm/budget_norm in MINUTES. Conversion: minutes/60 = hours.

test_cases.title: Cache update after vacation creation — month_norm recalculated
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee without vacation in current month.
SETUP: Note current statistic_report values.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login, sr.month_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) AND sr.employee_login NOT IN (SELECT employee FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE status != 'CANCELED' AND start_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Note current month_norm for the employee
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations to create a 5-day vacation in current month
1. Wait 10 seconds for MQ event processing
DB-CHECK: SELECT month_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
2. Verify month_norm has DECREASED by the vacation working hours
3. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports as ADMIN
4. Find the employee
5. Verify Norm column reflects the updated (reduced) value
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the vacation. Wait for MQ processing. Verify norm reverts.
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation creation triggers RabbitMQ event → month_norm recalculated within seconds. UI reflects the change.
test_cases.notes: MQ event: vacation create → month_norm recalc via RabbitMQ topic exchange. 10s wait for async processing.

test_cases.title: Cache update after sick leave creation — month_norm recalculated
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee without sick leave in current month.
SETUP: Note current month_norm.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Note current month_norm for the employee
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves to create a 3-day sick leave
1. Wait 10 seconds for event processing
DB-CHECK: SELECT month_norm FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
2. Verify month_norm decreased by sick leave working hours
3. If sick leave spans 2 months, verify BOTH months' records updated
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the sick leave. Verify norm reverts.
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave event triggers month_norm recalculation. Two-month sick leaves update both months separately.
test_cases.notes: !5200: SickLeaveCreated/Changed/DeletedEventListener. Two-month handling via separate events.

test_cases.title: Cache update after task report — reported_effort updated
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with existing statistic_report record.
SETUP: Note current reported_effort.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Note reported_effort for the employee in current month
SETUP: Via API — submit a task report (e.g., 4 hours for a task)
1. Wait 5 seconds for @TransactionalEventListener processing
DB-CHECK: SELECT reported_effort FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<login>' AND report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
2. Verify reported_effort increased by the submitted hours
3. Navigate to Employee Reports
4. Verify Reported column shows updated value
CLEANUP: Via API — delete the task report.
test_cases.expected_result: Task report add triggers reported_effort update via @TransactionalEventListener. Immediate (not waiting for cron).
test_cases.notes: TaskReportEventListener: add/patch/delete trigger effort recalc for employee/month.

test_cases.title: Dismissed employee — no ghost records after termination (#3345)
test_cases.preconditions:
Recently dismissed employee.
Query: SELECT ep.employee_login, ep.period_end FROM ttt_vacation.employee_period ep WHERE ep.period_end IS NOT NULL AND ep.period_end >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months' ORDER BY ep.period_end DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
DB-CHECK: SELECT report_date FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report WHERE employee_login = '<dismissed_login>' ORDER BY report_date DESC LIMIT 5
1. Verify no statistic_report records exist for months AFTER the employee's termination date
2. Verify records exist for months BEFORE termination
3. Verify the last record's report_date is <= termination month
4. Navigate to Employee Reports as ADMIN
5. Select the month after termination
6. Verify the employee is NOT listed
test_cases.expected_result: No ghost records for months after termination. #3345 fix: dismissed employees cleaned up during sync.
test_cases.notes: #3345: dismissed employees had records for months after termination (ghost data). Fixed during sync.

test_cases.title: Manual sync trigger — test endpoint (non-prod)
test_cases.preconditions:
Non-production environment (qa-1 or timemachine). API access.
SETUP: Note current statistic_report row count.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report
1. Via API — POST /api/ttt/test/v1/statistic-reports (trigger optimized sync)
2. Verify 200 response
3. Wait 30 seconds for sync to complete
DB-CHECK: COUNT(*) FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report
4. Verify row count is same or slightly changed (sync is idempotent)
5. Verify month_norm_updated_at and reported_updated_at timestamps updated for current month records
test_cases.expected_result: Manual sync trigger works on non-prod. Sync is idempotent — re-running doesn't duplicate data.
test_cases.notes: Test endpoint: POST /v1/test/statistic-reports. Non-prod only. Triggers optimized sync (current + previous month).

test_cases.title: #3195 Bug 4 — employee name click should open CS page, not report page
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user with Employee Reports access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Click an employee's NAME in the Employee column
4. Verify it opens CompanyStaff profile page (not report page)
5. Verify URL is https://companystaff.noveogroup.com/profile/{login}
6. Go back to Employee Reports
7. Hover over the employee row to reveal report icon
8. Click the report icon
9. Verify THIS navigates to the employee's report page
test_cases.expected_result: Name click → CS profile. Report icon click → report page. Two separate navigation targets.
test_cases.notes: #3195 QA: Bug 4 was 'employee name click opens report page (should open CS page)'. Fixed.

test_cases.title: #3195 Bug 9 — English translation for 'Only overage' switch
test_cases.preconditions: Privileged user. English language.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the privileged user
2. Switch language to English (if not already)
3. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
4. Find the 'Only excess over limit' toggle switch
5. Verify it has English text (not Russian 'Только превышения')
6. Switch language to Russian
7. Verify Russian text appears for the toggle
test_cases.expected_result: Toggle has proper English translation: 'Only excess over limit' (or similar). No untranslated Russian text in EN mode.
test_cases.notes: #3195 Bug 9: No English translation for the switcher. Should be fixed.

test_cases.title: #3320 — future employees excluded from list
test_cases.preconditions:
ADMIN user. Employee with hire date in the future (not yet started).
Query: SELECT ep.employee_login, ep.period_start FROM ttt_vacation.employee_period ep WHERE ep.period_start > CURRENT_DATE LIMIT 3
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the current month
4. Search for the future employee by name/login
5. Verify they are NOT shown in the list
6. Select the month when the employee will start
7. Verify the employee IS shown (if their start date is in that month)
8. Verify no false under-report percentages for future employees
test_cases.expected_result: Future employees (not yet hired) excluded from current month list. No false underreport data.
test_cases.notes: #3320: filter now uses ttt_vacation.employee_period dates, not ttt_backend.employee.last_date.

test_cases.title: #3380 — vacations affect personal monthly norm
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a vacation in the current month.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation if needed.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the month with the vacation
4. Find the employee
5. Verify personal norm is REDUCED by vacation working hours
6. Verify norm column shows lower value than full office norm
7. Compare with an employee without vacation in the same month
8. Verify their norm is the full office norm
test_cases.expected_result: Vacations reduce personal monthly norm. Employee with vacation: norm < office norm. Without vacation: norm = office norm.
test_cases.notes: #3380 OPEN: vacations don't affect personal monthly norm (wrong API call in frontend). Sprint 15.

test_cases.title: #2366 — 'No data' notification persistence after filter removal
test_cases.preconditions: User with statistics access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Apply a filter that returns NO data (e.g., search for non-existent employee name)
4. Verify 'No data' notification appears
5. Clear the filter (remove search text)
6. Verify the 'No data' notification disappears
7. Verify data reappears in the table
8. Apply and remove the filter several times
9. Verify notification state is always consistent with data availability
test_cases.expected_result: 'No data' notification appears when filter returns empty results and disappears when filter is removed.
test_cases.notes: #2366 OPEN: 'No data' notification persists after filter removal — state not cleared properly.

test_cases.title: #1175 — outdated tabs on first load
test_cases.preconditions: User with multiple roles (multiple tabs).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general for the FIRST time in this session
3. Verify tabs load correctly (no stale data from previous session)
4. Verify the correct tab is auto-selected (or first available)
5. Verify no flash of incorrect tab content
6. Switch between tabs
7. Verify each tab loads fresh data
test_cases.expected_result: First load shows correct, current tabs. No stale data from redux-persist. Tab auto-selection works.
test_cases.notes: #1175 OPEN: statisticsPermissionsTabName mostly commented out → currentTab=null. redux-persist may show stale filters.

test_cases.title: #2716 — error on 'Refresh data' click
test_cases.preconditions: User viewing General Statistics with data.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the user
2. Navigate to /statistics/general
3. Verify data is loaded
4. Click 'Refresh data' button
5. Verify NO error appears (no 500, no red notification)
6. Verify data refreshes correctly
7. Change date range
8. Click 'Refresh data' again
9. Verify data updates for the new range
test_cases.expected_result: 'Refresh data' button works without errors. Data refreshes for current filter/date selection.
test_cases.notes: #2716 OPEN: error on 'Refresh data' click. Regression test.

test_cases.title: #3368 — over/under report notification on Confirmation By Employee page
test_cases.preconditions:
User with confirmation access. Employee with over-report in current period.
Query: SELECT sr.employee_login FROM ttt_backend.statistic_report sr WHERE sr.report_date = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month') AND sr.reported_effort > sr.budget_norm / 60.0 + 10 LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as DM or confirmation approver
2. Navigate to 'Confirmation' page
3. Switch to 'By Projects' view
4. Verify over/under report notifications ARE shown for employees
5. Switch to 'By Employee' view
6. Verify over/under report notifications ARE ALSO shown (was missing)
7. Verify the notification data matches Employee Reports page excess values
test_cases.expected_result: 'Confirmation > By Employee' shows over/under report notifications. Cross-page API call integration complete.
test_cases.notes: #3368: 'By Employee' didn't call ttt/v1/statistic/report/employees API → notification missing. Sprint 15.

test_cases.title: #3353 — rehired employee pre-employment norm display
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who was rehired (multiple employment periods).
Query: SELECT ep.employee_login, COUNT(*) AS periods FROM ttt_vacation.employee_period ep GROUP BY ep.employee_login HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as ADMIN
2. Navigate to /statistics/employee-reports
3. Select the month of the GAP between employment periods
4. Verify the rehired employee is NOT shown (not employed in that month)
5. Select the month of re-hire
6. Verify the employee IS shown with partial norm (from hire date)
7. Select a month in the first employment period
8. Verify correct norm for that period
test_cases.expected_result: Rehired employees: gap months = not shown. Re-hire month = partial norm. Each period calculated separately.
test_cases.notes: #3353 OPEN: rehired employees show previously working days in orange. Pre-employment should be 0/0/{totalNorm}.

test_cases.title: API error handling — missing required params return 500 instead of 400
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/summary (WITHOUT required 'login' param)
2. Verify response is 500 (MissingServletRequestParameterException)
3. Note: should be 400 Bad Request
4. Via API — GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/effort (WITHOUT required 'taskId' param)
5. Verify response is 500 (same bug)
6. Via API — GET /api/ttt/v1/reports/total (WITHOUT required 'type' param)
7. Verify response is 400 (correct — uses DTO validation)
8. Document the inconsistency
test_cases.expected_result: Missing @RequestParam returns 500 (bug). Missing @Valid DTO params return 400 (correct). Inconsistent error handling.
test_cases.notes: Systematic: all @RequestParam-required endpoints return 500 on missing params. DTO-bound @Valid properly returns 400.

test_cases.title: Create a close tag — happy path (UI)
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with tracker integration. User is PM or SPM of the project.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON p.manager_id = e.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND p.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the project manager
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects tab
3. Select the project from dropdown
4. Click the Project Settings icon (gear icon at far right of table header)
5. In the Project Settings modal, click 'Tasks closing' tab
6. In the 'Add a tag' input field, type '[closed]'
7. Click the '+' button
8. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
9. Verify '[closed]' appears in the tags table
10. Verify the new tag row has green left-border highlight
11. Verify the table auto-scrolled to the new tag
test_cases.expected_result: Tag '[closed]' created and visible in tags table. Green highlight on new row. 'Changes saved' notification displayed. Auto-scroll to new item.
test_cases.notes: POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags returns HTTP 200 (not 201). Immediate save, no confirmation needed.

test_cases.title: Create duplicate tag — idempotent behavior
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with existing tag '[closed]'.
SETUP: Via API — POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with {"tag":"[closed]"}
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag '[closed]' for the project
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project > Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Type '[closed]' in the input field (same as existing tag)
4. Click '+' button
5. Verify behavior: either silent no-op or 'Changes saved' notification
6. Verify only ONE '[closed]' tag exists in the table (no duplicate)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/{tagId}
test_cases.expected_result: Duplicate create is idempotent — returns existing tag. No duplicate row in table. Backend catches DataIntegrityViolationException and returns existing entity.
test_cases.notes: Backend uses REQUIRES_NEW transaction; on unique constraint violation, catches and returns existing tag.

test_cases.title: Create blank/whitespace tag — validation error
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM of a project.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Leave the tag input empty and click '+'
4. Verify validation error or button disabled
5. Type '   ' (spaces only) and click '+'
6. Verify validation error: 'Tag must not be blank'
test_cases.expected_result: Blank and whitespace-only tags are rejected. @NotBlank validation enforced. HTTP 400 with error message.
test_cases.notes: Backend validates @NotBlank on tag field. Frontend may also have client-side validation.

test_cases.title: Inline edit a tag — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with existing tag 'test-tag'.
SETUP: Via API — create tag
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags with {"tag":"test-tag"}
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Click on the tag text 'test-tag' in the table (cursor should be pointer/hand)
4. Verify field becomes editable (inline edit mode)
5. Change text to 'updated-tag'
6. Press Enter to save
7. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
8. Verify tag text now shows 'updated-tag'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Inline editing works: click to edit, Enter to save. PATCH /close-tags/{tagId} called. Tag updated in table.
test_cases.notes: Inline editing uses PlannerTag.js component. Escape should cancel without saving.

test_cases.title: Inline edit — Escape cancels without saving
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with existing tag.
SETUP: Via API — create tag
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag 'original-tag'
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Click on 'original-tag' to enter inline edit mode
4. Change text to 'modified'
5. Press Escape key
6. Verify tag text reverts to 'original-tag'
7. Verify no 'Changes saved' notification appeared
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Escape cancels inline edit. No PATCH request sent. Original text preserved.

test_cases.title: Edit tag to duplicate value — validation error
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with two tags: 'tag-a' and 'tag-b'.
SETUP: Via API — create both tags
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create 'tag-a' and 'tag-b'
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Click on 'tag-b' to edit inline
4. Change text to 'tag-a' (duplicate of existing)
5. Press Enter
6. Verify error message: 'Planner close tag already exists for project'
7. Verify 'tag-b' remains unchanged
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE both tags
test_cases.expected_result: Duplicate update rejected. ValidationException thrown. Tag not modified.
test_cases.notes: No-op optimization: if new tag equals existing tag, returns without saving.

test_cases.title: Delete a tag — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with existing tag.
SETUP: Via API — create tag
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag 'to-delete'
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Find 'to-delete' in the tags table
4. Click the delete (trash) icon on that row
5. Verify 'Changes saved' notification appears
6. Verify 'to-delete' is removed from the tags table
test_cases.expected_result: Tag deleted from table. DELETE /close-tags/{tagId} called. Immediate removal, no confirmation dialog.
test_cases.notes: Deletion is immediate — no confirmation prompt (consistent with tag creation).

test_cases.title: List tags — empty state
test_cases.preconditions: Project with no close tags configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Verify the tags table shows 'No data' message
4. Verify explanatory text is visible above the table: 'Project tickets containing added values in the Info column will be automatically removed from the list on days when there are no more reports for them'
test_cases.expected_result: Empty state shows 'No data'. Informational text about auto-closing behavior is displayed.

test_cases.title: Permission — employee can list tags but cannot create
test_cases.preconditions:
Plain employee (not PM/SPM/admin) on a project with tags.
SETUP: Via API — create tags as PM
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API (as PM) — create tag 'test-tag'
1. Login as plain employee who is a member of the project
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select the project
3. Attempt to open Project Settings
4. If accessible: verify tags are visible in Tasks closing tab (GET returns tags)
5. Verify: add tag input and '+' button are disabled or hidden
6. Via API — POST /close-tags as plain employee → verify HTTP 403
7. Via API — PATCH /close-tags/{id} as plain employee → verify HTTP 403
8. Via API — DELETE /close-tags/{id} as plain employee → verify HTTP 403
CLEANUP: Via API (as PM) — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Employee can list tags (GET 200) but cannot create/update/delete (403). Permission model: binary all-or-nothing based on project role.
test_cases.notes: PlannerCloseTagPermissionService checks: isAdmin, managerId, seniorManagerId, ownerId.

test_cases.title: Permission — project manager can CRUD tags
test_cases.preconditions:
User is PM of a project.
Query: SELECT p.id, e.login FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON p.manager_id = e.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND p.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the project manager
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Create a tag 'pm-test' → verify success (200)
4. Edit 'pm-test' to 'pm-updated' → verify success (200)
5. Delete 'pm-updated' → verify success (200)
6. Verify all operations triggered 'Changes saved' notification
test_cases.expected_result: PM has full CRUD permissions on close tags for their project.
test_cases.notes: Permission check: current.getId().equals(project.getManagerId())

test_cases.title: Permission — senior manager can CRUD tags
test_cases.preconditions:
User is SPM of a project.
Query: SELECT p.id, e.login FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.employee e ON p.senior_manager_id = e.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND p.enabled = true AND p.senior_manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the senior project manager
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Create a tag 'spm-test' → verify success
4. Delete 'spm-test' → verify success
test_cases.expected_result: SPM has full CRUD permissions. Permission check: current.getId().equals(project.getSeniorManagerId()).

test_cases.title: Cross-project tag access — rejected
test_cases.preconditions:
Two projects: A (with tag) and B. User is PM of B but not A.
SETUP: Via API — create tag on project A
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API (as PM of A) — create tag 'cross-test' on project A, note tagId
1. Via API (as PM of B) — DELETE /v1/projects/{projectB_id}/close-tags/{tagId_from_A}
2. Verify HTTP 400
3. Verify error: 'Planner close tag does not belong to project {projectB_id}'
4. Via API — PATCH /v1/projects/{projectB_id}/close-tags/{tagId_from_A} → verify HTTP 400
CLEANUP: Via API (as PM of A) — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Cross-project access blocked. Ownership check: tag.project.id must equal pathVariable projectId.
test_cases.notes: Tested on qa-1 S72: returns 400 with ValidationException.

test_cases.title: Special characters in tag — Unicode, Cyrillic
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM of a project.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Create tag 'Done / Résolu' → verify success
4. Create tag 'Закрыто' (Cyrillic) → verify success
5. Create tag '<script>alert(1)</script>' → verify stored raw (XSS concern)
6. Verify all tags appear correctly in the table
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all created tags
test_cases.expected_result: Unicode and Cyrillic accepted. Tags stored as-is (no HTML sanitization). XSS concern: special characters not escaped.
test_cases.notes: Tested on qa-1 S72: Unicode accepted. No HTML sanitization — potential XSS if rendered unsafely.

test_cases.title: Long tag near VARCHAR(255) limit
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /close-tags with 255-character tag → verify success (200)
2. Via API — POST /close-tags with 256-character tag → verify truncation or error
3. Note: Confluence spec says 200 char limit (§7.4.2) but DB allows 255
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE created tags
test_cases.expected_result: DB accepts 255 chars (VARCHAR(255)). Confluence says 200 char limit but no frontend validation enforces it. Verify actual boundary.
test_cases.notes: Known discrepancy: Confluence 200 chars vs DB 255 chars. No frontend validation found.

test_cases.title: Multiple tags — create 5+ tags for a project
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Create tags: '[closed]', '[done]', '[resolved]', 'finished', 'cancelled'
4. Verify all 5 tags appear in the table
5. Verify table is scrollable if content exceeds visible area
6. Verify each tag can be edited and deleted independently
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all 5 tags
test_cases.expected_result: Multiple tags supported. No max tag count per project. Each tag independently manageable.
test_cases.notes: No pagination on GET /close-tags — returns all tags. No max count enforced.

test_cases.title: Apply — assignment without reports gets closed
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with tag matching an assignment's Info column. Assignment has NO reports on selected date.
SETUP: Via API — create tag, ensure assignment with matching ticket_info exists.
Query: SELECT ta.id, t.ticket_info FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment ta JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON ta.task_id = t.id WHERE ta.project_id = {projectId} AND t.ticket_info IS NOT NULL AND t.ticket_info != '' LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag matching an existing assignment's ticket_info
SETUP: Ensure the assignment has no task_report for the selected date
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project
3. Verify the matching assignment is visible in the table
4. Click Project Settings icon > Tasks closing tab
5. Verify the matching tag is listed
6. Click OK button
7. Verify loading spinner appears
8. Verify page reloads automatically
9. After reload: verify the matching assignment is NO LONGER visible in the table
DB-CHECK: SELECT closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment WHERE id = {assignmentId} → expect true
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment with matching tag and no reports is closed (closed=true). Row disappears from planner table. WebSocket PATCH event published.
test_cases.notes: POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/apply with {date}. Requires the apply endpoint to be deployed.

test_cases.title: Apply — assignment WITH reports stays visible
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with tag matching an assignment. Assignment HAS reports on selected date.
SETUP: Via API — create tag, ensure assignment has task_report for the date.
Query: SELECT ta.id FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment ta JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON ta.task_id = t.id JOIN ttt_backend.task_report tr ON tr.task = t.id AND tr.executor = ta.employee_id WHERE ta.project_id = {projectId} AND t.ticket_info ILIKE '%{tag}%' AND tr.report_date = '{date}' LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag, verify assignment has reports on date
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project
3. Note the assignment row with matching tag AND reported hours
4. Open Project Settings > Tasks closing > click OK
5. Wait for page reload
6. Verify the assignment is STILL visible in the table (because it has reports)
DB-CHECK: SELECT closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment WHERE id = {id} → may be true, but hasReportOnDate prevents disappearance
test_cases.expected_result: Assignment with reports stays visible even when tag matches. hasReportOnDate() returns true → close event NOT published. Hours data preserved.
test_cases.notes: Core safety rule: reported hours are never hidden by close-by-tag.

test_cases.title: Apply — case-insensitive matching
test_cases.preconditions:
Assignment with ticket_info '[CLOSED]' (uppercase). Tag 'closed' (lowercase).
SETUP: Via API — create tag 'closed'
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag 'closed' for project
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects
3. Verify an assignment with Info '[CLOSED]' is visible
4. Open Project Settings > Tasks closing > click OK
5. After reload: verify the '[CLOSED]' assignment is closed
DB-CHECK: Verify StringUtils.containsIgnoreCase matches 'closed' in '[CLOSED]'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the tag
test_cases.expected_result: Case-insensitive match: tag 'closed' matches '[CLOSED]', 'Closed', 'already-closed', etc. Uses StringUtils.containsIgnoreCase().

test_cases.title: Apply — substring matching (tag matches partial text)
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment with ticket_info 'prefix-closed-suffix'. Tag 'closed'.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag 'closed'
1. Verify assignment with Info containing 'closed' as substring is visible
2. Trigger apply via OK
3. After reload: verify assignment is closed
Note: This also means tag 'fix' would match 'prefix', 'fixed', 'fixture'
test_cases.expected_result: Substring matching: 'closed' matches any text containing 'closed'. Uses containsIgnoreCase (not exact match).
test_cases.notes: Known false positive risk: short tags can match unintended text.

test_cases.title: Apply — false positive: tag 'Done' matches 'Donetskaya'
test_cases.preconditions:
Assignment with ticket_info containing 'Anna Donetskaya'. Tag 'Done'.
Note: This is a KNOWN and ACCEPTED false positive.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag 'Done'
1. Verify assignment with Info 'Anna Donetskaya' is visible
2. Trigger apply
3. Verify the assignment gets closed (false positive match)
4. Document: stakeholders have accepted this behavior
test_cases.expected_result: False positive: 'Done' matches 'Donetskaya'. This is acknowledged and accepted by stakeholders. Substring matching has inherent false positive risk.
test_cases.notes: Stakeholders explicitly acknowledged this corner case.

test_cases.title: Apply on specific date — only affects selected date
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with close tag. Assignments on multiple dates.
SETUP: Via API — create tag and assignments on two different dates
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag matching assignments. Assignments exist on both Day1 and Day2.
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects, set date to Day1
3. Trigger apply (OK)
4. After reload: verify Day1 assignments closed
5. Navigate to Day2
6. Verify Day2 matching assignments are STILL visible (not affected)
DB-CHECK: Check closed status for both days' assignments
test_cases.expected_result: Apply is date-scoped. POST /apply sends {date: currentDay}. Only the selected date's assignments are processed.
test_cases.notes: Frontend sends currentDay from Redux store. Backend uses section.date or section.startDate.

test_cases.title: Apply with no tags — no API call, no reload
test_cases.preconditions: Project with NO close tags configured.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects > select project with no tags
3. Open Project Settings
4. Click OK
5. Verify: page does NOT reload (no API call made)
6. Verify: modal closes normally
DEBUG: Check browser DevTools Network tab — no POST /close-tags/apply request
test_cases.expected_result: Empty-tags guard: if projectTags.length === 0, saga returns immediately. No apply API call. No page reload.
test_cases.notes: Guard added in !5341. Critical for projects without close-by-tag configuration.

test_cases.title: Apply — page reloads after successful apply
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tags.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a tag
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects
3. Open Project Settings > click OK
4. Verify loading spinner appears (startLoadingEmployeeModal)
5. Verify page reloads (window.location.reload)
6. After reload: verify planner state is fresh (assignments updated)
test_cases.expected_result: After apply: loading spinner shown, then window.location.reload() refreshes the page. Full state refresh.
test_cases.notes: window.location.reload() is a blunt approach. The finally block (stopLoading + closeModal) is dead code since page reloads.

test_cases.title: Apply from Project Members tab — triggers if tags exist
test_cases.preconditions: Project with close tags. User is on Project Members tab.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a tag
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings modal
3. Stay on 'Project members' tab (do NOT switch to Tasks closing)
4. Click OK
5. Verify: apply is STILL triggered (if tags exist)
6. Verify: page reloads
Note: Both tabs share the same OK handler after !5341
test_cases.expected_result: Both tabs' OK button triggers apply. If project has tags, apply runs regardless of which tab is active. Mitigated by empty-tags guard.
test_cases.notes: Design side effect: user who only changed Project Members still triggers close-tags apply.

test_cases.title: Apply — generated (not-yet-opened) assignments also closed
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee whose assignments are NOT yet 'opened for editing' (readOnly). Project has matching tag.
SETUP: Via API — create tag
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag matching a task's ticket_info
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects
3. Verify employee row shows as read-only (not opened for editing)
4. Trigger apply via OK
5. After reload: verify the generated assignments for that employee are also processed
DB-CHECK: SELECT id, closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment WHERE employee_id = {id} AND closed = true → verify new closed records created
test_cases.expected_result: Close-by-tag handles generated (id=null) assignments by creating them as closed via createForCloseByTag(). Works even for employees not yet 'opened for editing'.
test_cases.notes: Silent failure: if createForCloseByTag throws, error is swallowed with log.debug() only.

test_cases.title: Apply after 'Open for editing' — newly generated assignments eligible
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with readOnly=true. Project has matching tag.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects
3. Click 'Open for editing' for an employee → generates assignments
4. Verify new assignment rows appear for the employee
5. Open Project Settings > Tasks closing > click OK
6. After reload: verify matching newly-generated assignments are closed
7. Verify non-matching assignments remain visible
test_cases.expected_result: Sequence: Open for editing → Apply. Newly generated assignments with matching Info are eligible for closing.
test_cases.notes: Both features are date-scoped and use currentDay from Redux.

test_cases.title: Apply — multiple tags, partial matches
test_cases.preconditions:
Project with 3 tags. Assignments: some match tag1, some match tag2, some match none.
SETUP: Via API — create tags
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tags '[closed]', '[done]', '[resolved]'
1. Login as PM
2. Verify: 5 assignments visible — 2 matching '[closed]', 1 matching '[done]', 2 matching nothing
3. Trigger apply
4. After reload: verify 3 assignments closed (matching ones without reports)
5. Verify 2 non-matching assignments still visible
test_cases.expected_result: Each assignment checked against ALL tags. An assignment matching ANY tag is eligible for closing. Non-matching assignments unaffected.
test_cases.notes: OR logic: assignment closed if it matches any one of the project's tags.

test_cases.title: Apply — assignment with blank ticket_info is skipped
test_cases.preconditions: Assignment where task.ticket_info is null or empty.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Ensure an assignment exists with blank/null ticket_info
1. Trigger apply
2. Verify the blank-info assignment is NOT closed
DB-CHECK: SELECT t.ticket_info, ta.closed FROM ttt_backend.task_assignment ta JOIN ttt_backend.task t ON ta.task_id = t.id WHERE ta.project_id = {id} AND (t.ticket_info IS NULL OR t.ticket_info = '')
test_cases.expected_result: Assignments with null/blank ticket_info are skipped. No false positives on empty data.
test_cases.notes: Code: if ticketInfo is blank, skip.

test_cases.title: Apply endpoint — API direct call
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tags. API access.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag
1. Via API — POST /v1/projects/{projectId}/close-tags/apply with body {"date": "2026-03-28"}
2. Verify HTTP 200 (void response)
3. DB-CHECK: Verify matching assignments now have closed=true
4. Via API — POST /apply with empty body → verify behavior (null date guard)
5. Via API — POST /apply for project with no tags → verify no-op
test_cases.expected_result: Direct API call works. Returns 200 with void body. Guard: null projectId or date returns immediately.
test_cases.notes: Apply endpoint added in !5335. Requires deployment verification.

test_cases.title: Bug 1 regression — popup closes only via OK button
test_cases.preconditions: Project with Project Settings accessible. User is PM.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings modal
3. Click outside the modal (overlay area)
4. Verify: modal does NOT close (clicking outside has no effect)
5. Press Escape key
6. Verify: modal may or may not close via Escape (test both)
7. Click OK button
8. Verify: modal closes properly
test_cases.expected_result: Modal is NOT closable by clicking outside. Only OK button closes it. This is by design (confirmed by ishumchenko in comment 908000).
test_cases.notes: Regression: this was a reported bug — now 'by design'. Verify it remains consistent.

test_cases.title: Bug 3 regression — correct column header in Tasks Closing tab
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings modal > Tasks closing tab
3. Verify column header reads 'Tags for closing tasks' (EN)
4. Switch language to Russian
5. Verify column header reads 'Теги для закрытия задач' (RU)
6. Verify it does NOT show 'Role on the project' or 'Роль на проекте'
test_cases.expected_result: Correct header: 'Tags for closing tasks' (not 'Role on the project'). Bug #2724/Bug 3 was about wrong header. Fixed by !5313.
test_cases.notes: Both EN and RU affected in original bug.

test_cases.title: Bug 4 regression — OK button present in Tasks Closing tab
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings > switch to Tasks closing tab
3. Verify OK button is visible and clickable
4. Click OK
5. Verify modal closes (and triggers apply if tags exist)
test_cases.expected_result: OK button present on both tabs. Fixed by !5313. Both Project Members and Tasks closing have the OK button.

test_cases.title: Bug 6 — cannot reopen popup on heavy data project
test_cases.preconditions:
Heavy data project (many assignments, e.g., DirectEnergie-ODC-like). Tags previously configured.
Query: SELECT p.id, p.name, COUNT(ta.id) FROM ttt_backend.project p JOIN ttt_backend.task_assignment ta ON ta.project_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id, p.name ORDER BY COUNT(ta.id) DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM of a heavy-data project
2. Open Project Settings modal → Tasks closing tab
3. Add a tag → click OK (triggers apply + reload)
4. After reload: attempt to reopen Project Settings
5. Verify: does the page hang with spinner? Or does it open normally?
6. Check browser DevTools for network requests during hang
7. Document: is Bug 6 still reproducible?
test_cases.expected_result: Bug 6 (OPEN): After adding close tags on heavy data project, reopening Project Settings may hang with spinner. No network requests visible. Likely frontend state/rendering issue with large dataset.
test_cases.notes: Not reproducible on small-data projects (e.g., Diablocom-AI). Specific to large projects.

test_cases.title: Bug 8 regression — auto-refresh after closing
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tags and matching assignments.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag with matching assignments
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Planner > Projects
3. Note current assignment list
4. Open Project Settings > click OK
5. Verify loading spinner appears
6. Verify page automatically reloads (no manual refresh needed)
7. Verify assignment list is updated (closed assignments removed)
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-refresh works via window.location.reload(). Fixed by !5335/!5339. Previously required manual page reload.

test_cases.title: Task order not disrupted after close-by-tag apply
test_cases.preconditions: Project with DnD-reordered assignments. Some assignments match close tag.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create tag. Manually reorder tasks via DnD first.
1. Login as PM
2. Open for editing on an employee
3. Drag-reorder tasks: move task C above task A
4. Verify custom order persists
5. Open Project Settings > click OK (triggers apply)
6. After reload: verify remaining (non-closed) tasks maintain the custom DnD order
7. Verify no duplicate rows appear
test_cases.expected_result: Close-by-tag apply does not disrupt DnD ordering of remaining tasks. Related to #3332 and #3314 bugs.
test_cases.notes: Close-by-tag publishes TaskAssignmentPatchEvent/GenerateEvent which may trigger the #3314 re-sort bug.

test_cases.title: Informational text on Tasks Closing tab
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Open Project Settings > Tasks closing tab
3. Verify informational text is present below the tabs
4. Verify text reads: 'Project tickets containing added values in the Info column will be automatically removed from the list on days when there are no more reports for them'
5. Verify text is visible in both EN and RU
test_cases.expected_result: Explanatory text present. Matches Confluence §7.4.1 specification. Visible in both languages.

test_cases.title: Confluence discrepancy — 200 char limit not enforced
test_cases.preconditions: User is PM.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Navigate to Project Settings > Tasks closing
3. Type a 201-character tag in the input field
4. Click '+' to add
5. Verify: does frontend enforce 200-char limit (per Confluence §7.4.2)?
6. If no frontend limit: verify tag is accepted (DB allows 255)
7. Document actual boundary behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Discrepancy: Confluence says 200 chars, DB allows VARCHAR(255). Verify whether frontend has maxLength attribute on input.
test_cases.notes: Known req/code mismatch. No frontend validation found in code analysis.

test_cases.title: Apply error handling — silent failure on backend error
test_cases.preconditions: Project with tags. Simulated backend error condition.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as PM
2. Trigger apply in a condition that might cause backend error
3. Observe UI behavior: does any error message appear?
4. Check browser console for devLog(error) output
5. Note: frontend swallows errors in catch block (devLog only)
6. Note: backend swallows generated assignment creation errors with log.debug()
test_cases.expected_result: If apply fails: spinner appears, then nothing happens (no reload, no error message). Error logged to console only. Known design issue: no user-facing error feedback.
test_cases.notes: Both frontend (catch→devLog) and backend (catch→log.debug) silently swallow errors.

test_cases.title: EN tooltip text: "Reschedule event" (no "an")
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as employee with day-offs in open period. UI language = EN.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to My Vacations page → Days Off tab
2. Find a day-off row with edit icon visible
3. Hover over the edit (pencil) icon
4. Verify tooltip text reads "Reschedule event"
test_cases.expected_result: Tooltip shows "Reschedule event" (not "Reschedule an event").
test_cases.notes: Translation key: vacation.weekends.rescheduleAnEvent

test_cases.title: EN dialog title: "Reschedule event"
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as employee with editable day-off. UI language = EN.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to My Vacations page → Days Off tab
2. Click edit icon on an editable day-off row
3. Verify the dialog title reads "Reschedule event"
test_cases.expected_result: Dialog title is "Reschedule event".
test_cases.notes: Same i18n key used for both tooltip and dialog title.

test_cases.title: RU tooltip text: "Перенести событие" (unchanged)
test_cases.preconditions: Logged in as employee with day-offs. UI language = RU.
test_cases.steps:
1. Navigate to Мои отпуска → tab "Выходные"
2. Hover over the edit icon on an editable day-off
3. Verify tooltip text reads "Перенести событие"
test_cases.expected_result: RU tooltip unchanged: "Перенести событие".
test_cases.notes: Regression check — RU translation should not be affected.

test_cases.title: Edit icon visible for day-off in current open month (future date)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with day-offs in current month (approve period open). Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_dayoff_request edr ON edr.employee = e.id WHERE edr.last_approved_date >= (SELECT start_date FROM ttt_backend.office_period WHERE type = 'APPROVE' LIMIT 1) AND edr.status NOT IN ('DELETED', 'DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR') AND edr.last_approved_date > CURRENT_DATE AND e.enabled = true LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Find a day-off with date in current open month that is after today
4. Verify edit (pencil) icon is visible in the Actions column
test_cases.expected_result: Edit icon is visible for future day-offs in open approve period.
test_cases.notes: Baseline check — this behavior existed before #3404.

test_cases.title: Edit icon visible for PAST day-off in open month (core new behavior)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a day-off whose lastApprovedDate is in the current month but BEFORE today. Approve period for employee office must be open. Query: SELECT e.login, edr.last_approved_date FROM ttt_backend.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_dayoff_request edr ON edr.employee = e.id WHERE edr.last_approved_date >= (SELECT start_date FROM ttt_backend.office_period WHERE type = 'APPROVE' LIMIT 1) AND edr.last_approved_date < CURRENT_DATE AND edr.status NOT IN ('DELETED', 'DELETED_FROM_CALENDAR') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY edr.last_approved_date DESC LIMIT 5
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
