3. Find the day-off with a past date (within current open month)
4. Verify edit icon IS visible in the Actions column
5. Verify the day-off is not greyed out in the table
test_cases.expected_result: Edit icon visible for past day-offs within open approve period. This is the core new behavior from #3404.
test_cases.notes: CORE TEST — previously edit icon was hidden for past day-offs.

test_cases.title: Edit icon HIDDEN for day-off in closed month
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with day-offs in January or February (months before the approve period). Same employee as TC-T3404-005 preferred.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Switch year selector if needed to show day-offs in closed months (Jan, Feb)
4. Find a day-off with date in January or February
5. Verify edit icon is NOT visible in the Actions column
test_cases.expected_result: No edit icon for day-offs in closed approve period months.
test_cases.notes: Critical negative test — closed months must not be editable.

test_cases.title: Boundary: day-off ON approve period start date
test_cases.preconditions: Need a day-off whose lastApprovedDate equals the approve period start date exactly. This requires a holiday on the 1st of a month. May 1 (Labour Day, Russia) is a candidate if the test is run when May approve period is open.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee with a day-off on May 1 (or another 1st-of-month holiday)
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Find the day-off on May 1
4. Verify whether edit icon is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Edit icon SHOULD be visible (day-off is in open period). NOTE: Code uses > (not >=) — ST-1 risk — edit icon may be MISSING.
test_cases.notes: Tests ST-1 boundary bug. useWeekendTableHeaders.tsx:113 uses strict > instead of >=. On qa-1, March 1 is a Sunday (no holiday). May 1 is a better candidate.

test_cases.title: Edit icon hidden for last day of closed month
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a day-off on Feb 23 (Defender Day, Russia) or last day of another closed month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Find the day-off on Feb 23
4. Verify edit icon is NOT visible
test_cases.expected_result: No edit icon — Feb 23 is in a closed approve period.
test_cases.notes: Boundary: last actionable day of a closed month.

test_cases.title: Previous year day-offs — all edit icons hidden
test_cases.preconditions: Employee who had day-offs in previous year (2025).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Switch year selector to 2025
4. Verify NO edit icons are visible for any 2025 day-offs
test_cases.expected_result: All 2025 day-offs have no edit icon (previous year is fully closed).
test_cases.notes: Regression — previous year should never be editable.

test_cases.title: Closed month (January): all dates disabled in datepicker
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with an editable day-off in open month.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Click edit on an editable day-off
4. In the datepicker, navigate backward to January
5. Verify ALL January dates are disabled (greyed out / not selectable)
test_cases.expected_result: All January dates disabled — January approve period is closed.
test_cases.notes: minDate is set to approvePeriod - 1 day. January is below minDate, so entire month should be unreachable or disabled.

test_cases.title: Closed month (February): all dates disabled in datepicker
test_cases.preconditions: Same as TC-T3404-010.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Click edit on an editable day-off
3. Navigate datepicker to February
4. Verify ALL February dates are disabled
test_cases.expected_result: All February dates disabled — February approve period is closed.
test_cases.notes: Feb 28 may be the actual minDate (approvePeriod 2026-03-01 minus 1 day). Feb 28 is Saturday — disabled by renderDay.

test_cases.title: Open month (March): working days are enabled
test_cases.preconditions: Same as TC-T3404-010. Approve period starts March 1.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Click edit on an editable day-off in March
3. In the datepicker, view March dates
4. Verify working days (Mon-Fri, not holidays) are enabled/selectable
5. Verify weekends (Sat, Sun) are disabled
6. Verify dates with existing day-offs are disabled
test_cases.expected_result: March working days are enabled. Weekends and existing day-off dates are disabled.
test_cases.notes: Positive test — dates in open period should be selectable.

test_cases.title: Future month (April): working days are enabled
test_cases.preconditions: Same as TC-T3404-010.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Click edit on an editable day-off
3. Navigate datepicker forward to April
4. Verify working days are enabled, weekends disabled
test_cases.expected_result: April working days are enabled.
test_cases.notes: Future months with open approve period should always work.

test_cases.title: Boundary: Feb 28 (day before approve period) is disabled
test_cases.preconditions: Same as TC-T3404-010. Approve period starts March 1.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click edit on an editable day-off
2. Navigate datepicker to show Feb 28
3. Verify Feb 28 is disabled (not selectable)
test_cases.expected_result: Feb 28 is disabled. It is the day before the approve period start AND a Saturday.
test_cases.notes: minDate = moment(approvePeriod).subtract(1, "d") = Feb 28. Feb 28 is Saturday → double-disabled.

test_cases.title: Boundary: March 2 (first working day of open period) is enabled
test_cases.preconditions: Same as TC-T3404-010. March 1 is Sunday, March 2 is Monday.
test_cases.steps:
1. Click edit on an editable day-off
2. In the datepicker, locate March 2
3. Verify March 2 is enabled (selectable)
test_cases.expected_result: March 2 is the first working day of the open period — it should be selectable.
test_cases.notes: March 1 is Sunday (disabled). March 2 is the practical minimum selectable date.

test_cases.title: Select earlier date within same month (core new behavior)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a past day-off in open month (e.g., March 12). Edit icon is visible.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Click edit icon on the March 12 day-off
4. In the datepicker, select March 3 (an earlier working day)
5. Verify the selected date appears in the dialog as the new transfer date
6. Verify the "OK" button is enabled
test_cases.expected_result: March 3 is selectable. Transfer date shows "12.03.2026 → 03.03.2026". OK button is enabled.
test_cases.notes: CORE TEST — previously dates before the original date were disabled.

test_cases.title: First working day of month is selectable
test_cases.preconditions: Same as TC-T3404-016.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open the reschedule dialog for a mid-month day-off
2. In the datepicker, verify March 2 (first working day of March) is selectable
3. Click March 2
4. Verify it is accepted as the transfer target
test_cases.expected_result: March 2 is selectable as transfer target date.
test_cases.notes: Lower boundary of selectable dates within the open month.

test_cases.title: February dates NOT selectable (closed period)
test_cases.preconditions: Same as TC-T3404-016.
test_cases.steps:
1. Open the reschedule dialog for a March day-off
2. Navigate the datepicker backward to February
3. Verify ALL February dates are disabled/not selectable
test_cases.expected_result: No February dates are selectable — the month is in a closed approve period.
test_cases.notes: Negative test — must not allow transfer to closed months.

test_cases.title: Future holiday: minDate still uses original date (ST-4)
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with a FUTURE day-off on a mid-month holiday (e.g., May 9 Victory Day in Russia). The day-off must be in the future (after today).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee (Russian calendar office)
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Click edit on the May 9 day-off
4. In the datepicker, try to select May 2 (first working day after May 1 holidays)
5. Verify whether May 2-8 working days are selectable or disabled
test_cases.expected_result: Per requirement: May 2+ should be selectable (1st of month = May 1). Per current code: May 2-8 are NOT selectable (minDate = May 9). ST-4 risk applies.
test_cases.notes: Tests ST-4 incomplete implementation. Code uses originalDate as minDate for future day-offs instead of startOf(month).

test_cases.title: E2E: full reschedule to earlier date + approval flow
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with editable past day-off in open month, has a manager as approver.
SETUP: Via API — ensure employee has an untransferred day-off in open period (or verify existing one).
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations → Days Off tab
3. Click edit on a past day-off in the open month
4. Select an earlier date within the same month
5. Click OK to submit the transfer request
6. Verify success notification and status changes to "New" in the table
7. Logout
8. Login as the approver (manager)
9. Navigate to My Vacations → Requests → Days off rescheduling tab
10. Find the transfer request in the "For Approval" sub-tab
11. Approve the request
12. Verify status changes to "Approved"
CLEANUP: Via API — delete or revert the transfer if needed
test_cases.expected_result: Full workflow completes: employee creates backward transfer → manager approves → status is Approved.
test_cases.notes: E2E test. Modifies data — needs cleanup. Tests the complete user journey.

test_cases.title: Month-close auto-rejection: backward transfer in closing month
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW (pending) backward transfer request where both original date and target date are in the same month.
Admin access to change approve period.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Create a backward transfer request via UI (employee picks earlier date in open month)
1. Login as admin/accountant
2. Change the approve period for the employee's office to advance by one month (close the current month)
3. Verify the NEW backward transfer request is auto-rejected
4. Login as employee and verify the request shows status "Rejected"
CLEANUP: Revert the approve period to the original value
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-rejection fires on month close. The backward transfer request with both dates in the closed month is rejected.
test_cases.notes: Tests that the existing auto-rejection logic (unchanged per ticket) still works correctly for backward transfers.

test_cases.title: Vacation recalculation: transfer to date overlapping vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a day-off and an active vacation that includes a date within the same month.
The transfer target date should overlap with the vacation period.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation for the employee covering several working days in the open month
SETUP: Via API — approve the vacation
1. Login as employee
2. Navigate to Days Off tab
3. Edit a day-off and select a date that falls within the approved vacation period
4. Submit the transfer
5. Have the manager approve the transfer
6. Verify the vacation is recalculated (days reduced or vacation auto-deleted if single day)
CLEANUP: Delete test vacation and transfer
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation recalculation is not broken by the new earlier-date logic. Day-off transfer onto vacation date triggers vacation adjustment.
test_cases.notes: Regression test for P.3 requirement. Related bugs: #2833 (vacation not recalculated), #3223 (balance not updated).

test_cases.title: Max date unchanged: Dec 31 of original year
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with an editable day-off.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Click edit on a day-off
3. Navigate datepicker forward to December of the same year
4. Verify Dec 31 area is reachable but Jan 1 of next year is NOT selectable
5. Verify the maxDate boundary has not changed
test_cases.expected_result: maxDate = Dec 31 of the original date's year (unchanged behavior).
test_cases.notes: Regression — maxDate formula unchanged: moment(originalDate.format("YYYY")).add(1,"y").subtract(1,"d").

test_cases.title: Global approve period: employee in office with different period
test_cases.preconditions: Two offices with DIFFERENT approve periods (if available on env). Otherwise, note as untestable.
test_cases.steps:
1. Identify two employees in offices with different approve period start dates
2. Login as employee in the office with LATER approve period
3. Navigate to Days Off tab
4. Verify edit icon visibility matches the GLOBAL minimum approve period, not the employee's office period
test_cases.expected_result: Edit icon uses the minimum approve period across all offices (GET /v1/offices/periods/approve/min). More permissive than per-office.
test_cases.notes: Tests ST-5 design note. On qa-1, all offices have same period (2026-03-01) — may be untestable without admin manipulation.

test_cases.title: Create REGULAR vacation — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with sufficient available vacation days (>= 5) and a manager assigned.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 5 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to My Vacations page (/vacation/my)
3. Verify 'Available vacation days' counter shows >= 5
4. Click 'Create a request' button
5. In the creation dialog, select start date = next Monday, end date = next Friday
6. Verify 'Number of days' auto-calculates (expect 5 working days, may be 4 if holiday)
7. Verify payment month auto-populates to the month containing start date
8. Leave 'Unpaid vacation' unchecked (REGULAR type)
9. Verify 'Approved by' shows the employee's manager
10. Click 'Save'
11. Verify success notification appears
12. Verify new vacation row in the table with status 'New'
13. Verify 'Available vacation days' counter decreased
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation created with status NEW. Table shows correct dates, regular days count, type 'Regular', approver name, and status 'New'. Available days counter decreases by the number of working days.
test_cases.notes: Working days may be 4 instead of 5 if Mon-Fri range includes a public holiday.

test_cases.title: Create ADMINISTRATIVE (unpaid) vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Any enabled employee with a manager.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select start date = a future Monday, end date = same Monday (1-day)
5. Check 'Unpaid vacation' checkbox
6. Verify payment type changes to Administrative
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify vacation created with type 'Administrative'
9. Verify 'Available vacation days' counter did NOT decrease (unpaid doesn't consume balance)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Administrative vacation created. Type column shows 'Administrative'. Available days unchanged — ADMINISTRATIVE does not consume paid balance.
test_cases.notes: ADMINISTRATIVE skips duration and availability checks. No minimum working day requirement enforced.

test_cases.title: Create vacation with comment
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with sufficient vacation days.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 3 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select future dates (3 working days)
5. In the Comment field, type 'Family trip'
6. Click 'Save'
7. Verify vacation created
8. Click the '...' icon on the new row to open Request Details
9. Verify comment 'Family trip' is displayed in the details dialog
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Comment is saved and visible in Request Details dialog.
test_cases.notes: No character limit on comment field per DTO analysis.

test_cases.title: Create vacation with 'Also notify' recipients
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a colleague in the same office.
Query: SELECT e.login, e2.login AS colleague FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e2 ON e.office_id = e2.office_id AND e.id != e2.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND e2.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my, click 'Create a request'
3. Select future dates (3 working days)
4. In the 'Also notify' multi-select, type colleague's name and select them
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify vacation created
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.vacation_notify_also vna JOIN ttt_vacation.vacation v ON vna.vacation = v.id WHERE v.id = <created_id>
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation created. DB shows notify-also record for the selected colleague.
test_cases.notes: GET /vacations/{id} does NOT include notifyAlso — verification via DB only. Note UI typo: 'Also notifty'.

test_cases.title: Edit vacation dates (NEW status)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing NEW vacation.
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations to create a REGULAR vacation.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 10 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation for the employee (Mon-Fri next week)
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Find the NEW vacation row
4. Click the pencil (edit) icon
5. Verify 'Editing vacation request' dialog opens
6. Change end date to one week later (extend by 5 days)
7. Verify 'Number of days' recalculates
8. Click 'Save'
9. Verify vacation row updates with new dates
10. Verify status remains 'New'
11. Verify available days counter reflects the new duration
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation dates updated. Day count recalculated. Status remains NEW. Available days adjusted.
test_cases.notes: PUT update requires 'id' in request body in addition to URL path parameter.

test_cases.title: Edit APPROVED vacation → resets status to NEW
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an APPROVED vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, then approve it.
Query: SELECT e.login, e.manager_id FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 10 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation, then PUT /approve/{id} as approver to set APPROVED status
1. Login as the vacation owner (employee)
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Find the APPROVED vacation row
4. Click the pencil icon
5. Verify warning text: 'Changing the vacation dates will move the request to the New status and send it for approval once again.'
6. Change start date to one day later
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify status resets from 'Approved' to 'New'
9. Verify optional approvals reset to ASKED status (DB check)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Status resets to NEW after date edit. Warning was displayed before save. Optional approvals reset to ASKED.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2640 was about this NOT resetting — now fixed. Regression test.

test_cases.title: Cancel NEW vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW vacation.
SETUP: Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations to create.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 3 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Note available days counter value
4. Click the '...' icon on the NEW vacation row
5. In Request Details dialog, click 'Delete' button
6. Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
7. Verify vacation disappears from 'Open' tab
8. Click 'Closed' tab
9. Verify vacation appears with status 'Deleted' or 'Canceled'
10. Verify available days counter increased back to original value
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation moved to Closed tab with terminal status. Available days restored.
test_cases.notes: UI uses 'Delete' button for both cancel and delete actions. Status may show as 'Deleted'.

test_cases.title: Cancel APPROVED vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an APPROVED vacation in the future.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation then approve.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 5 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation (future dates), approve as manager
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Note available days counter
4. Find the APPROVED vacation row
5. Click '...' icon → Request Details
6. Click 'Cancel' or 'Delete' button
7. Confirm in confirmation dialog
8. Verify vacation moves to Closed tab with status 'Canceled'
9. Verify available days counter increases (days returned to pool)
CLEANUP: No cleanup needed — vacation is already canceled
test_cases.expected_result: APPROVED vacation canceled. Days returned to available balance. FIFO redistribution may affect other vacations.
test_cases.notes: canBeCancelled guard: REGULAR + APPROVED + reportPeriod after paymentDate → CANNOT cancel. Future vacations should pass.

test_cases.title: Re-open CANCELED vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a CANCELED vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create, then cancel.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 5 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, then PUT /cancel/{id}
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Closed' tab
4. Find the CANCELED vacation row
5. Click the pencil (edit) icon (if available)
6. In the editing dialog, update dates if needed
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify vacation moves back to 'Open' tab with status 'New'
9. Verify available days decreased again
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: CANCELED vacation re-opened to NEW status. Available days decreased. Vacation appears in Open tab.
test_cases.notes: CANCELED is in FINAL_STATUSES but has explicit CANCELED→NEW transition in the map.

test_cases.title: View Request Details dialog
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with at least one vacation in any status.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation if none exist.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation if needed
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click the '...' icon on any vacation row
4. Verify 'Request details' dialog opens
5. Verify all fields displayed: Period, Number of days, Status, Vacation type, Payment month, Approved by
6. Verify the Close button (X) dismisses the dialog
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id} if created
test_cases.expected_result: Request Details dialog shows all vacation attributes. Period matches table row dates. Close button works.

test_cases.title: Available days counter — per-year breakdown tooltip
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with vacation days across multiple years (current + prior).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days > 0 GROUP BY e.login HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ev.year) > 1 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Locate 'Available vacation days: N in YYYY' counter
4. Click the info (i) icon next to the counter
5. Verify tooltip/popup opens showing per-year breakdown
6. Verify each year shows: year number and available days for that year
7. Verify sum of all years matches the total shown in the counter
test_cases.expected_result: Per-year breakdown popup shows individual year balances that sum to the total displayed.
test_cases.notes: DOM: label and value are sibling divs, not parent-child. Value uses   (U+00A0).

test_cases.title: Vacation events feed
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with recent vacation activity.
SETUP: Via API — create and then delete a vacation to generate timeline events.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation, then delete it (generates 2 timeline events)
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Vacation events feed' button
4. Verify events feed panel opens
5. Verify at least 2 recent events: vacation creation and deletion
6. Verify each event shows: date, event type, description
CLEANUP: No cleanup needed
test_cases.expected_result: Events feed shows chronological vacation activity including creation and status changes.
test_cases.notes: DB table: ttt_vacation.timeline tracks all events. Event types: VACATION_CREATED, VACATION_DELETED, etc.

test_cases.title: Delete PAID+NON-EXACT vacation (allowed)
test_cases.preconditions:
This is an edge case — PAID+NON_EXACT vacations can be deleted. Requires a PAID vacation with periodType != EXACT.
Note: Difficult to set up without direct DB manipulation. Consider API-only verification.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: This test requires a PAID vacation with non-EXACT periodType. Use test API or DB if available.
1. Via API — attempt DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id} on the PAID+NON-EXACT vacation
2. Verify deletion succeeds (HTTP 200)
3. Verify vacation status changes to DELETED in DB
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: PAID+NON_EXACT vacation can be deleted — this is a design issue. PAID should be terminal but the guard only blocks PAID+EXACT.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: deleteVacation only guards PAID+EXACT. PAID+NON_EXACT passes through.

test_cases.title: Soft delete — record persists in DB
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation, note the vacation ID
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Delete the vacation via Request Details dialog
4. Verify vacation disappears from 'Open' tab
5. Click 'Closed' tab — verify vacation appears with status 'Deleted'
DB-CHECK: SELECT id, status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify status = 'DELETED' (not physically removed)
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation is soft-deleted: status set to DELETED in DB, still visible in Closed tab. Record is not physically removed.
test_cases.notes: Soft delete with status=DELETED. GET API still returns the record. Crossing check counts DELETED records (design issue).

test_cases.title: Approve NEW vacation — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a NEW vacation, logged in as the assigned approver (manager).
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation for the employee.
Query: SELECT e.login AS employee, m.login AS manager FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND m.enabled = true AND e.id != m.id ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation for the employee
1. Login as the manager (approver)
2. Navigate to Employee Requests page (/vacation/request)
3. Click 'Vacation requests' tab
4. Click 'Approval' sub-filter
5. Find the employee's vacation request in the table
6. Click the approve (checkmark) button on the row
7. Verify success notification
8. Verify the row disappears from the Approval list (or status changes to Approved)
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify status = 'APPROVED'
CLEANUP: Via API — PUT /cancel/{id} then DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation status changes to APPROVED. Row disappears from approval queue. DB confirms APPROVED status.
test_cases.notes: Approve adjusts paymentDate if before approvePeriodStartDate.

test_cases.title: Reject NEW vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with a pending approval request.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation for the employee.
Query: (same as TC-VAC-015)
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation for the employee
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to /vacation/request
3. Click 'Approval' sub-filter
4. Find the employee's vacation
5. Click the reject (X) button
6. Verify rejection confirmation or success
7. Verify row disappears from Approval list
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify 'REJECTED'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation rejected. Status changes to REJECTED. Days returned to employee's available balance.
test_cases.notes: Reject uses VACATIONS_DELETE permission — same as cancel and delete.

test_cases.title: Reject APPROVED vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with an APPROVED vacation assigned to them.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, then approve it.
Query: (same as TC-VAC-015)
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, approve it
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to /vacation/request
3. Find the APPROVED vacation (may need 'My department' or search filter)
4. Verify reject button is available for APPROVED status
5. Click the reject button
6. Verify status changes to REJECTED
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify 'REJECTED'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
test_cases.expected_result: APPROVED vacation can be rejected. Status transitions to REJECTED. Days returned to pool, FIFO redistribution triggers.
test_cases.notes: REJECTABLE_STATUSES = {NEW, APPROVED}. canBeCancelled guard also applies.

test_cases.title: Re-approve REJECTED vacation (without edit)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with a REJECTED vacation assigned to them.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, reject it.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, then reject it via PUT /reject/{id}
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to /vacation/request
3. Find the REJECTED vacation
4. Verify approve button is available
5. Click approve
6. Verify status changes to APPROVED
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify 'APPROVED'
CLEANUP: Via API — PUT /cancel/{id} then DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: REJECTED vacation can be directly re-approved without the employee editing it. State machine allows REJECTED→APPROVED.
test_cases.notes: APPROVABLE_STATUSES = {NEW, REJECTED}. No edit required between reject and re-approve.

test_cases.title: CPO self-approval on create
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER (CPO role) and a manager assigned.
Query: SELECT e.login, m.login AS manager FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id WHERE be.login = e.login AND r.name = 'ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER') ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the CPO employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select future dates
5. Verify 'Approved by' field shows the employee themselves (self-approval)
6. Verify 'Agreed by' shows their manager
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify vacation created with status 'Approved' (auto-approved on create for CPO)
DB-CHECK: SELECT v.approver_id, v.employee_id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v WHERE v.id = <id> — verify approver_id = employee_id
DB-CHECK: SELECT va.status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation_approval va WHERE va.vacation = <id> — verify manager has ASKED status
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: CPO vacation is self-approved on create. Manager added as optional approver with ASKED status. Status is NEW (not auto-APPROVED — requires explicit approve action).
test_cases.notes: CPO = ROLE_DEPARTMENT_MANAGER. pvaynmaster confirmed as CPO on qa-1. Status is NEW, not auto-APPROVED.

test_cases.title: Change approver (redirect request)
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with a NEW vacation request to approve.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation.
A second manager must exist to redirect to.
Query: SELECT e.login AS employee, m.login AS manager, m2.login AS alt_manager FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m ON e.manager_id = m.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee m2 ON m2.enabled = true AND m2.id != m.id AND m2.id != e.id WHERE e.enabled = true AND m.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation for the employee
1. Login as the current approver (manager)
2. Navigate to /vacation/request
3. Find the vacation request
4. Click the redirect (arrow) button
5. In the redirect dialog, search and select the alternative manager
6. Confirm redirect
7. Verify the request disappears from current approver's queue
DB-CHECK: SELECT approver_id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify changed to alt_manager's ID
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Approver changed. Old approver becomes optional (ASKED). New approver sees the request in their queue.
test_cases.notes: Bug #2718 (OPEN): Redirected approved/rejected request doesn't reset status to NEW.

test_cases.title: Optional approver — approve
test_cases.preconditions:
Vacation with an optional approver in ASKED status.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation with optionalApprovers=[login].
Query: SELECT e.login AS employee, oa.login AS optional FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee oa ON oa.enabled = true AND oa.id != e.id AND oa.id != e.manager_id WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation with optionalApprovers parameter including the optional approver's login
1. Login as the optional approver
2. Navigate to /vacation/request
3. Click 'Agreement' sub-filter
4. Find the vacation request
5. Click approve (checkmark)
6. Verify optional approval status changes
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation_approval WHERE vacation = <id> AND employee = <oa_employee_id> — verify 'APPROVED'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Optional approver's status changes from ASKED to APPROVED. Vacation overall status remains unchanged (optional approval doesn't drive state machine).
test_cases.notes: Optional approval is informational — doesn't change vacation status. Only primary approver drives transitions.

test_cases.title: Approval resets on date edit
test_cases.preconditions:
Vacation with optional approvals that have been acted on.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation with optionalApprovers, then approve optionally.
Query: (same as TC-VAC-021)
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation with optionalApprovers, have the optional approver approve
1. Login as the vacation owner
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Edit the vacation dates (change end date)
4. Click 'Save'
5. Verify dates updated
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation_approval WHERE vacation = <id> — verify all reset to 'ASKED'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the vacation
test_cases.expected_result: All optional approvals reset to ASKED status after date edit. Primary approver remains unchanged.

test_cases.title: Employee Requests page — view pending approvals
test_cases.preconditions:
Manager with at least one pending vacation request.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation for an employee managed by this manager.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation for a managed employee
1. Login as the manager
2. Navigate to /vacation/request
3. Verify 'Vacation requests (N)' tab shows count >= 1
4. Click 'Approval' sub-filter
5. Verify table shows: Employee, Vacation dates, Vacation type, Manager, Approved by, Agreed by, Payment month, Status, Actions
6. Verify the created vacation appears in the list
7. Verify action buttons: approve (checkmark), reject (X), redirect (arrow), details (eye)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Employee Requests page displays pending approvals with all columns and action buttons.
test_cases.notes: Multiple rows may exist for same employee from previous test runs. Filter by employee + period.

test_cases.title: No-manager employee — self-approval
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with no manager assigned (manager_id IS NULL).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE e.manager_id IS NULL AND e.enabled = true AND ev.available_vacation_days >= 3 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the no-manager employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select future dates
5. Verify 'Approved by' shows the employee themselves
6. Click 'Save'
7. Verify vacation created
DB-CHECK: SELECT approver_id, employee_id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — verify approver_id = employee_id
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Employee with no manager self-approves. Approver set to themselves.
test_cases.notes: Rare case — most employees have managers. May not find a test user.

test_cases.title: Pay APPROVED REGULAR vacation — happy path
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant user. Employee with APPROVED REGULAR vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, approve it.
Query accountant: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.login = be.login WHERE r.name IN ('ROLE_ACCOUNTANT', 'ROLE_CHIEF_ACCOUNTANT') AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a 5-day REGULAR vacation for an employee, approve it
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to Accounting section (dropdown → vacation payments or similar)
3. Find the APPROVED vacation in the payment queue
4. Enter regular days paid = 5, administrative days paid = 0
5. Confirm payment
6. Verify vacation status changes to 'Paid'
DB-CHECK: SELECT vp.regular_days, vp.administrative_days, vp.payed_at FROM ttt_vacation.vacation v JOIN ttt_vacation.vacation_payment vp ON v.vacation_payment_id = vp.id WHERE v.id = <id>
Note: PAID+EXACT vacations cannot be deleted — permanent record in test env
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation paid. Status = PAID. vacation_payment record created with correct day split. Payment date recorded.
test_cases.notes: PAID+EXACT is terminal and undeletable. Creates permanent data in test environment.

test_cases.title: Pay ADMINISTRATIVE vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant. Employee with APPROVED ADMINISTRATIVE vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create ADMINISTRATIVE vacation (paymentType=ADMINISTRATIVE), approve it.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create ADMINISTRATIVE vacation, approve
1. Login as accountant
2. Find the APPROVED ADMINISTRATIVE vacation
3. Pay with regularDaysPayed=0, administrativeDaysPayed=N (matching total days)
4. Verify status changes to PAID
DB-CHECK: Verify vacation_payment.administrative_days = N, regular_days = 0
test_cases.expected_result: ADMINISTRATIVE vacation paid with all days as administrative. No impact on paid vacation balance.

test_cases.title: Payment validation — wrong day sum rejected
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation with known day count.
SETUP: Via API — create 5-day vacation, approve.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create 5-day vacation, approve
1. Via API — PUT /pay/{id} with body: {regularDaysPayed: 3, administrativeDaysPayed: 0} (sum=3, expected=5)
2. Verify HTTP 400 response
3. Verify errorCode: 'exception.vacation.pay.days.not.equal'
4. Verify vacation status remains APPROVED
CLEANUP: Via API — cancel and delete
test_cases.expected_result: Payment rejected with 400. Error code indicates day sum mismatch. Status unchanged.
test_cases.notes: Sum validation: regularDaysPayed + administrativeDaysPayed must equal vacation.days.

test_cases.title: Cannot pay NEW vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
NEW vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation (status remains NEW).
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation
1. Via API — PUT /pay/{id} with body: {regularDaysPayed: 5, administrativeDaysPayed: 0}
2. Verify HTTP 400 response
3. Verify error indicates status must be APPROVED
4. Verify vacation status remains NEW
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Payment blocked for non-APPROVED status. PayVacationServiceImpl.checkForPayment validates APPROVED + EXACT.

test_cases.title: PAID vacation — terminal state, no further transitions
test_cases.preconditions:
PAID vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create, approve, pay.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, approve, pay
1. Via API — PUT /cancel/{id} — verify HTTP 400
2. Via API — PUT /reject/{id} — verify HTTP 400
3. Via API — PUT update with new dates — verify HTTP 400
4. Via API — DELETE /{id} — verify HTTP 400 (for PAID+EXACT)
5. Verify all attempts return errorCode: 'exception.vacation.status.notAllowed' or 'exception.vacation.delete.notAllowed'
Note: PAID+EXACT vacation remains as permanent record
test_cases.expected_result: PAID is terminal. Cancel, reject, edit, and delete all blocked. Error codes confirm denial.
test_cases.notes: NON_EDITABLE_STATUSES = {CANCELED, PAID}. Permission service returns empty set for PAID.

test_cases.title: Delete PAID+EXACT blocked
test_cases.preconditions:
PAID+EXACT vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create, approve, pay (creates EXACT period).
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, approve, pay
1. Via API — DELETE /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id}
2. Verify HTTP 400
3. Verify errorCode: 'exception.vacation.delete.notAllowed'
4. Verify vacation still exists with PAID status
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Deletion blocked for PAID+EXACT. ServiceException with delete.notAllowed error code.
test_cases.notes: Guard: status==PAID && periodType==EXACT → throw.

test_cases.title: Payment month validation — closed period blocked
test_cases.preconditions:
Vacation with payment month in a closed accounting period.
Note: Requires knowledge of current open/closed periods on the test env.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select future dates
5. Attempt to select a past/closed month for payment
6. Verify the month picker restricts selection to open periods
7. Or verify error on save if closed month is somehow selected
test_cases.expected_result: Payment month picker prevents selection of closed accounting periods. Backend validates: paymentDate must be within approval period range.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3379 (fixed): was using Report period instead of Approval period.

test_cases.title: Auto-pay expired APPROVED vacations (cron)
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation older than 2 months (requires timemachine or clock manipulation).
SETUP: Via test API — PATCH clock to advance 3 months, or find naturally expired vacation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via test API — create vacation in the past (via timemachine clock), approve it, advance clock 3 months
1. Via test API — POST /api/vacation/v1/test/vacations/pay-expired-approved
2. Verify the cron-like endpoint processes expired vacations
3. Verify vacation status changes to PAID
DB-CHECK: SELECT status, vacation_payment_id FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Auto-pay processes APPROVED vacations >2 months old. Status changes to PAID with auto-distributed day types.
test_cases.notes: Test endpoint: POST /api/vacation/v1/test/vacations/pay-expired-approved. Needs timemachine env.

test_cases.title: Error 500 on AV=true negative balance payment (#3363)
test_cases.preconditions:
AV=true employee with negative vacation balance. APPROVED vacation.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = true AND e.enabled = true AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev WHERE ev.employee = e.id AND ev.available_vacation_days < 0) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create and approve a vacation for the AV=true employee with negative balance
1. Via API — PUT /pay/{id} with correct day split
2. Verify HTTP 500 response (known bug)
3. Verify vacation status remains APPROVED (payment failed)
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id> — should still be APPROVED
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3363 (OPEN): HTTP 500 on payment when AV=true balance is negative. No validation — unhandled NPE/exception.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug. Regression test — should catch if the 500 changes to a proper validation error.

test_cases.title: Start date in past — rejected
test_cases.preconditions:
Any employee.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Attempt to select yesterday as start date
5. Verify date picker prevents past date selection OR
6. If date can be entered, click 'Save'
7. Verify error message: validation.vacation.start.date.in.past (may show as raw key — no i18n)
CLEANUP: No cleanup — creation should fail
test_cases.expected_result: Past start date rejected. Error shown. Boundary: today is accepted, yesterday is rejected (isBefore(today)).
test_cases.notes: Missing i18n: error key displayed as raw string. Boundary at today (not tomorrow).

test_cases.title: Start date > end date — rejected
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Set start date = next Friday, end date = next Monday (before start)
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify error: validation.vacation.dates.order
test_cases.expected_result: Date order validation rejects startDate > endDate. Error code: validation.vacation.dates.order.
test_cases.notes: Non-short-circuiting: both past-date and order errors returned simultaneously if both fail.

test_cases.title: Insufficient available days — REGULAR blocked
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with known available days < requested duration.
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days BETWEEN 1 AND 5 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select dates spanning more working days than available (e.g., if 3 days available, select 2-week period)
5. Verify red error message appears indicating insufficient days
6. Verify 'Save' button is disabled or click 'Save' to get backend error
7. Verify error code: validation.vacation.duration
test_cases.expected_result: Insufficient days blocks creation. Frontend shows red error. Backend returns validation.vacation.duration if submitted.
test_cases.notes: AV=false: red error, block submit. AV=true: Error 11.4. Frontend validation fires dynamically on field change.

test_cases.title: Overlapping vacation — crossing check
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an existing vacation in specific date range.
SETUP: Via API — create vacation for specific dates.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days >= 15 AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a REGULAR vacation from Monday to Friday (week 1)
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select dates that overlap with the existing vacation (e.g., Wednesday to next Wednesday)
5. Verify crossing validation fires — error shown
6. Verify error code: exception.validation.vacation.dates.crossing
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE both vacations
test_cases.expected_result: Overlapping vacation blocked. Error: exception.validation.vacation.dates.crossing. Three overlap patterns: start-in-range, end-in-range, enclosing.
test_cases.notes: IMPORTANT: Crossing check counts DELETED records too (ghost conflicts). Soft-deleted vacations block date ranges permanently.

test_cases.title: Weekend-only vacation (0 working days) — rejected
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select Saturday to Sunday (2 calendar days, 0 working days)
5. Verify 'Number of days' shows 0
6. Click 'Save'
7. Verify error: validation.vacation.duration (minimum is 1 working day)
test_cases.expected_result: Vacation with 0 working days rejected. minimalVacationDuration=1 in config. Only Sat-Sun or holiday-only ranges produce 0.
test_cases.notes: Config: vacation.minimal-vacation-duration: 1 (not 5 as Javadoc says). Counts working days only.

test_cases.title: Next year not available before Feb 1
test_cases.preconditions: Test requires current date to be before Feb 1 (use timemachine to set clock to January).
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via test API — set clock to January 15 of current year (timemachine env)
1. Login as employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Attempt to select a start date in the next year
5. Verify error: validation.vacation.next.year.not.available
CLEANUP: Via test API — reset clock
test_cases.expected_result: Before Feb 1, next-year vacation creation is blocked. After Feb 1, it's allowed.
test_cases.notes: Needs timemachine env with clock set to January. Boundary: Jan 31 rejected, Feb 1 allowed.

test_cases.title: First 3 months restriction — new employee (#3014)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee hired within last 3 months.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.first_date > CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the recently-hired employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Verify dates within first 3 months of employment are disabled in the calendar picker
5. Verify calendar opens to first available (non-disabled) date
6. Select a date after the 3-month restriction period
7. Verify creation succeeds for dates after restriction
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: First 3 months of employment dates are disabled for REGULAR vacations. ADMINISTRATIVE vacations are NOT restricted.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3014-30: calculation was incorrect for employees hired before July 2024. Column: employee.first_date.

test_cases.title: First 3 months — ADMINISTRATIVE not restricted

test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the recently-hired employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Check 'Unpaid vacation' checkbox (ADMINISTRATIVE)
5. Select dates within the first 3 months of employment
6. Verify dates are NOT disabled (no restriction for ADMINISTRATIVE)
7. Click 'Save'
8. Verify vacation created successfully
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: ADMINISTRATIVE vacations bypass the 3-month restriction. Employee can take unpaid leave immediately.

test_cases.title: Payment month range — 2 months before to end month
test_cases.preconditions: Employee creating a vacation with specific month range.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select dates starting 3 months from now
5. Open payment month picker
6. Verify allowed range: from 2 months before start date to the month of the end date
7. Attempt to select a month outside this range
8. Verify it's either disabled or rejected on save
test_cases.expected_result: Payment month restricted to valid range. Backend: correctPaymentMonth() adjusts if needed, isPaymentDateCorrect() validates.
test_cases.notes: Error code: validation.vacation.dates.payment.

test_cases.title: Null paymentMonth → server error (NPE bug)
test_cases.preconditions: Any employee.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations with valid fields but paymentMonth: null
2. Verify HTTP 500 response
3. Verify error is an NPE (no @NotNull annotation on paymentMonth field)
DB-CHECK: Verify no vacation was created
test_cases.expected_result: Bug: null paymentMonth causes HTTP 500 (NPE). No DTO-level validation exists for this field.
test_cases.notes: Confirmed active on qa-1. NPE in correctPaymentMonth().

test_cases.title: Dynamic validation — messages update on field change
test_cases.preconditions: Employee with limited available days.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select dates exceeding available days → verify red error appears
5. Change to shorter dates within available days → verify red error disappears
6. Change back to long dates → verify red error reappears
7. Check 'Unpaid vacation' → verify error disappears (ADMINISTRATIVE skips balance check)
test_cases.expected_result: Validation messages update dynamically on field change (not just on Save click). Exception: 'required field' validation only on Save.

test_cases.title: Accrued days validation — future request auto-conversion (#3015)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with multiple future REGULAR vacations consuming most available days.
Note: Complex setup — requires creating 2+ vacations that together exhaust the balance.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create 2 REGULAR vacations: one in month M, one in month M+2, together consuming ~90% of available days
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Create a 3rd vacation for month M+1 that would push total over available
5. Click 'Save'
6. Verify either: error for insufficient days, OR auto-conversion of a future request to Administrative
7. Check the vacation list for any automatically converted vacations
8. Verify orange warning appears about accrued days impact on future requests
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all created vacations
test_cases.expected_result: Accrued days validation checks all future requests. May auto-convert earliest-deficit request to Administrative. Orange warning shown.
test_cases.notes: 31 sub-bugs in #3015. Conversion order: chronological by payment month, select request with minimum X value.

test_cases.title: Holiday impact on working days count
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in an office with a public holiday on a weekday.
Query: SELECT e.login, cd.event_date FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_calendar.calendar c ON c.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_calendar.calendar_day cd ON cd.calendar_id = c.id WHERE cd.event_date > CURRENT_DATE AND cd.duration = 0 AND EXTRACT(DOW FROM cd.event_date) BETWEEN 1 AND 5 AND c.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true AND e.manager_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY cd.event_date LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select a Mon-Fri range that includes the public holiday
5. Verify 'Number of days' shows 4 (not 5) for a 5-calendar-day range with 1 holiday
6. Verify the calculation uses the office-specific production calendar
CLEANUP: Cancel dialog without saving
test_cases.expected_result: Working days count excludes public holidays from the employee's office calendar. Mon-Fri with 1 holiday = 4 working days.
test_cases.notes: Different offices may have different holiday calendars. Day count is per-office.

Employee with both REGULAR and ADMINISTRATIVE vacations.

test_cases.title: Column filter — Status: Approved only
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with vacations in NEW and APPROVED statuses.
SETUP: Via API — create 2 vacations, approve one.
test_cases.steps:

5. Verify only APPROVED vacation shown
6. Verify NEW vacation hidden
7. Press Escape
CLEANUP: Via API — cancel approved, delete both
test_cases.expected_result: Status filter correctly shows only Approved vacations.
test_cases.notes: Status checkboxes: All, New, Approved, Rejected, Paid, Finished, Deleted.

4. Verify rows ordered by start date earliest first
5. Click again to sort descending
6. Verify rows ordered latest first
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all 3
test_cases.expected_result: Sorting by Vacation dates works in both ascending and descending order.

test_cases.title: Table footer — Total row sums

Employee with 2+ vacations showing in current tab.

5. Verify Total Administrative days = sum of individual Administrative days columns
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE both
test_cases.expected_result: Footer Total row correctly sums Regular days and Administrative days across all visible rows.
test_cases.notes: Total row is in <tfoot>, separate from data rows in <tbody>.

test_cases.title: Availability chart — vacation display

Employee with APPROVED vacation.

SETUP: Via API — create and approve a vacation

3. Search for the employee by name
4. Navigate to the month containing the vacation
5. Verify green bar appears for the APPROVED vacation dates
6. Verify weekend columns have yellow background
CLEANUP: Via API — cancel and delete the vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Availability chart shows APPROVED vacations as green bars. Days-off/holidays shown in blue.
test_cases.notes: Chart has Day/Month view toggle and timeline navigation.

test_cases.title: Employees Vacation Days page — search by name

4. Verify results filter to matching employees
5. Verify each result shows: Employee (link), Vacation days, Pending approval
6. Click employee link — verify navigation to their vacation detail
test_cases.expected_result: Search filters employees by name. Results show vacation day balances and pending approval counts.

test_cases.title: Latin name search bug (#3297)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with Latin first/last name in DB.
Query: SELECT e.login, e.latin_first_name, e.latin_last_name FROM ttt_backend.employee e WHERE e.latin_first_name IS NOT NULL AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to /vacation/vacation-days
3. In search box, type the employee's Latin last name
4. Verify: does the employee appear in results?
5. If not found — bug #3297 confirmed (Latin name search broken)
6. Try Cyrillic name for the same employee — verify it works

test_cases.notes: DB columns: latin_first_name, latin_last_name (NOT first_name, last_name).

test_cases.title: AV=true — full year balance available from Jan 1
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=true office (e.g., Cyprus, Germany).
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.available_vacation_days, ev.year FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE o.advance_vacation = true AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the AV=true employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Check 'Available vacation days' counter
4. Verify the count reflects full annual norm (e.g., 24 days) even early in the year
5. Click info icon for per-year breakdown
6. Verify current year shows full allocation, not prorated
DB-CHECK: SELECT available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = <id> AND year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
test_cases.expected_result: AV=true employees see full year balance from January. Not monthly-prorated like AV=false.
test_cases.notes: Formula: available = currentYearDays + pastYearDays + futureDays + editedDays.

test_cases.title: AV=true — negative balance allowed for current year
test_cases.preconditions:
AV=true employee with enough balance to demonstrate negative scenario. Needs to create vacations consuming more than available.
Note: Complex test — requires consuming entire balance plus more.
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as AV=true employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Check current available days (e.g., 24)
4. Create multiple vacations consuming all days
5. Attempt to create one more vacation (would go negative)
6. Verify: system allows creation (AV=true permits negative for current year)
7. Verify available days counter shows negative value
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE all created vacations
test_cases.expected_result: AV=true offices allow negative balance for current year. Counter can show negative values.
test_cases.notes: AV=false: NEVER negative (display 0). AV=true: negative allowed for current year.

test_cases.title: AV=false — monthly accrual, no negative
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee in AV=false office (Russia).
Query: SELECT e.login, ev.available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE o.advance_vacation = false AND ev.year = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) AND ev.available_vacation_days > 0 AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the AV=false employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Check 'Available vacation days'
4. Verify the count is proportional to months elapsed (roughly month/12 * annual_norm)
5. Verify the counter never shows a negative value
6. Attempt to create a vacation exceeding available days
7. Verify error blocks creation
test_cases.expected_result: AV=false employee balance is monthly accrual. Counter never negative. Excess creation blocked.
test_cases.notes: Formula: available = (month × norm/12) + yearRemainder + priorYears − norm + futureDays + editedDays.

test_cases.title: FIFO day consumption — earliest year first
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with vacation days from multiple years (current + prior).
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE ev.available_vacation_days > 0 AND e.enabled = true GROUP BY e.login HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ev.year) > 1 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click info icon on available days — note per-year breakdown
4. Create a REGULAR vacation consuming some days
5. After creation, click info icon again
6. Verify earliest year's balance decreased first (FIFO order)
DB-CHECK: SELECT year, available_vacation_days FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = <id> ORDER BY year
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: FIFO consumption: days consumed from earliest year first. Per-year breakdown reflects this.
test_cases.notes: Cross-year splits tracked in vacation_days_distribution (vacation_id + year → days).

test_cases.title: FIFO redistribution on cancel — days returned
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with an APPROVED vacation consuming days from multiple years.
SETUP: Via API — create and approve a vacation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a long vacation consuming days from 2+ years, approve it
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click info icon — note per-year breakdown (reduced by vacation)
4. Cancel the vacation
5. Click info icon again
6. Verify days returned to the correct years (FIFO redistribution)
DB-CHECK: Compare employee_vacation.available_vacation_days before and after cancel
test_cases.expected_result: On cancel, days redistributed back. VacationRecalculationService returns all days then re-distributes among remaining vacations.
test_cases.notes: Recalculation: returns ALL regular+exact days, then re-distributes. May convert if insufficient.

test_cases.title: AV=true multi-year balance distribution (#3361)
test_cases.preconditions:
AV=true employee with vacation days across 3+ years.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev ON e.id = ev.employee WHERE o.advance_vacation = true AND e.enabled = true GROUP BY e.login HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ev.year) >= 3 ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click info icon for per-year breakdown
4. Verify all years with remaining balance are shown
5. Attempt to create a vacation consuming days from current year
6. Verify: does creation succeed? (Bug #3361: multi-year distribution may block creation)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE if created
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3361 (OPEN): AV=True multi-year balance distribution blocks current-year creation even when overall balance is sufficient.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug. Regression test.

test_cases.title: Day correction — AV=false prohibits negative
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant or admin user. AV=false employee.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e JOIN ttt_vacation.office o ON e.office_id = o.id WHERE o.advance_vacation = false AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as accountant/admin
2. Navigate to Employees Vacation Days page
3. Find the AV=false employee
4. Attempt to manually set vacation days to a negative value
5. Verify: correction is blocked (value must be >= 0 for AV=false)
test_cases.expected_result: AV=false: manual corrections floor at >= 0. Negative values prohibited in inline editing.
test_cases.notes: Bug #3283: auto corrections can go negative even for AV=false. Manual edits correctly floor at 0.

test_cases.title: Create vacation → notification to approver
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a manager. Email service accessible.

5. Verify email sent to manager about new vacation request
6. Verify email contains: employee name, vacation dates, vacation type
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Manager receives email notification about new vacation request with details.
test_cases.notes: Email templates catalog: 50+ templates across 10 categories.

NEW vacation. Email service accessible.
SETUP: Create vacation, clear employee's email.

2. Approve the vacation via Employee Requests page
3. Wait 30 seconds
4. Via API — check employee's email inbox
5. Verify approval notification received
6. Verify email contains: vacation dates, approved status, approver name
CLEANUP: Via API — cancel and delete
test_cases.expected_result: Employee receives email notification about vacation approval.

test_cases.title: Reject vacation → notification to employee

test_cases.steps:

2. Reject the vacation
3. Via API — check employee's email
4. Verify rejection notification received
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Employee receives rejection notification with approver name and vacation details.

4. Verify cancellation notification sent to approver
CLEANUP: None needed

4. Verify notification sent to the also-notify recipient
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Colleagues in 'Also notify' list receive email about the vacation.

test_cases.title: Wrong payment month in notification (#2925)
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation with specific payment month.
test_cases.steps:

1. Login as employee, create vacation
2. Via API — check notification email
3. Verify payment month in email matches the vacation's actual payment month
4. If mismatched — bug #2925 confirmed
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #2925 (OPEN): Wrong payment month displayed in email notification.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug regression test.

test_cases.title: Notification on auto-conversion to ADMINISTRATIVE (#3015)

CLEANUP: DELETE all vacations

test_cases.notes: Bug #3015-30: silent conversion (no notification) was fixed.

test_cases.title: Regression: Overlapping vacations not blocked by frontend (#3240)

Employee with an existing vacation.

test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select dates that overlap with the existing vacation
5. Verify frontend shows overlap error BEFORE clicking Save
6. Verify the calendar picker or form indicates the conflict
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE the first vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Frontend detects overlapping dates and shows error. Bug #3240 (fixed): previously frontend did not block overlapping vacations.
test_cases.notes: 3-way overlap check: start-in-range, end-in-range, enclosing.

test_cases.title: Regression: Payment month not updated in edit modal (#2705)

1. Login as the employee
2. Click edit on the vacation
3. Change dates to a different month range
4. Verify payment month auto-updates to reflect new dates
5. Click Save
6. Re-open edit dialog — verify payment month persisted correctly
test_cases.expected_result: Payment month updates when dates change in edit dialog. Bug #2705 (fixed): previously payment month didn't update after save.

test_cases.title: Regression: Edit own vacation shows 0 available (#3014-21)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a vacation consuming most of their balance.
SETUP: Via API — create a large vacation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation consuming 80% of available days
1. Login as the employee
2. Click edit on the vacation
3. Verify available days shown in edit dialog EXCLUDE the current vacation's days from the consumed total
4. Verify the available counter shows original balance minus OTHER vacations only
5. Verify Save button is enabled (not disabled due to false 0-available calculation)
test_cases.expected_result: Edit dialog correctly excludes the current vacation's days from consumed total. Bug #3014-21 (fixed): previously showed 0 available.
test_cases.notes: VacationUpdateValidator adjusts limitations: adds back current vacation's consumed days.

test_cases.title: Regression: Redirected request status not reset (#2718)
test_cases.preconditions:
APPROVED vacation with a specific approver.
SETUP: Via API — create, approve.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation, approve it
1. Login as the approver
2. Redirect the APPROVED vacation to another manager
3. Verify: does the status reset to NEW? (Expected per business rules)
4. If status remains APPROVED — bug #2718 confirmed (OPEN)
DB-CHECK: SELECT status FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE id = <id>
CLEANUP: Via API — cancel and delete
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #2718 (OPEN): Redirected approved/rejected request doesn't reset status to NEW. Status should reset when approver changes.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug. 21% of vacations get redirected — high-impact issue.

test_cases.title: Regression: Double accrual on salary office change (#2789)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee who has recently changed salary office.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.enabled = true AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation ev WHERE ev.employee = e.id GROUP BY ev.employee HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT year) FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = e.id)) ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Via DB — check employee_vacation records for the employee
2. Verify no duplicate year entries (same employee + same year appearing twice)
3. If duplicates found — bug #2789 active
4. Calculate total available days and compare with expected (annual_norm * years_worked)
DB-CHECK: SELECT employee, year, COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = <id> GROUP BY employee, year HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #2789 (OPEN): Double accrual on salary office change creates duplicate year entries.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug. Data integrity issue.

test_cases.title: Regression: last_date not updated during CS sync (#3374)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with last_date set (dismissed or leaving).
Query: SELECT e.login, e.last_date FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.last_date IS NOT NULL ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — trigger CS sync: POST /api/ttt/test/v1/employees/sync
2. Wait for sync completion
3. Via DB — check if last_date values match CompanyStaff source
DB-CHECK: SELECT login, last_date, enabled FROM ttt_vacation.employee WHERE last_date IS NOT NULL
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3374 (OPEN): last_date not updated during CS sync — TTT may miss dismissal dates.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug. Affects vacation availability calculations for departing employees.

test_cases.title: Regression: Maternity leave overlap — days not returned (#3352)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee on maternity leave with existing vacations.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.maternity_leave = true AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Find employee on maternity leave
2. Check their vacation day balance
3. Verify days from overlapping periods are correctly handled
4. If balance is incorrect (days not returned) — bug #3352 confirmed
DB-CHECK: SELECT available_vacation_days, year FROM ttt_vacation.employee_vacation WHERE employee = <id>
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3352 (OPEN): Maternity leave overlap → vacation days not returned to balance.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug. Related to #3370 (maternity user can't edit vacation).

test_cases.title: Regression: Maternity leave user can't edit vacation (#3370)
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee on maternity leave with available vacation days = 0.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.maternity_leave = true AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
1. Login as the maternity leave employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Verify available days shows 0
4. Attempt to create or edit a vacation
5. Verify: is the action blocked? (Bug #3370 expected)
CLEANUP: None
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3370 (OPEN): Maternity leave user shows 0 available days and can't edit existing vacations.
test_cases.notes: OPEN bug. Related to #3352.

test_cases.title: Regression: Ghost conflicts from soft-deleted vacations
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with a deleted vacation at specific dates.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation, delete it.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create vacation for Week X dates, then DELETE it
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Click 'Create a request'
4. Select the SAME dates as the deleted vacation (Week X)
5. Verify: does crossing validation block the creation?
6. If blocked — ghost conflict confirmed (DELETED records counted in crossing check)
CLEANUP: None needed
test_cases.expected_result: Design issue: crossing check includes DELETED records. Soft-deleted vacations create permanent ghost conflicts that block future creates at those dates.
test_cases.notes: DELETED should be excluded from crossing validation. Every test run that creates+deletes a vacation permanently blocks that date range.

test_cases.title: Regression: Approver field missing from API (#3329)

test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — GET /api/vacation/v1/vacations (list endpoint)
2. Check each vacation in the response
3. Verify all vacations have 'approver' field populated
4. If any have null/missing approver — bug #3329
DB-CHECK: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ttt_vacation.vacation WHERE approver_id IS NULL AND status NOT IN ('DELETED', 'CANCELED')
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3329 (fixed as hotfix): Approver field was intermittently missing from API response. Verify fix is stable.
test_cases.notes: Hotfix applied. Regression test to ensure it doesn't recur.

test_cases.title: Regression: Flash of irrelevant validation on first date pick (#3127)

4. Click on the start date field for the very first time

test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3127 (closed): Flash of validation message should not appear on first date selection.
test_cases.notes: Low severity but annoying UX issue.

test_cases.title: Regression: Russian messages in English events feed (#3344)

6. If any Russian text appears — bug #3344 not fully fixed
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3344 (closed): Events feed should show English messages when UI is set to English.

test_cases.title: Regression: Null optionalApprovers → NPE on CPO path

test_cases.steps:

2. Verify HTTP 500 (NPE)
3. The CPO create path calls request.getOptionalApprovers().add(manager) which NPEs if null
DB-CHECK: Verify no vacation was created

test_cases.notes: Confirmed active on qa-1. No @NotNull on optionalApprovers field.

test_cases.title: Regression: Calendar change converts ALL vacations (#3338)

test_cases.steps:
1. Find employee with multiple approved vacations
2. Admin modifies production calendar (add a holiday within one vacation's dates)
3. Verify: does only the affected vacation get checked for conversion?
4. Or does every vacation for the employee get reconverted? (Bug #3338)
DB-CHECK: Check status changes across all employee's vacations after calendar change
test_cases.expected_result: Bug #3338 (closed): Calendar change should only affect the vacation containing the changed date, not all vacations.
test_cases.notes: Regression test for calendar-vacation interaction.

test_cases.title: Owner can edit own vacation (not PAID)

Employee with own NEW vacation.

SETUP: Via API — create a vacation

4. Click edit
5. Change end date

CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE

test_cases.notes: Permission EDIT: Owner, status not PAID (always).

test_cases.title: Owner cannot edit PAID vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
PAID vacation.

SETUP: Via API — create, approve, and pay a vacation

2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Find the PAID vacation in 'Closed' tab
4. Verify: edit (pencil) icon is NOT visible

Note: PAID+EXACT creates permanent record
test_cases.expected_result: PAID vacation cannot be edited. No edit icon shown. API returns 400 on update attempt.
test_cases.notes: NON_EDITABLE_STATUSES = {CANCELED, PAID}.

test_cases.title: Non-approver cannot approve vacation
test_cases.preconditions: Vacation assigned to Manager A. Login as Manager B (different person).

SETUP: Via API — create a vacation assigned to Manager A
1. Login as Manager B (NOT the assigned approver)
2. Navigate to /vacation/request
3. Verify the vacation does NOT appear in Manager B's approval queue
4. Via API — PUT /approve/{id} as Manager B → verify HTTP 400 (exception.vacation.status.notAllowed)
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Non-assigned managers cannot approve. hasAccess() checks approver_id match. 400 error returned.
test_cases.notes: MANAGER_ROLES = {PM, DM, CACC}. ROLE_ADMIN is NOT in MANAGER_ROLES (design issue).

test_cases.title: ReadOnly user cannot create vacation
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with readOnly=true.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_vacation.employee e WHERE e.read_only = true AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:

2. Navigate to /vacation/my
