4. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations — verify rejection
CLEANUP: None

test_cases.title: Accountant can pay but not approve
test_cases.preconditions:
Accountant user.
Query: SELECT e.login FROM ttt_backend.employee be JOIN ttt_backend.employee_role er ON be.id = er.employee_id JOIN ttt_backend.role r ON er.role_id = r.id JOIN ttt_vacation.employee e ON e.login = be.login WHERE r.name = 'ROLE_ACCOUNTANT' AND e.enabled = true ORDER BY random() LIMIT 1
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create and approve a vacation
1. Login as the accountant
2. Navigate to vacation payment section
3. Verify payment controls are available for APPROVED vacations
4. Via API — PUT /approve/{id} as accountant → verify this fails (accountant not in approval roles)
5. Via API — PUT /pay/{id} → verify this succeeds
test_cases.expected_result: Accountant has PAY permission but NOT APPROVE. Can process payments but cannot change approval status.
test_cases.notes: ROLE_ACCOUNTANT is in ACCOUNTANT_ROLES for payment, but NOT in MANAGER_ROLES for approval.

test_cases.title: canBeCancelled guard — REGULAR+APPROVED after period close
test_cases.preconditions:
REGULAR + APPROVED vacation with paymentDate before current report period.
Note: Requires timemachine env to manipulate period timing.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via timemachine — create vacation, approve, then advance clock past paymentDate
1. Login as the employee
2. Navigate to /vacation/my
3. Find the APPROVED vacation
4. Verify: cancel/delete buttons are NOT available
5. Via API — PUT /cancel/{id} → verify HTTP 400
CLEANUP: Reset clock
test_cases.expected_result: canBeCancelled returns false when: REGULAR + APPROVED + reportPeriod after paymentDate. Cancel/reject/delete all blocked.
test_cases.notes: Protects against canceling after accounting period closure. Needs timemachine env.

test_cases.title: Empty request body → empty 400 response
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations with empty body (no JSON)
2. Verify HTTP 400
3. Verify response body is EMPTY (no JSON error details)
4. HttpMessageNotReadableException returns ResponseEntity<Void>
test_cases.expected_result: Empty body returns HTTP 400 with completely empty response body. No error details provided — unique behavior.
test_cases.notes: Design issue: should return structured error response.

test_cases.title: Invalid type parameter → type mismatch error
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — GET /api/vacation/v1/vacations/abc (string instead of Long ID)
2. Verify HTTP 400
3. Verify errorCode: 'exception.type.mismatch'
4. Verify message includes expected type 'Long'
test_cases.expected_result: Type mismatch returns 400 with exception.type.mismatch error code.

test_cases.title: Missing required fields → validation errors array
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations with body: {} (empty JSON object)
2. Verify HTTP 400
3. Verify response contains 'errors' array
4. Verify each error has: field, code, message
5. Verify errors for: login (NotNull), startDate (NotNull), endDate (NotNull), paymentType (NotNull)
test_cases.expected_result: Multiple validation errors returned in errors[] array. Each with field name, code, and message.

test_cases.title: Exception class leakage in error responses
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations with past startDate
2. Verify HTTP 400 response
3. Check 'exception' field in response
4. Verify it contains full Java class name: 'com.noveogroup.ttt.common.exception.ServiceException'
5. This leaks internal implementation details
test_cases.expected_result: Security issue: error responses include 'exception' field with full Java class name. Information disclosure vulnerability.
test_cases.notes: All error responses (except empty-body) include this field. OWASP information disclosure.

test_cases.title: Update without id in body → IllegalArgumentException
test_cases.preconditions:
Existing vacation.
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation, note the ID
1. Via API — PUT /api/vacation/v1/vacations/{id} with body missing 'id' field
2. Verify HTTP 400
3. Verify error: 'IllegalArgumentException: The given id must not be null!'
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Missing id in update body causes IllegalArgumentException. JPA findById called with null from DTO.
test_cases.notes: id required in BOTH URL path AND request body.

test_cases.title: Crossing validation error format inconsistency
test_cases.preconditions:
Two vacations with overlapping dates.
SETUP: Via API — create first vacation.
test_cases.steps:
SETUP: Via API — create a vacation
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations with overlapping dates (create endpoint)
2. Check error response format: errorCode, message, errors[].code, errors[].message
3. Note which field contains the specific crossing error code
4. Via API — PUT /vacations/{id} with overlapping dates (update endpoint)
5. Compare error format between create and update
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE
test_cases.expected_result: Inconsistency: create uses errors[].code = specific code. Update uses errors[].code = 'exception.validation.fail' with specific code in errors[].message.
test_cases.notes: Always check both errorCode and message fields for reliable assertion.

test_cases.title: Sick leave crossing vacation → 409 CONFLICT
test_cases.preconditions:
Employee with existing vacation. Ability to create sick leave.
Note: Blocked by API_SECRET_TOKEN — sick leave endpoint requires AUTHENTICATED_USER authority.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — create a vacation for specific dates
2. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/sick-leaves with dates overlapping the vacation
3. Verify HTTP 409 CONFLICT
4. Verify SickLeaveCrossingVacationException in response
Note: May need per-user CAS auth — API_SECRET_TOKEN returns 403 for sick leave endpoint
CLEANUP: Via API — DELETE vacation
test_cases.expected_result: Sick leave overlapping a vacation returns 409 CONFLICT. SickLeaveCrossingVacationException thrown.
test_cases.notes: BLOCKED: API_SECRET_TOKEN cannot access sick leave endpoint (403). Needs CAS JWT.

test_cases.title: Non-existent vacation ID → 404
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — GET /api/vacation/v1/vacations/999999999
2. Verify HTTP 404
3. Verify errorCode: 'exception.not.found'
test_cases.expected_result: Non-existent vacation returns 404 with exception.not.found error code.

test_cases.title: Invalid notifyAlso login → 400
test_cases.preconditions: API access.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — POST /api/vacation/v1/vacations with valid fields but notifyAlso: ['nonexistent_user']
2. Verify HTTP 400
3. Verify validation error for notifyAlso field (EmployeeLoginCollectionExists)
CLEANUP: None — creation should fail
test_cases.expected_result: @EmployeeLoginCollectionExists validates all logins exist. Invalid login returns 400.

test_cases.title: Batch deadlock on concurrent operations
test_cases.preconditions: Ability to send concurrent API requests.
test_cases.steps:
1. Via API — simultaneously send 3 vacation create requests for the same employee
2. Observe responses — at least 1 should succeed
3. Check for 500 errors with 'deadlock detected' message
4. Verify VacationRecalculationService row contention triggers PostgreSQL deadlock
test_cases.expected_result: Concurrent vacation operations may cause PostgreSQL deadlocks. CannotAcquireLockException thrown. Not all requests succeed.
test_cases.notes: Root: employee_vacation table row contention during FIFO redistribution. Mitigation: 2+ second gaps.
